nama: Yéné soramé Jonene si résagh ár nuwaneyayénd, Yesum si yérnyai árfefaf Jonenemén, “Fém némé fandaghét néngtor fanfne yuwot? Yéné tombé yus mbormboram kés efnje?
english: When John ’s messengers had departed , he began to tell the multitudes about John , “ What did you go out into the wilderness to see ? A reed shaken by the wind?

nama: Yau njam, némé fandaghét fém néngtor? Ár takayongérwén mer sáláme umaneghkaf? Yau, efe tawongérwén mer sáláme a efe tamorwén sotafé mer rokarafé warsagh ár sifayan fá tamorwén.
english: But what did you go out to see ? A man clothed in soft clothing ? Behold , those who are gorgeously dressed and live delicately are in kings ’ courts.

nama: Wénde némé fandaghét fém néngtor? Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár? O, yéndon némndetan, fá ewainde méinyotio ámb Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár.
english: But what did you go out to see ? A prophet ? Yes , I tell you , and much more than a prophet .

nama: Yéné fiyém endenemén yéfarotaménd: 'Yéndon so yéngwáfáretan fronde si résagh ár, emon so fene end yéfrende.'”
english: This is he of whom it is written , ‘ Behold , I send my messenger before your face , who will prepare your way before you .’ + 7:27 Malachi 3:1

nama: Yesu kor norayai, “Yéndon emndetan, Jon walitogh árom ewainde méinyotio ár efe náráftat yéné mband maratan. Wénde efe yém némbnar ambumnit Ngánjánene limánéghan, yémo so taindangé Jon.”
english: “ For I tell you , among those who are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptizer; yet he who is least in God ’s Kingdom is greater than he .”

nama: Yesuene si njam náyárayénd, méinyotio ár, kété tawafnyangérwén mani kafégh ár, mat namndayénd ndené Ngánjánene yam siyém efalo, ménamén Joném té tawawaitotau.
english: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this , they declared God to be just , having been baptized with John ’s baptism.

nama: Wénde Farasi ár a nák watamegh árém yékenayénd Ngánjánene ménde yéfendio, ménamén fá nakenayénd Joném sái tawalitotau.
english: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God , not being baptized by him themselves .

nama: “Némafaf yéndo so yétémndan yéné árfenemén? Némanit fá em?
english: “ To what then should I compare the people of this generation ? What are they like ?

nama: Ndenanit em ambumtértér yénau emorang rokar térarégh sifayan a fárofáro námotat, “'Yénd nambnam sifraféré feyot, a fém yau neremndé Yénd neremndam ghérghérsisafé kérmén wagh, a fém yau nende.'
english: They are like children who sit in the marketplace and call to one another , saying , ‘ We piped to you , and you didn’ t dance . We mourned, and you didn’ t weep .’

nama: “Jon walitogh ár yénmormén, njamke a wéri nu yau tánetau, a fém norayati, 'Fá yaufi mbérmbérafé yém.'
english: For John the Baptizer came neither eating bread nor drinking wine , and you say , ‘ He has a demon .’

nama: Ároene Ambum yénmormén, njamke a nu nénet, a fém norayati, 'Yéna yém amanjarégh ár a wéri nu nénero, mani kafégh ár a yaufi árfene fútar.'
english: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking , and you say , ‘ Behold , a glutton and a drunkard , a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’

nama: Káfeté mwighé siyém mbarkánd yiyátotat méinyotio yáne waitogh árém.” Yesu yéfagharyéng yaufiyam kér ro amafom
english: Wisdom is justified by all her children .”

nama: Ámb Farasi ároene waráuwégh yénmormén Yesueyot séite njamket, fá néngwanoi kétán Farasi ároene méngot námnjoi nénet.
english: One of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him . He entered into the Pharisee ’s house and sat at the table.

nama: Ámb amaf efe témorangérwén yaufi ghérsayan yéné tesenan mat náyátoi ndené Yesum njamke tánetau ámb Farasi ároene méngon, yéné amafom yénérsam séfkwénafé mer wimafé wékwér,
english: Behold , a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that he was reclining in the Pharisee ’s house , brought an alabaster jar of ointment .

nama: amaf nokayoi yá sémta Yesuene kafkafot a niyai yáfaf, yáne yengam kuitawét Yesuene kafkafot. Yémon wányányogh yawafngoi yánjo séfe, a esnetai a yawasuryéng mer wimafé wékwére.
english: Standing behind at his feet weeping , she began to wet his feet with her tears , and she wiped them with the hair of her head , kissed his feet , and anointed them with the ointment .

nama: Njam yéné Farasi árom yinjoi yéné yam, fá norayai yánjo kénjún, “Yéné ár njam Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár yém, fá mat té yém emo fá yiyanje a nderé sáfot amaf yéné yém, fá siyém yaufiyamafé amaf.”
english: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it , he said to himself , “ This man , if he were a prophet , would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him , that she is a sinner.”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Saimon, yéndon ámb si so kémaufron.” Yémo korayangé, “Kwaufro, watamegh ár.”
english: Jesus answered him , “ Simon , I have something to tell you .” He said , “ Teacher , say on .”

nama: “Sómbio árét mani ewarameyai maniafé árém, ámb ároe 5,000 kina, ámb ároe 500 kina.
english: “ A certain lender had two debtors . The one owed five hundred denarii , and the other fifty .

nama: Sómbio ár maniofnar namndeyayénd wangoghét, féyo yémo mwighafnar ewawaineyai. Emo so minde méndefé yéfande?”
english: When they couldn’t pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most ?”

nama: Saimon nuwangoi, “Yénd ndené nemnan endene ndimbal fai mwighafnar yawitam.” Yesum yémndai, “Fémo mer nuwango.”
english: Simon answered , “ He , I suppose , to whom he forgave the most .” He said to him , “ You have judged correctly .”

nama: Féyo fá nawengoi amafoe tambén a yémndai Saimon, “Té yéfande yéné amaf? Yénd njam kwéném fene méngot, fémo yau nu waram tane kafkaf umbarghét, wénde amafom yengam fétre kwaramete a tawaine yánjo séfe.
english: Turning to the woman , he said to Simon , “ Do you see this woman ? I entered into your house , and you gave me no water for my feet , but she has wet my feet with her tears , and wiped them with the hair of her head.

nama: Fémo yau wésneta, wénde yéné amafamro, kétándmayo yénd nélawan, yau fá nékmbo tane kafkaf séneghan ta umyoghét.
english: You gave me no kiss , but she , since the time I came in , has not ceased to kiss my feet .

nama: Fémo yau yéfai oil tane sénkoyan ta umyoghét, wénde amafom mer wimafé wékwér yasuryéng tane kafkafan.
english: You didn’ t anoint my head with oil , but she has anointed my feet with ointment .

nama: Yénémamén nde yéndon némaufrotan fe, yéné amafoene tárfár yaufi té yawalindangén, ménamén minde uwéghafé téfandau. Wénde emon njam ewawalinde némbne kuyafongé némbnar uwéghafé wéi ndenáyo.”
english: Therefore I tell you , her sins, which are many , are forgiven, for she loved much . But one to whom little is forgiven, loves little .”

nama: Féyo Yesum yémndai amaf, “Fene yaufi té némawalindangén.”
english: He said to her , “ Your sins are forgiven.”

nama: Ámb wan téfnár si nufngoyénd yéfene kénjúnro, “Efe yéné yém emo yaufi tawainangé?”
english: Those who sat at the table with him began to say to themselves , “ Who is this who even forgives sins?”

nama: Yesum yawangoi amafoe, “Fene ndimbal mwighayam té némanjar, nguwano mere a némor.”
english: He said to the woman , “ Your faith has saved you . Go in peace .”

nama: Yéné soramé, Yesu karendau tesenamé a méngotuamé, mer si téngwaitotau Ngánján limánéghmén. 12ro tamorwén yémafé,
english: Soon afterwards , he went about through cities and villages , preaching and bringing the good news of God ’s Kingdom. With him were the twelve ,

nama: ámb amaf tamorwén efe mer eyátoi yaufi mbérmbérafé a enjnefé, Meri Makdalin ende tambén 7 yaufi mbérmbér néntoryénd.
english: and certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary who was called Magdalene , from whom seven demons had gone out ;

nama: Erodene méngo wanjengégh ár Kusa, yáne amaf Joana, Susanaf a amaf tárfár. Yéné amafém tawáutánwét yéfenjo rokare.
english: and Joanna , the wife of Chuzas, Herod ’s steward ; Susanna ; and many others who served them from their possessions .

nama: Njamandio tárfár ár káumendawét Yesuefaf fénatafénaté tesenta, yémon rengerenge si tawaitotau:
english: When a great multitude came together and people from every city were coming to him , he spoke by a parable :

nama: “Ndau téfnár nuwanoi yánjo wén mbwe taranéghét. Njam yémon téngtarnau, ámb nuyáyénd téndomé, yéné tatumndawét kafkafe, a menam tánetawét.
english: “ The farmer went out to sow his seed . As he sowed , some fell along the road , and it was trampled under foot , and the birds of the sky devoured it .

nama: Ámb nuyáyénd wímafé mbandan, njam kanotawét, kér emormén ménamén nuofnarmén.
english: Some seed fell on the rock , and as soon as it grew , it withered away , because it had no moisture .

nama: Ámb mbwe nuyáyénd wétwét sifayan wétwétam njangénjangén eyátoyénd anoghan.
english: Some fell amid the thorns, and the thorns grew with it and choked it .

nama: Ámb mbwe nuyáyénd mer mbandot. Kanotawét a nafoyénd kwéki 100 mindefifi tárfár ndené kés eitaménd.” Fá njam norayai yéné, yémon emndai, “Fá efe njam ténganafé em áyárghét, fá so káyárangi.”
english: Some fell into the good ground and grew and produced one hundred times as much fruit .” As he said these things , he called out , “ He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”

nama: Yáne waitogh árém yémotayénd yéné rengerenge fifi némé yém.
english: Then his disciples asked him , “ What does this parable mean ?”

nama: Yémon emndai, “Ngánjánam yuyafote féfe fronde némé yau tauyafotau Ngánján limánéghmén, wénde ámb árét yéndon norayan rengerenge sie, ménát, “'Ghékfe so yéfandat, wénde yau so tinjongi, so náyáretat, wénde yau so tayamngongi.'
english: He said , “ To you it is given to know the mysteries of God ’s Kingdom, but to the rest it is given in parables , that ‘seeing they may not see , and hearing they may not understand .’

nama: Rengerengeyoene fifi siyém: Mbwe siyém Buk si.
english: “ Now the parable is this : The seed is the word of God .

nama: Áyárégh ár efe em endmé fá em, féyo Sáifér kénwanongé yéfene tikéfta si orot, ménát yémofem yau so efalo tiyátongi a kawanjarngi.
english: Those along the road are those who hear; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their heart , that they may not believe and be saved .

nama: Yéné wímafé mbandan fá em emofem si tanangi áuwéghafé njam yémofem náyáretat, wénde fá yénéf em sérémbofnar. Yéfene efalo yiyátogh siyém tekofnar, wénde ufogh efghon fá kuyangi.
english: Those on the rock are they who , when they hear, receive the word with joy ; but these have no root . They believe for a while , then fall away in time of temptation .

nama: Árké mbwe nuyáyénd wétwét sifayan, fá esérmbéne efe náyáretat, wénde fá méghmégh em tárfár rokarmén ménát so em wemár yéfene ghérsayan, a yéfene ndimbal mwighé yau kénamndangi muyafé.
english: What fell among the thorns, these are those who have heard, and as they go on their way they are choked with cares , riches , and pleasures of life; and they bring no fruit to maturity .

nama: Wénde árké mbwe mer mbandot nuyáyénd fá esérmbéne efe em mer a efalo ár tikfan, efe náyáretat si, yarawenat wafroghyamot yékmetat a ámb mbwe nafotat. Rengerenge si kwayaghamén
english: Those in the good ground , these are those who with an honest and good heart , having heard the word , hold it tightly, and produce fruit with perseverance .

nama: “Árom yau lambefé korongé tétnongé sin kénjún o tékmangé yáf mbanmbanan. Wénde yémon so téfayongé kitarékitarayan.
english: “ No one , when he has lit a lamp , covers it with a container or puts it under a bed ; but puts it on a stand, that those who enter in may see the light .

nama: Warambnoghe némé yém so tinjongi, a némé ténoghe yém sáfét so kakmangi.
english: For nothing is hidden that will not be revealed , nor anything secret that will not be known and come to light .

nama: Kor fété ndernáye fém náyárete. Efe ár rokarafé em yéfe minde fifi so tawaramangé, wénde endefene yau ewam, a nemnat ndené tane féyotaro em so tawakawangé.” Yesue yátmé a yánngnéf
english: Be careful therefore how you hear. For whoever has , to him will be given ; and whoever doesn’t have , from him will be taken away even that which he thinks he has .”

nama: Yesue yátmé a yanngnéf enmormén yá fandaghét, wénde ménamén ár tárfár tamorwén, fá yau kaka tamorwén yáfaf.
english: His mother and brothers came to him , and they could not come near him for the crowd .

nama: Ámb árom yaufroi yá, “Fene amé a fenngnéf kétándmé ewakai safén, nawanjetat fe fandaghét.”
english: Some people told him , “ Your mother and your brothers stand outside , desiring to see you .”

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, “Tane amé a nénngnéf fá em efe náyáretat Ngánjánene si a wafroghyaman ekmetat.” Yesum yékmboi ndimbal mbormbor
english: But he answered them , “ My mother and my brothers are these who hear the word of God and do it .”

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár, “Kuwanongém sawot kéférmangém witmé karéf.” Féyo fá nuflitaménd boutan nuwanoyénd.
english: Now on one of those days , he entered into a boat , himself and his disciples , and he said to them , “ Let ’s go over to the other side of the lake .” So they launched out .

nama: Njamandio fá karayawét, Yesu nakmai. Ndimbal mbormboram eutfoi sawan, féyo bout táfandio olitoghétro témorwén, fá tamorwén ndimbal kértayan.
english: But as they sailed , he fell asleep. A wind storm came down on the lake , and they were taking on dangerous amounts of water.

nama: Waitogh ár naflayénd yanoyénd fá, nutnayénd, “Yuré, Yuré, yénd yéném olitoghétro!” Fá nanoyéng ekmbeyai mbormbor a sof. Táf fété ndimbal mbormbor nékmboyéng méinyotio métar namndai.
english: They came to him and awoke him , saying , “ Master , Master , we are dying!” He awoke and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water ; then they ceased , and it was calm .

nama: Yémo emndai yánjo waitogh ár, “Ánde yém féfene ndimbal mwighé?” Waitogh ár férfér a sélngwél emormén, fáro yémndayénd, “Efe fá yém? Njam fá korayangé mbormbor a sofam tiyárangi fá!” Yesum mer yiyátoi yaufi mbérmbérafé ár
english: He said to them , “ Where is your faith ?” Being afraid , they marveled , saying to one another , “ Who is this then , that he commands even the winds and the water , and they obey him ?”

nama: Fá nufaryénd Gerasenes yuwot, árké yém witmé karéf kétamé Galili yu.
english: Then they arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is opposite Galilee .

nama: Yesum njam kafkaf eyai, yaufi mbérmbérafé árom yéfángoi. Teke yéné ár yau sáláme tawongérwén a yau témorangérwén méngon, wénde mbéwé sifayan.
english: When Jesus stepped ashore , a certain man out of the city who had demons for a long time met him . He wore no clothes , and didn’ t live in a house , but in the tombs .

nama: Njam Yesu yinjoi, fá nutnai a nuyai yáne kafkafot, nutnam tukén, “Fém némé ménde ném tafaf, Yesu, Minde Tukén Ngánjánene ambum? Yéndon némamnendan, yau tékrátan kwékém!”
english: When he saw Jesus , he cried out and fell down before him , and with a loud voice said , “ What do I have to do with you , Jesus , you Son of the Most High God ? I beg you , don’ t torment me !”

nama: Ménamén Yesum yémndai yaufi mbérmbér étorghét ároe tambén. Túmén yaufi mbérmbéram tusérmbotawét a árém yumbetaménd ngángayan a kafkafan wambégh mbanmbanan témorwén, yémon etiaryéng sein a yaufi mbérmbéram yalimai fanfne yuwot.
english: For Jesus was commanding the unclean spirit to come out of the man . For the unclean spirit had often seized the man . He was kept under guard and bound with chains and fetters . Breaking the bonds apart, he was driven by the demon into the desert .

nama: Yesum yémotai, “Fene yétkwén efe yém?” Yémo yawangoi, “Tárfár” ménamén tárfár yaufi mbérmbér té engmormén yáfaf.
english: Jesus asked him , “ What is your name ?” He said , “ Legion ,” for many demons had entered into him .

nama: A yémofem yamnendayénd fá férghe, “Yénd yau so yéngém kétán kémboghofnar yékwét.”
english: They begged him that he would not command them to go into the abyss.

nama: Tárfár mérén kimb taménjnawét mband tunakéf téndon. Yaufi mbérmbéram yamnendayénd Yesu ménát élaughét yéfefaf féyo wafté ewaramai.
english: Now there was there a herd of many pigs feeding on the mountain , and they begged him that he would allow them to enter into those . Then he allowed them .

nama: Njam yaufi mbérmbér nétoryénd árta, fá nélawayénd kimbafefaf, a néngengoyénd ngewonawét sawot a kolitotawét.
english: The demons came out of the man and entered into the pigs , and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and were drowned.

nama: Njam kimb wanjengégh téfnáram yinjoyénd némé yam kufrotau, fá nétonaménd a nafroyénd yéné tesenan a witmé karéf yuta.
english: When those who fed them saw what had happened , they fled and told it in the city and in the country.

nama: A nuwanoyénd fandaghét némé kufrotau. Fá njam engmorménd kétán Yesuefaf, yémofem yinjoyénd ár ende tambén yaufi mbérmbér ewifnam témorangérwén Yesuene kafkaffaf. A umaneghkaf témorwén a mer mwighafé. Wénde fá férfér tamorwén.
english: People went out to see what had happened . They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone out , sitting at Jesus ’ feet , clothed and in his right mind ; and they were afraid .

nama: Yémofem efe yinjoyénd ewafroyénd árét ndernáye yaufi mbérmbér wasérmboghkaf ár mer namndai.
english: Those who saw it told them how he who had been possessed by demons was healed .

nama: Kétamé Gerasenes méinyotio árém Yesu yamnendayénd sái tafrangongai, ménamén fá férféram ewifoi. Féyo fá boutan nuflayénd álighét.
english: All the people of the surrounding country of the Gadarenes asked him to depart from them , for they were very much afraid. Then he entered into the boat and returned .

nama: Yéné ár kétándmé ende tambén yaufi mbérmbér tawifnau, yémon yamnendai Yesu yémafé álighét, wénde Yesum yéngwatembai ndené siafé,
english: But the man from whom the demons had gone out begged him that he might go with him , but Jesus sent him away , saying ,

nama: “Ngangota méngotuot a tawaufro nderé ndimbal Ngánjánam kémawafrote feyot.” Féyo ár nuwanoi, a kafrotau tesenamé nderé ndimbal Yesum yawafrotam yáyot. Kér mérés a enjnefé amaf
english: “ Return to your house , and declare what great things God has done for you .” He went his way , proclaiming throughout the whole city what great things Jesus had done for him .

nama: Yesu njam néngangotam, ár mérnam yumyoyénd, ménamén yémofem méghméghafé tanjetawét.
english: When Jesus returned , the multitude welcomed him , for they were all waiting for him .

nama: Ár yétkwén Jairus, wáwefnogh méngo ndimbal ár yénmormén a nénuyai Yesuene kafkafot, yamnendai álighét yánjo méngot
english: Behold , a man named Jairus came . He was a ruler of the synagogue . He fell down at Jesus ’ feet and begged him to come into his house ,

nama: ménamén yáne féfét mérés, 12 yunjéf krotro témorwén. Njam Yesu néndkfan néngayatoi, tárfár árém téngsotawét fá.
english: for he had an only born daughter , about twelve years of age , and she was dying. But as he went , the multitudes pressed against him .

nama: A amaf kété témorwén efe férék kertau 12 yunjéf, wénde emon yau yéfrendai.
english: A woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years , who had spent all her living on physicians and could not be healed by any ,

nama: Fá sémta yénmormén a yiyánjai Yesuene sáláme fétfétro férék táf nékmboi.
english: came behind him and touched the fringe of his cloak . Immediately the flow of her blood stopped .

nama: Yesu namotai, “Emo wiyánja?” Njam fá méinyotio nawanyotayénd, Fité norayai, “Yuré, ár mérnam némarendangi a néngsotat fém.”
english: Jesus said , “ Who touched me ?” When all denied it , Peter and those with him said , “ Master , the multitudes press and jostle you , and you say , ‘ Who touched me ?’ ”

nama: Wénde Yesu norayai, “Ámb árom té wiyánja. Yénd mat náyáton wafté té néngférém ta tambén.”
english: But Jesus said , “ Someone did touch me , for I perceived that power has gone out of me .”

nama: Njam amaf yénmormén fandaghofnar a mat siofnar, férférafé ténmorwén nénuyai yáne kafkafot. Méinyotio árfene sáfréré, yémon yémndai némamén yémon yiyánjai a ndernáye fá táfandio frengégham yanai.
english: When the woman saw that she was not hidden, she came trembling ; and falling down before him declared to him in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched him , and how she was healed immediately .

nama: Ndenaye Yesum yémdayéng, “Mérés, fenjo ndimbal mwighayam mer niyáto. Kuwano mer so ném.”
english: He said to her , “ Daughter , cheer up . Your faith has made you well . Go in peace .”

nama: Njamandio Yesu mé korayau, ámb ár yénmormén Jairusene méngota, efe témormén áumengégh méngo ndimbal ár. Yémon yémndai, “Fene mérés kér tém. Yau yawérsota watamegh ár nde nayu.”
english: While he still spoke , one from the ruler of the synagogue’s house came , saying to him , “ Your daughter is dead . Don’ t trouble the Teacher .”

nama: Yesu njam náyárai yéné, fá yémndai Jairus, “Yau férfér ném. Efalo tiyáto a so mer káyátongé.”
english: But Jesus hearing it , answered him , “Don’ t be afraid . Only believe , and she will be healed .”

nama: Njam fá nufaryéng Jairusene méngot, yémon yau efe yumyoi yémafé álighét fáro Fité, Jon, Jems a ambumoe yárefé a yátmé.
english: When he came to the house , he didn’ t allow anyone to enter in , except Peter , John , James , the father of the child , and her mother .

nama: Njamandio méinyotio ár kendawét a kutnawét ghérghérsisé tamorwén yáfaf. Yesum emndayai, “Yau nendati, yau kér tém wénde kémégh yékém.”
english: All were weeping and mourning her , but he said , “Don’ t weep . She isn’ t dead, but sleeping .”

nama: Yémofem turtawét ménamén fá mat tamorwén ndené kér tém.
english: They were ridiculing him , knowing that she was dead.

nama: Wénde yerayéng ngángayan a yémndai, “Tane ambum, kano!”
english: But he put them all outside, and taking her by the hand , he called , saying , “ Child , arise !”

nama: Yáne mbérmbér nénangoi, táfandio nénokayoi. Féyo Yesum emndai njamke taraménd nénet.
english: Her spirit returned , and she rose up immediately . He commanded that something be given to her to eat .

nama: Yárefaf sélngwél emormén, wénde Yesum emndayéng yau nafrotati némé kufrote.
english: Her parents were amazed , but he commanded them to tell no one what had been done .

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum eumendai yáne 12 waitogh ár mérne a ewarametam wafté a muyé ménát yaufi mbérmbér étorghét a enjneta mer yátoghét.
english: He called the twelve together and gave them power and authority over all demons , and to cure diseases .

nama: A fá ewáfáretai Ngánjánene limánéghmén si waitoghét a enjne téfnár frengéghét
english: He sent them out to preach God ’s Kingdom and to heal the sick.

nama: Yémon emndai, “Némé yau álighan tawanangi-yau wétu, yau yáf, yau njamke, yau mani, yau ámb awalogh sáláme.
english: He said to them , “ Take nothing for your journey — no staffs, nor wallet, nor bread, nor money. Don’ t have two tunics each .

nama: Árké méngo njam kélawangi, kétémiya emor kétánotio yéné tesen téfrangongi.
english: Into whatever house you enter , stay there , and depart from there .

nama: Árém yau njam taumyongi, kafkaf ngwérméng tasénangi táf ténfrangongi yéfene tesen, awéfarogh so yém yéfeyot njam Ngánjánene nékw kufarngé ménamén yáne si yékenayénd.”
english: As many as don’ t receive you , when you depart from that city , shake off even the dust from your feet for a testimony against them .”

nama: Féyo fá náfrendaménd a tayotau méinyotio méngotuamé, Ngánjánene mer si táitotawét a ár tangéfrendawét méinyotio sifayan.
english: They departed and went throughout the villages , preaching the Good News and healing everywhere .

nama: Erod limánégh ár náyárai méinyotio némé kufrotawét. A fá kéwérsotawét ménamén ámb korayawét, “Yéné siyém Jon kér tambén nénano,”
english: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him ; and he was very perplexed , because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead ,

nama: ámb kemnawét, “Elaija nufar,” a ámb kemnawét nde ámb tándáwár Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár nénango ghérsat.
english: and by some that Elijah had appeared , and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.

nama: Wénde Erod norayai, “Yéndon emndayén Jonene sénko néfghét. Efe yéné yém yénd tárfár si náyáretan yánemén?” A fá ménde témorwén sái tinjongai Yesu. Yesum emanjnam 5,000 ár
english: Herod said , “ I beheaded John , but who is this about whom I hear such things ?” He sought to see him .

nama: Njam Yesuene muyafé ár nénangoyénd, yémofem nafroyénd Yesuefaf némé tawafrotawét. Yémon engwasroi a fá néngwanoi enaghét tesen yétkwén Betsaidat.
english: The apostles, when they had returned , told him what things they had done . He took them and withdrew apart to a desert region of + 9:10 NU omits “a desert region of”. a city called Bethsaida.

nama: Wénde tárfár ár mat náyátoyénd yénémamén a yaitoyénd fá. Yémon eumyoi a emndayeng Ngánjánene limánéghmén a efrendayéng fá efe frengégh ménde tamorwén.
english: But the multitudes , perceiving it , followed him . He welcomed them , spoke to them of God ’ s Kingdom, and he cured those who needed healing .

nama: Seitefifi 12 waitogh ár enmormén Yesuefaf a yémndayénd, “Tawafarna ár mérén, fá so néngangotat kaka méngotuot a téndo yuwot ménát njamke a kémégh sifayat áronjaghét, ménamén yénd yéném yauyau sifayan.”
english: The day began to wear away ; and the twelve came and said to him , “Send the multitude away , that they may go into the surrounding villages and farms and lodge and get food, for we are here in a deserted place .”

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, “Fémofem némndafi tawaramangi nénet yéfeyot.” Yémofem yawangoyénd, “Téfene em 5 féskaf flawé a sómbio wagif, yénd njam ménde yéném yéné ár manjarghét, yénd so kuwanongém yéfene njamke résaghét.”
english: But he said to them , “ You give them something to eat .” They said , “ We have no more than five loaves and two fish , unless we should go and buy food for all these people .”

nama: Yéné ár tamorwén 5,000nit. Wénde emndai yánjo waitogh ár, “Kámnjongi mérén tawafrotangi 50nit.”
english: For they were about five thousand men . He said to his disciples , “ Make them sit down in groups of about fifty each .”

nama: Féyo waitogh árém yafrotaménd, a méinyotio ár námnjotaménd.
english: They did so , and made them all sit down .

nama: Yesum euyoi 5 féskaf flawé a sómbio wagif, a néwewoi njafar tukét, kánjo yaramai a etfotam. Féyo eramai waitogh árfefaf tawarametawét árét.
english: He took the five loaves and the two fish , and looking up to the sky , he blessed them , broke them , and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude .

nama: Méinyotio árém enetaménd eremngoi, a waitogh árém ekafayénd 12 yáf afoghare njamke mbarmbar árkéméné ninamoyénd. Fité kwayaghé noraye nde Yesu siyém Keriso
english: They ate and were all filled . They gathered up twelve baskets of broken pieces that were left over .

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum efrangoi ár mérén Ngánjánafé oraighét yambia a yánjo waitogh árfé tamorwén, yémon emotai fá, “Efe mérén korayawét yénd efe wém?”
english: As he was praying alone , the disciples were near him , and he asked them , “ Who do the multitudes say that I am ?”

nama: Yémofem yawangoyénd, “Ámb korayawét Jon walitogh ár, ámb árém korayawét Elaija, wénde ámb árém korayawét ndené tándáwár ámb Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár kénangote ghérsat.”
english: They answered , “‘ John the Baptizer,’ but others say , ‘ Elijah ,’ and others , that one of the old prophets has risen again.”

nama: Yémon emotai, “Wénde fém wét? Fém korayangi yénd efe wém?” Fitam yawangoi, “Ngánjánene Keriso.” Yesu korayau yánjo kérmén
english: He said to them , “ But who do you say that I am ?” Peter answered , “ The Christ of God .”

nama: Yesum táikér si tawawéngsotau yéfe ende yau taufrongi yénémamén.
english: But he warned them and commanded them to tell this to no one ,

nama: A fá norayai, “Ároene Ambumom so fété kérté yéngfande tárfár yaman a yékarotat sénko árém, limánégh ghéngén wárogh árém a nák watamegh árém, a fá so fété yésmetat a nambio efogh soramé Ngánjánam so tanongé ghérsat.”
english: saying , “ The Son of Man must suffer many things , and be rejected by the elders , chief priests, and scribes , and be killed, and the third day be raised up .”

nama: Féyo yémo emndayeng méinyotio: “Njam efe waitoghét kénufngo ta soramé, fá so fété kakenangé fáyo a tanangé yánjoyo wafatarégh wén méinyotio efghon a kwénmaitongé yénd.
english: He said to all , “ If anyone desires to come after me , let him deny himself , take up his cross , and follow me .

nama: Efe ménde njam yém yánjo ghérsé wanjarghét so yétarne, wénde emo njam yáne ghérsé yétarna tanemén so tanjarngé.
english: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it , but whoever will lose his life for my sake will save it .

nama: A nderé mer yém ároe sái tanangé méinyotio mband marat, wénde yétarna o nawasérmbota fáyo?
english: For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world , and loses or forfeits his own self?

nama: Njam wáiwáiafé efe yém tanemén a tane simén, Ároene Ambum so yém wáiwái yánemén njam fá nénangota yáne ndimbal kwayaghé kénjún a Yárefane ndimbal kwayaghé kénjún a kétándmé njúnnjúnofnar njafar si résagh ár.
english: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words , of him will the Son of Man be ashamed when he comes in his glory , and the glory of the Father , and of the holy angels.

nama: Yéndon efalo ewaufrotan, ámb efe ewakai yéna, yémofem so yéfandati Ngánjánene limánégh soramé so kér em.” Nambio waitogh árém yinjoyénd Yesuene ndimbal kwayaghé
english: But I tell you the truth: There are some of those who stand here who will in no way taste of death until they see God ’s Kingdom.”

nama: 8 efogh soramé Yesum ewasroi Fité, Jon a Jems yémafé néngwanoyénd mband tunakfot Ngánjánafé koraiwét.
english: About eight days after these sayings , he took with him Peter , John , and James , and went up onto the mountain to pray .

nama: Njamandio fá Ngánjánafé koraiwét, yáne sáf natambnai, a yáne sáláme náyátoi mé kwayagharo nálálai toyanit.
english: As he was praying , the appearance of his face was altered , and his clothing became white and dazzling.

nama: Sómbio ár, Moses a Elaija, ndimbal kwayaghé nufaryéng, Yesufé korayawét.
english: Behold , two men were talking with him , who were Moses and Elijah ,

nama: Yémofem korayawét yáne álighmén, árké témorwén Ngánjánene ménde so kufrotau sái kér témorwén Jerusalemén.
english: who appeared in glory and spoke of his departure , which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem .

nama: Fité a yáne mérén kémégh kér tákmangéwén, wénde njam fá nanoyénd, yémofem yinjoyénd yáne ndimbal kwayaghé a sómbio ár tawakayarwén yémafé.
english: Now Peter and those who were with him were heavy with sleep , but when they were fully awake , they saw his glory , and the two men who stood with him .

nama: Njamandio Moses a Elaijafem téfrangowét Yesu, Fitam yémndai, “Yuré, mer nérete fémafé sái yéna yéném. So erametam nambio méngo, ámbiro feyot, ámbiro Mosese a ámbiro Elaija.” Fá mafina témorwén némamén korayau.
english: As they were parting from him , Peter said to Jesus , “ Master , it is good for us to be here . Let ’s make three tents : one for you , one for Moses , and one for Elijah ,” not knowing what he said .

nama: Njamandio fá korayau, njafar kwamkwam nufaryéng a fá engwafaryéng, a fá férfér tamorwén njam ewafaryéng njafar kwamkwaman.
english: While he said these things , a cloud came and overshadowed them , and they were afraid as they entered into the cloud .

nama: Si kém yénmormén njafar kwamkwamta, nénorayai, “Yéné siyém tane ambum, efe yéndon yaferyén, yiyáretati fá.”
english: A voice came out of the cloud , saying , “ This is my beloved Son . Listen to him !”

nama: Si njam nefoi, Yesuro yinjoyénd yambia. Endefet ámb yau ewafroyénd yéné efghon némé yémofem yinjoyénd. Yesum yéfrendai yaufi mbérmbérafé ambum
english: When the voice came , Jesus was found alone . They were silent , and told no one in those days any of the things which they had seen .

nama: Ámb efghon, njam fá kénangotawét mband tunakéfta, ndimbal mérnam yéfángoyénd.
english: On the next day , when they had come down from the mountain , a great multitude met him .

nama: Ár nutnai mérén kénjúta, “Watamegh ár, yéndon némamnendan ménát tane ambum so tinjo, ménamén tane yénaro ambum fiyém.
english: Behold , a man from the crowd called out , saying , “ Teacher , I beg you to look at my son , for he is my only born child .

nama: Yaufi mbérmbéram yarsote fá a sunsune kutnangé, yémo tétarmangé táikram yafrote a féfokam ewétote táf sénfneta. Yaufi mbérmbéram yau téfrangongé fá mé yasérmbote.
english: Behold , a spirit takes him , he suddenly cries out , and it convulses him so that he foams; and it hardly departs from him , bruising him severely .

nama: Yéndon ewamnendan fene waitogh ár ménát yaufi mbérmbér witérmboghét, wénde fá féyotarofnar em.”
english: I begged your disciples to cast it out , and they couldn’ t .”

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, “Fém ndimbal mwighafnar a áyáréghofnar ár, nderé teke fémafé so yénmor a ewamarotan fém? Ténémnjo fene ambum yénan.”
english: Jesus answered , “Faithless and perverse generation , how long shall I be with you and bear with you ? Bring your son here .”

nama: Njamandio ambum ténmorwén, yaufi mbérmbéram yitam mbandot kosénau. Wénde Yesum yaufi mbérmbér yifnai, yéfrendai ambum a yangoi yárefafaf.
english: While he was still coming , the demon threw him down and convulsed him violently. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit , healed the boy , and gave him back to his father .

nama: A fá sélngwél emormén Ngánjánene ndimbal yam wafroghan. Yesu korayau yánjo kérmén yénamayo Njam méinyotio ár sélngwél emormén a korayawét Yesuene yam wafroghmén, yémon emndai yánjo waitogh ár,
english: They were all astonished at the majesty of God . But while all were marveling at all the things which Jesus did , he said to his disciples ,

nama: “Mer kéráye káyárangi yéndon némé waufroghét féfe ewawafngon. Ároene Ambum so sén yérametat árfene ngángat.”
english: “ Let these words sink into your ears , for the Son of Man will be delivered up into the hands of men .”

nama: Wénde fá yau mat náyátoyénd némé tésérmbnau. Ménamén ténoghe témorwén yéfefaf, fá yau mat náyátoyénd, a fá férfér tamorwén ámoghét yáfaf yénémamén. Efe so yém ndimbal
english: But they didn’ t understand this saying . It was concealed from them , that they should not perceive it , and they were afraid to ask him about this saying .

nama: Yáne waitogh ár mwighayan ásnanéghan tamorwén efe so yém ndimbal.
english: An argument arose among them about which of them was the greatest .

nama: Wénde Yesu mat náyátoyéng yéfene mwighé, féyo yémon yakayoi némbnar ambum tosé kété yá téndon.
english: Jesus , perceiving the reasoning of their hearts , took a little child , and set him by his side ,

nama: Féyo yémon emndai fá, “Emo njam tumyongé yéné némbnar ambum tosé tane yétkwénan kumyongé yénd, a emofem yénd umyotat fá kumyongé emo wémáfáretam. Efe némbne yém féfefaf méinyotio fá siyém ndimbal.”
english: and said to them , “ Whoever receives this little child in my name receives me . Whoever receives me receives him who sent me . For whoever is least among you all , this one will be great .”

nama: Jon norayai, “Yuré, yéndfem ár yinjongém yaufi mbérmbér tawifnau fene yétkwénan a yéta tékmbotawém, ménamém fá yau téfene yém.”
english: John answered , “ Master , we saw someone casting out demons in your name , and we forbade him , because he doesn’ t follow with us .”

nama: Yesum emndai, “Yau yékmbotati ménamén emo njam yau nékarota fá siyém téfeta.” Samariamén árém yékenayénd Yesu
english: Jesus said to him , “Don’t forbid him , for he who is not against us is for us .”

nama: Yesuene efogh kaka kénamndetau angoghét njafarot, Yesum sotafé mwighé yékmai álighét Jerusalemét.
english: It came to pass , when the days were near that he should be taken up , he intently set his face to go to Jerusalem

nama: Yémon engwáfáretam si résagh ár frondeyo, efe engmormén Samaria méngotuot rokar frengéghét yáyot,
english: and sent messengers before his face . They went and entered into a village of the Samaritans , so as to prepare for him .

nama: wénde árém kété yau umyoyénd fá, ménamén fá tiotau Jerusalemét.
english: They didn’ t receive him , because he was traveling with his face set toward Jerusalem .

nama: Njam waitogh ár Jems a Jon yinjeyayénd yéné, yémofem yémotangeyayénd, “Yuré, fém ménde ném yénd fésot kutnangém njafarot yéfe wasérmboghét.”
english: When his disciples , James and John , saw this , they said , “ Lord , do you want us to command fire to come down from the sky and destroy them , just as Elijah did ?”

nama: Wénde Yesu nawengoi ewárnam fá.
english: But he turned and rebuked them, “You don’t know of what kind of spirit you are.

nama: A fá néwanoyénd ámb méngotuot. Efalo Yesuene waitogh ár
english: For the Son of Man didn’t come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them .” They went to another village .

nama: Fá njam endmé tangmormén árom yémndai Yesu, “Yéndon so némaitotan árkamé fém njam niota.”
english: As they went on the way , a certain man said to him , “ I want to follow you wherever you go , Lord .”

nama: Yesum yawangoyéng, “Ághéághafene féne té em a menafene njén té em, wénde Ároene Ambumoene sifayé yau yém kéméghét a enághét.”
english: Jesus said to him , “ The foxes have holes and the birds of the sky have nests , but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head .”

nama: Yémon yémndai ámb ár, “Kwénmaito yénd.” Wénde árém yawangoi, “Yuré, yénd so fronde nangotan méngot kétán tane afé kér njam yém yéndon so yiyátotan soramé yéndon so kémaiton.”
english: He said to another , “ Follow me !” But he said , “ Lord , allow me first to go and bury my father .”

nama: Yesum yémndai fá, “Kér ár yémofem so eyátotat efe em mbérmbére kérwén, wénde fém nguwano Ngánjánene limánéghmén si waitoghét.”
english: But Jesus said to him , “Leave the dead to bury their own dead , but you go and announce God ’s Kingdom.”

nama: Wéi ámb norayai, “Yéndon so kémaiton fém, Yuré, wénde so fronde néngangotan tane mérén watembghét.”
english: Another also said , “ I want to follow you , Lord , but first allow me to say good-bye to those who are at my house .”

nama: Yesum yawangoyéng, “Emo tarégh ásáfogh térnyangé a néreta sémta, fá féyotarofnar yém Ngánjánene limánégh ásáfoghét.”
english: But Jesus said to him , “ No one , having put his hand to the plow and looking back , is fit for God ’s Kingdom.”

nama: Yéné soramé Yuram ewaferyéng 72 ámb waitogh ár a sómbiosómbioe ewáfáretam méinyotio tesenot a yuwot ándan fá álighét ménde témorwén.
english: Now after these things , the Lord also appointed seventy others , and sent them two by two ahead of him into every city and place where he was about to come .

nama: Yémon emndai, “Wau yu njamke téfén tarégh siyém tárfár, wénde ásáfogh ár em nambio. Yénémaménnde témndangi wau yuwan njamke téfén Yuré, so ewáfárete ásáfogh ár yáne njamke téfén ndawot.
english: Then he said to them , “ The harvest is indeed plentiful , but the laborers are few . Pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest , that he may send out laborers into his harvest .

nama: Kuwanongiǃ Yéndon fém ewáfáretan ndené wáutáréghofnar tosé sifnit yéfe kénjún munjan ághé.
english: Go your ways . Behold , I send you out as lambs among wolves .

nama: Yau ersati mani yáf a áwogh yáf. Ámb sandol yau ersati. Yau teke árfé asneghét endén.
english: Carry no purse, nor wallet, nor sandals . Greet no one on the way .

nama: “Njam fém kélawangi méngon fronde korayangi, 'Frengégh yém yéné méngon.'
english: Into whatever house you enter , first say , ‘ Peace be to this house .’

nama: Yéné frengégh ár njam kété yém, féfene frengégh so nene yáfaf, yau njam, féfefaf so kénangongé.
english: If a son of peace is there , your peace will rest on him ; but if not , it will return to you .

nama: Kété táf emor yéné méngon, njamke nu némé njam ewarametat nénet, ménamén ásáfogh árém táf ersat yéfenjo fai. Yau nusanati wélafé méngomé.
english: Remain in that same house , eating and drinking the things they give , for the laborer is worthy of his wages . Don’ t go from house to house .

nama: “Njam tesenot kélawangi a taumyongi, enetati féfeyot némé em frengéghkaf.
english: Into whatever city you enter and they receive you , eat the things that are set before you .

nama: Enjne téfnár mer eyátotati efe kété em ewaufrotati ndené, 'Ngánjánene limánégh siyém kaka yém féfefaf.'
english: Heal the sick who are there and tell them , ‘ God ’s Kingdom has come near to you .’

nama: Wénde njam tesenot kélawangi a yau taumyongi, endmé kuwanongi a korayangi,
english: But into whatever city you enter and they don’ t receive you , go out into its streets and say ,

nama: 'Féfene tesenmén ngwérméng nakayongi téfene kafkafan, yéndfem ewainangém féfe matot. Wénde nawafarotati yénémamén: Ngánjánene limánégh kaka yém.'
english: ‘ Even the dust from your city that clings to us , we wipe off against you . Nevertheless know this , that God ’s Kingdom has come near to you .’

nama: Yéndon ewaufrotan, falogh efghon Sodommén némbnare yénmormén wénde minde tukén falogh so yém yéné tesenan.
english: I tell you , it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city .

nama: “Nderé yaufi so yém féfeyot, Korasinǃ Nderé yaufi so yém féfeyot, Betsaidaǃ Yéné sélngwélafé yam árké kufrotawét féfefaf té kufrotawét kété Taire a Sidonén, fá fiya kawengongayénd tándái, fá tamorangérwén saghafé a sakwéfafé.
english: “ Woe to you , Chorazin ! Woe to you , Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you , they would have repented long ago , sitting in sackcloth and ashes .

nama: Falogh efghon Taire a Sidon yéfene tékrat so yém minde tukén, wénde féfene tékratam so taindangé yéfene tékrat.
english: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you .

nama: A fém, Káfenam, so kuyongi njafarot? Yau, fém so keworngi kémboghofnar yékwét.”
english: You , Capernaum , who are exalted to heaven , will be brought down to Hades.

nama: Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár, “Emo fém njam niyáreta, yénd yémon so wiyárete, Njam emo fém nékena, yénd yémon wékene, wénde emo wékene yénd, fá yékene emo yénd wénmáfáretam.”
english: Whoever listens to you listens to me , and whoever rejects you rejects me . Whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me .”

nama: 72 waitogh ár kénangotawét áuwéghafé fá nafroyénd, “Yuré, yaufi mbérmbér mé kénamnyongayénd téfefaf fene yétkwénan.”
english: The seventy returned with joy , saying , “ Lord , even the demons are subject to us in your name !”

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, “Yéndon yinjon Sáifér nénuyai toyanit njafarta.
english: He said to them , “ I saw Satan having fallen like lightning from heaven .

nama: Káyárangiǃ Yéndon muyé ewaramayénd álighét kamban a ndérkamé yátmé a waingéghét mék téfnárfene wafté, némé yam yau so némaghne.
english: Behold , I give you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions , and over all the power of the enemy . Nothing will in any way hurt you .

nama: Wénde yau náuwétati ménamén yaufi mbérmbér namnyongi féfefaf, wénde áuwégh féfeyot ménamén féfene yétkwén ekai njafaran.”
english: Nevertheless , don’ t rejoice in this , that the spirits are subject to you , but rejoice that your names are written in heaven .”

nama: Yéné efghon, Ngánján Mbérmbéram Yesu áuwéghe yafoi norayai, “Yéndon némuwetan fém, Afé, Yuré njafarmén a mbandmén, ménamén fémon némé etnoi yéné yam kétándmé mer mwighé ár a matyam ár tambén wénde sáfét tawakmetau kor matyamofnar árét ambumnit. Néméwét, Afé, yéné témorwén fene mer frengéghafé.
english: In that same hour , Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit , and said , “ I thank you , O Father , Lord of heaven and earth , that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to little children . Yes , Father , for so it was well-pleasing in your sight .”

nama: “Méinyotio yam enramai tafaf tane Afam. Efe ámb yau mat yém Ambumoenemén mé Afaro, a efe ámb yau mat yém efe Afé yém mé Ambumro a fáro efe Ambumom ewaferyéng yá uyafoghét.”
english: Turning to the disciples , he said , “ All things have been delivered to me by my Father . No one knows who the Son is , except the Father , and who the Father is , except the Son , and he to whomever the Son desires to reveal him .”

nama: Féyo Yesu nawengoi yánjo waitogh árfefaf emndai yéfembiaye, “Frengégh yém yéfeyot emofem yinjoyénd fémofem némé yéfandati.
english: Turning to the disciples , he said privately , “ Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,

nama: Ménamén yéndo emndetan ndené tárfár Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a warsagh ár ménde em fandaghét fémofem némé yinjongi, wénde yau yinjoyénd, a náyárangi fémofem némé náyáretati, wénde yau náyárayénd.” Rengerenge si mer Samariamén ároenemén
english: for I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which you see , and didn’t see them , and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them .”

nama: Ámb efghon ámb nák watamegh ár nokayoi Yesue ufoghét a yémotai, “Watamegh ár, yéndon némé so yafrotan efalo tuot ghérsé résaghét?”
english: Behold , a certain lawyer stood up and tested him , saying , “ Teacher , what shall I do to inherit eternal life ?”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Némé si yékai nák kénjún? Fémo ndernáye yafene?”
english: He said to him , “ What is written in the law ? How do you read it ?”

nama: Yémo yawangoi, “'Yuwetang Yuré fene Ngánján méinyotio fene tikfe, méinyotio fene mbérmbére, méinyotio fene waftaye, méinyotio fene mwighaye,' a 'Yuweta fenjo kaka ár ndenéyameyo fémyo ndernáye náuwete.'”
english: He answered , “ You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart , with all your soul , with all your strength , and with all your mind ; and your neighbor as yourself .”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Efalo, yéné yafrota a fém so nakete.”
english: He said to him , “ You have answered correctly . Do this , and you will live .”

nama: Wénde fá ménde témorwén fáyo sái kauyafongai, féyo yémo Yesu yémotai, “A efe yém tane fútar?”
english: But he , desiring to justify himself , asked Jesus , “ Who is my neighbor ?”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Ár nuwanoi Jerusalemta kétán Jeriko, endén yésmetamén oro téfnáram. Yémofem sáláme yéfitérnaménd, yésmetaménd yéfrangoyénd krotro fá nuwanoyénd táf,
english: Jesus answered , “ A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho , and he fell among robbers , who both stripped him and beat him , and departed, leaving him half dead .

nama: ghéngén wárogh ár téngmorwén yéné endmayo wénde njam yinjoi ár, yamnjai téndomé.
english: By chance a certain priest was going down that way . When he saw him , he passed by on the other side .

nama: Yéné endmayo Lefi mérénmén ár ténmorwén yinjoi fá yamnjai téndomé.
english: In the same way a Levite also , when he came to the place and saw him , passed by on the other side.

nama: Wénde Samariamén ár nénwanoi yéné endmayo njam yinjoi karémbkarémb a tikfan ghérghérsisé nawerai.
english: But a certain Samaritan , as he traveled, came where he was . When he saw him , he was moved with compassion ,

nama: Fá néwanoi yáfaf, tiyátotau olif wékére a wéri nue, tumbwetau ngifafmé. Féyo yémon yéfayoi yánjo ndonkiyan a térsau fá kétán yánjo wanjengégh méngot a tanjetau.
english: came to him , and bound up his wounds , pouring on oil and wine . He set him on his own animal , brought him to an inn , and took care of him .

nama: Ámb efghon yémo sómbio silfé mani mbwe yaramai méngo wanjengégh téfnárai. Fá noyarai, 'Yanjeta fá, yénd njam nénangotan, fémon njam tárfár mani etarna, yéndon so kémawasongén.' Mer Samaria ár (Luk 10.31-32)|src="SPP-231.tif" size="col" copy="Nan Pollard © 1998 Standard Publishing." ref="10.31-32"
english: On the next day , when he departed, he took out two denarii , gave them to the host, and said to him , ‘ Take care of him . Whatever you spend beyond that , I will repay you when I return .’

nama: “Yéné nambio mérénta fém ndernáye nemne efe fútar yém yáyot efe oro téfnáram yésmetaménd?”
english: Now which of these three do you think seemed to be a neighbor to him who fell among the robbers ?”

nama: Nák watamegh árom yawangoi, “Yéné ár fiyém emon ghérghérsisafé yinjoi.” Yesum yémndai, “Nguwano a yénéyamyo yafrota.” Mata a Meri
english: He said , “ He who showed mercy on him .” Then Jesus said to him , “ Go and do likewise .”

nama: Yesu a yánjo waitogh ár tanmorwén néndkéfan, nufaryénd méngotuot, amaf yétkwén Mata Yesu yumyoi yánjo méngot.
english: As they went on their way , he entered into a certain village , and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house .

nama: Matane yánngén yétkwén Meri efe námnjoi Yurane kafkaffaf tiyáretau fá némé korayau.
english: She had a sister called Mary , who also sat at Jesus ’ feet and heard his word .

nama: Wénde Mata mwighayam tawérsotau ásáfogh tárfármén. Féyo Mata yénmormén Yesuefaf yémotai, “Yuré, fém mat ném Merim yénd té wéfrango tambia ásáfoghan? Ténémnde ta wáutárghét.”
english: But Martha was distracted with much serving , and she came up to him , and said , “ Lord , don’ t you care that my sister left me to serve alone ? Ask her therefore to help me .”

nama: Yuram yawangoi, “Mata, Mata, fém némawérsotat a mwighat néméindat tárfár rokaram,
english: Jesus answered her , “ Martha , Martha , you are anxious and troubled about many things ,

nama: wénde ámbiro rokar náláite. Merim té yafer némé mer yém, a yau so tanangé yá tambén.”
english: but one thing is needed . Mary has chosen the good part , which will not be taken away from her .”

nama: Ámb efghon Yesu Ngánjánjafé koraiwét fénaté sifayan. Njam yifoi, ámb yánjo waitogh árém yémndai fá, “Yuré, ténmatameta Ngánjánafé orayégh, ndené fété Joném ndernáye tawatametau yánjo waitogh ár.”
english: When he finished praying in a certain place , one of his disciples said to him , “ Lord , teach us to pray , just as John also taught his disciples .”

nama: Yesum emndai fá, “Fém njam Ngánjánafé norayet, ndené koraya: “Afé, utmanéghafé fene njúnnjúnofnar yétkwén, fene limánégh so yéném.
english: He said to them , “ When you pray , say , ‘ Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy. May your Kingdom come . May your will be done on earth, as it is in heaven.

nama: Méinyotio efghon njamke so yénmaramete.
english: Give us day by day our daily bread.

nama: Kénmawalindangé téfene yaufi, ménamén yénd yéfene yaufi yénmawalinda emofem ewafrotat téfefaf. A yau yénd yénérsa ufoghan.”
english: Forgive us our sins, for we ourselves also forgive everyone who is indebted to us . Bring us not into temptation , but deliver us from the evil one .’”

nama: Féyo Yesum emndai fá, “Fém ámb njam ném fútarafé, a fá nuwanoi yáfaf sémbár néndkéfan a norayai, 'Fútar, kwaram nambio féskaf flawé,
english: He said to them , “ Which of you , if you go to a friend at midnight and tell him , ‘ Friend , lend me three loaves of bread,

nama: ménamén tane fútar té téném nayuta tafaf, a yéndon némé ámb yau téfrendan yáyot!'
english: for a friend of mine has come to me from a journey , and I have nothing to set before him ,’

nama: “Efe kénjún témorwén yawangoi, 'Yau wawérsota. Sésafne siyém manéghkaf yém, tane ambumtértér a yénd kémégh yénkém. Yénd yau so kanon a némé yau so kémaraman.'
english: and he from within will answer and say , ‘Don’ t bother me . The door is now shut , and my children are with me in bed . I can ’ t get up and give it to you ’?

nama: Yéndon némndetan fém, fá yau njam kanongé a kémaramangé féskaf flawé ménamén fém ném yáne fútar, wénde fá so kanongé a kémaramangé méinyotio fém némat ménde ném, fém yau wáiwái ném ménamén mé sái nómnenda.
english: I tell you , although he will not rise and give it to him because he is his friend , yet because of his persistence, he will get up and give him as many as he needs .

nama: “Féyo yénd norayan féfefaf: Kamota a so kémaramangé; yéronja a so tinjo; náutána a feyot sésafne so kasawérngé.
english: “ I tell you , keep asking , and it will be given you . Keep seeking , and you will find . Keep knocking , and it will be opened to you .

nama: Ménamén méinyotio ár emofem namotat so yanangi, méinyotio ár efe yéronjat so tinjongi; fá efe náutánat, sésafne so kasawérngé.
english: For everyone who asks receives . He who seeks finds . To him who knocks it will be opened.

nama: “Fém afé, fenjo ambumom njam kénmamnendangé wagifot, némé so taram kamban?
english: “ Which of you fathers , if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone ? Or if he asks for a fish , he won’ t give him a snake instead of a fish , will he ?

nama: O njam kénmamnendangé ghakér kowi mbweyot, némé so taram ndérkamé yátmé?
english: Or if he asks for an egg , he won’t give him a scorpion , will he ?

nama: Féyo fém ndemamén yaufi ném, fém mat ném mer rokar ramégh fenjo ambumafet. Nderé mer yém minde tukén fene Afé njafaran tawaramangé Ngánján Mbérmbér yéfeyot efe nómnendat yáfafǃ” Yesu a Beljebab
english: If you then , being evil , know how to give good gifts to your children , how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him ?”

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum yaufi mbérmbér yutoryéng babé ároeta, a njam yaufi mbémbéram yéfrangoi, ár efe babé témorwén si nufngoi. Ár mérén sélngwél emormén.
english: He was casting out a demon , and it was mute . When the demon had gone out , the mute man spoke ; and the multitudes marveled .

nama: Wénde ámb ár norayayénd, “Yaufi mbérmbérafene sénko téfnároene yétkwénan Beljebab, Yesum yaufi mbérmbér eutone.”
english: But some of them said , “ He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons .”

nama: Ámb árém yufotam Yesu ámogh sie yémotayénd njafar tukmén yam wiyafoghét.
english: Others , testing him , sought from him a sign from heaven .

nama: Yesu mat namndai yéfene mwighé, féyo emndai fá: “Árké yu afaloghét kufngongé yambiayot so kirorngé a árké méngo mérén afaloghét kufngongé yambiayot so kuyangé.
english: But he , knowing their thoughts , said to them , “ Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation. A house divided against itself falls .

nama: Njam Sáifér afaloghét kufngongé yambiayot, ndernáye yáne yu so yakai? Yéndon norayan yéné ménamén fém nemnati ndené Beljebabene yétkwénan yéndon kar yaufi mbérmbér eutonan.
english: If Satan also is divided against himself , how will his kingdom stand ? For you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.

nama: Njam yéndo eutonan yaufi mbérmbér Beljebabene yétkwénan, wét endene yétkwénan eutonat fene waitogh árém? Fenjo waitogh árém efalo niyátotat fém yau mbarkánd ném.
english: But if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your children cast them out ? Therefore they will be your judges .

nama: Wénde njam yéndon eutonan yaufi mbérmbér Ngánján waftaye táf nde Ngánján limánégh yéna yém féfefaf.
english: But if I by God ’s finger cast out demons , then God ’s Kingdom has come to you .

nama: “Njam muyafé ár yakai umaneghkaf yambwete yánjo méngo táf yáne rokar mer so em.
english: “ When the strong man , fully armed , guards his own dwelling, his goods are safe .

nama: Wénde njam minde muyafé árom tésmangé a taindangé fá, yémon tangwanangé mék rokar árkéménayan yuré kamyotau a efaloi némé yémon ewanai oroe.
english: But when someone stronger attacks him and overcomes him , he takes from him his whole armor in which he trusted , and divides his plunder .

nama: “Efe yau yéndfé yénre, fá siyém ta kenagh ár, a emon yénd yau sái wáutána umengan, fá so nironat.
english: “ He who is not with me is against me . He who doesn’ t gather with me scatters .

nama: “Yaufi mbérmbér njam kéntorngé ár tambén, fá nguwanongé fanfne yuwot sifayé ronjaghét enághét. Wénde yau njam sifayé tinjongé, so korayangé, 'Yénd so nangotan kétán árké méngota wénmormén.'
english: The unclean spirit , when he has gone out of the man , passes through dry places , seeking rest; and finding none , he says , ‘ I will turn back to my house from which I came out .’

nama: Njam kénangongé tinjongé méngo sámburéghkaf njúnnjúnofnar a mer frengéghkaf.
english: When he returns, he finds it swept and put in order .

nama: Féyo yaufi mbérmbér kuwanongé a tanwasrongé ámb 7 mbérmbér minde yaufi ndené fáyo, fá kélawangi a emor kété. A ároene ghérsé minde mámáfi kamndangé kétándmé fronde ndernáye témorwén.”
english: Then he goes and takes seven other spirits more evil than himself , and they enter in and dwell there . The last state of that man becomes worse than the first .”

nama: Yesum njam korayau yénémamén, amaf nutnayéng ár mérén kénjún, “Frengéghkaf amaf yém emo náráftam a endene nonoyan kanotau.”
english: It came to pass , as he said these things , a certain woman out of the multitude lifted up her voice and said to him , “ Blessed is the womb that bore you , and the breasts which nursed you !”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Frengéghkaf fá em efe náyáretat Ngánjánene si a wafroghan tékmangi.” Jonane yam ufarogh
english: But he said , “ On the contrary , blessed are those who hear the word of God , and keep it .”

nama: Njam ár mérén tárfár kénáyátotau, Yesu norayai, “Yéné yaufiyaufi ár mérén em. Fá namotat Ngánjánene wafroghyamot, wénde némé yau so tawaramangan mé Jonane ufarogh yamro.
english: When the multitudes were gathering together to him , he began to say , “ This is an evil generation . It seeks after a sign . No sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah the prophet.

nama: Ndené Jona témorwén Ninéfa tesenmén árét yam ufarogh, ndené endeyo Ároene Ambum so wéi yém yam ufarogh yéné ár mérnat.
english: For even as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites , so the Son of Man will also be to this generation .

nama: Sifa ndimbal yuoene warsagh amé so kokayongé moghét yéné ár mérnafefaf a so tawafalngonga ndené fá srem yaufiyamafé; ménamén yéné amaf yénmormén yu nayu Solomonene káfeté mwighé áyárghét, a ámb ár yéna yém yá tukén Solomon.
english: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation and will condemn them , for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon ; and behold , one greater than Solomon is here .

nama: Ár Ninéfamén so kokayongi moghan yéné ár mérénfaf a so tafalngongi, ménamén fá nawengoyénd Jonane si waitoghan, a ámb ár yéna yém yá tukén Jona. Fifioene Kwayaghé
english: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation , and will condemn it , for they repented at the preaching of Jonah ; and behold , one greater than Jonah is here .

nama: “Árké árom kwayaghé yau so tékmangé kété ánde so yétno, o mbáfyáf mbanmbanan. Wénde yémon téfayongé yánjo fayogh sifayan, ménát efe njam eném yémofem kwayaghé so yéfandat.
english: “ No one , when he has lit a lamp , puts it in a cellar or under a basket , but on a stand, that those who come in may see the light .

nama: Fene ghékéf sem kwayaghé em fene fifiot. Fene ghékéf njam mer ere, fene méinyotio fifi so yém kwayaghayan. Wénde njam mámáfi ere fene ghékéf, fene fifi so yém méinyotio kwéfkwéfan.
english: The lamp of the body is the eye . Therefore when your eye is good , your whole body is also full of light ; but when it is evil , your body also is full of darkness .

nama: Kor fém aném ménát kwayaghé fene kénjún yau táf yém kwéfkwéf.
english: Therefore see whether the light that is in you isn’ t darkness .

nama: Féyo táf fene méinyotio fifi sái yém ndimbal kwayaghayan, ámb fifi kafe yau so yém kwéfkwéfan, so yém méinyotio kwayaghayan njam lambefane kwayaghé kátremngongé fefaf.” Yesum táikér si tamndetau Farasi ár a nák watamegh téfnár.
english: If therefore your whole body is full of light , having no part dark , it will be wholly full of light , as when the lamp with its bright shining gives you light .”

nama: Yesum njam si yifoi Farasi árom yumyoi yémafe nénet. Féyo Yesu naflai a námnjoi nénet.
english: Now as he spoke , a certain Pharisee asked him to dine with him . He went in and sat at the table.

nama: Wénde Farasi ár sélngwél yémormén ménamén ngángé umbarghofnar Yesum néne ewafngoi.
english: When the Pharisee saw it , he marveled that he had not first washed himself before dinner.

nama: Yuram yémndai Farasi ár, “Fém Farasi ár kafusi a sin faruro esénnati, wénde kénjún fém sem kwénjta a yaufi kér ro.
english: The Lord said to him , “ Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter , but your inward part is full of extortion and wickedness .

nama: Fém afrengéghofnar árǃ Fém mat em emo faru yafrotam, wéi ndenáyo yafrotam kénjú?
english: You foolish ones, didn’ t he who made the outside make the inside also ?

nama: Wénde tawaram némé enyau fene kénjún yauyau árét, méinyotio so em féfeyot njúnnjúnofnar.
english: But give for gifts to the needy those things which are within , and behold , all things will be clean to you .

nama: “Nderé mámáfi so yém Farasi ár féfeyot, ménamén féfene ramégh siyém Ngánjáneyot méinyotio ndau tambén, wénde fémofem yékenati Ngánjánene uwégh a mbarkánd yam. Fémofem so otameghét yékmetati Ngánjánene uwégh a mbarkánd yam, a yau nékmbotati ndau njamke raméghta.
english: But woe to you Pharisees ! For you tithe mint and rue and every herb , but you bypass justice and God ’s love. You ought to have done these , and not to have left the other undone.

nama: “Nderé mámáfi so yém Farasi ár féfeyot, ménamén fémofem méndefé yéfandati mer ámnjogh sifayé áumengégh méngon, a áuwégh si utmanégh térarégh sifayan.
english: Woe to you Pharisees ! For you love the best seats in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces.

nama: “Nderé mámáfi so yém Farasi ár féfeyot, ménamén fém ndenanit em wayamngoghofnar mbéwé, árkamé ár eyota matofnar.”
english: Woe to you , scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like hidden graves , and the men who walk over them don’ t know it .”

nama: Ámb nák watamegh árom yawangoi, “Watamegh ár, njam fém noraye yénémamén, fémon mámáfi yénd yényátote ndenáye.”
english: One of the lawyers answered him , “ Teacher , in saying this you insult us also .”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Fém, nák watamegh ár, nderé mámáfi so yém féfeyot, ménamén fémofem árét ewafayotati kérté résaghét, wénde fémofem yau so ewáutánati kérté résaghét ámbiro tombé min ngángaye yéfe wáutárghét.
english: He said , “ Woe to you lawyers also ! For you load men with burdens that are difficult to carry, and you yourselves won’ t even lift one finger to help carry those burdens .

nama: “Nderé mámáfi so yém féfeyot, ménamén fémofem mbéwé méngo erametati yéfeyot Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfet, efe féfene ákifem té esmetaménd.
english: Woe to you ! For you build the tombs of the prophets , and your fathers killed them .

nama: Fém nafrotati efalo yiyátotati féfene ákifem némé té yafrotaménd; yémofem esmetaménd Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a fémofem yéfene mbéwé méngo erametati.
english: So you testify and consent to the works of your fathers . For they killed them , and you build their tombs.

nama: Yéné téfnan, Ngánján yánjo káfeté mwighé norayam, 'Yéndon so ewáfáretan Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a Yesuene muyafé ár, wénde ámb so esmetat, ámb wéi so ewifnat.'
english: Therefore also the wisdom of God said , ‘ I will send to them prophets and apostles; and some of them they will kill and persecute ,

nama: Féyo ár mérén yénanotio sénonjo efogh so em téfnot ménamén Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfene férék eréghmén kétándmayo rényégh,
english: that the blood of all the prophets , which was shed from the foundation of the world , may be required of this generation ,

nama: kétándmé Abelene férék kétán Jakarayane férék, efe yésmetaménd ghéngén wárogh kitaré a njúnnjúnofnar sifayé yéné néndkéfan. Néméwét, yéndon némaufrotan fe, ár mérén yénanotio sénonjo efogh so em téfeyot méinyotioat.
english: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary.’ Yes , I tell you , it will be required of this generation .

nama: “Nderé mámáfi so yém nák watamegh téfnárét, ménamén fémofem mé té tamndawét yéné ki matyam sésafne sauréghmén. Fémyo yau so kélawangi kénjút, a fémofem ekmbotati fá efe élaughét narametat.”
english: Woe to you lawyers ! For you took away the key of knowledge . You didn’ t enter in yourselves , and those who were entering in , you hindered .”

nama: Njam Yesu nuwanoi kété, Farasi ár a nák watamegh téfnár nékwe tasotawét a ámogh si tarametawét tárfár yammén,
english: As he said these things to them , the scribes and the Pharisees began to be terribly angry, and to draw many things out of him ,

nama: kawanjetawét sái terangayénd Yesu ménáyan némé yaufi si sái korayangai.
english: lying in wait for him , and seeking to catch him in something he might say , that they might accuse him .

nama: Njam ár mérén náumendaménd, féyo fárofáro kafkafe katumndawét. Yesum si yérnyai fronde yánjo waitogh árfefaf korayau, “Fém so nawarawenati kétándmé Farasi árfé unyégh ghérsé tambén.
english: Meanwhile , when a multitude of many thousands had gathered together , so much so that they trampled on each other , he began to tell his disciples first of all , “ Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees , which is hypocrisy .

nama: Efogh yéném némé méinyotio yafar so kanendangé, a némé ténoghe yém so nufanat.
english: But there is nothing covered up that will not be revealed, nor hidden that will not be known .

nama: Fém némé noraye kwéfitayan so kufarngé kwayaghat, a fémon némé yawanjai ténganan kénjúnro so yaitote kétándmé méngo sémsota.
english: Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light . What you have spoken in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops .

nama: “Yéndon ewaufrotan, tane fútaréf, férfér yau em emofem fifi esmetat a yéné soramé yémofem yau némé so yafrotat.
english: “ I tell you , my friends , don’ t be afraid of those who kill the body , and after that have no more that they can do .

nama: Wénde yéndon ewauyafotan féfeyot efe so férférafé yéfandati: férférafé yéfandati Ngánján, féfene ngi soramé, fá muyafé yém emo so ewite fését. Néméwét, fá yéné yém efalofifi fémofem efe so férférafé yéfandati.
english: But I will warn you whom you should fear . Fear him who after he has killed, has power to cast into Gehenna. Yes , I tell you , fear him .

nama: Némé 5 némbne men tamorwén 20 toyaye? Wénde némé yau wawalingé yém Ngánjánene kénjún.
english: “Aren’ t five sparrows sold for two assaria coins ? Not one of them is forgotten by God .

nama: Néméwét, féne sénko séf sem Ngánjánene waféghkaf em. Fém yau férférafé em. Fémofem ewaindati némbne men tértér.
english: But the very hairs of your head are all counted. Therefore don’ t be afraid . You are of more value than many sparrows .

nama: “Yéndon ewaufrotan, emofem yénd wiyátotat safén nde fá em tane, ndenéyameyo Ároene Ambumom so efalo tayátongé nde fá em tane kété Ngánjánene njafar si résagh árfene sáfrére.
english: “ I tell you , everyone who confesses me before men , the Son of Man will also confess before the angels of God ;

nama: Wénde emofem njam wékenat yénd ár safén wéi so ekenan Ngánjánene njafar si résagh árfene sáfrére.
english: but he who denies me in the presence of men will be denied in the presence of God ’s angels.

nama: Efe njam noraya Ároene Ambumoeta so tawalindangé, wénde efe njam noraya yaufi si Ngánján Mbérmbéroefaf yau so tawalindangé.
english: Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but those who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

nama: “Njam fém nénémnjat áumengégh méngot, limánégh ár a muyafé árfefaf, fém yau mámé mwighé em ndernáye fém so nawamarotati o fém némé so norayati,
english: When they bring you before the synagogues , the rulers, and the authorities , don’ t be anxious how or what you will answer or what you will say ;

nama: ménamén Ngánján Mbérmbéram so ewatamete fém yéné efghon némé so norayati.” Wemár mámáfi ároene rengerenge si
english: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that same hour what you must say .”

nama: Ámb ár norayai mérén kénjún yáfaf, “Watamegh ár, témnde tane ané faloghét némé tane afam euroi.”
english: One of the multitude said to him , “ Teacher , tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me .”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Fútar, emo wékafai yénd ménát yéndon efalotan rokarrokar féfene néndkéfan sómbio ár?”
english: But he said to him , “ Man , who made me a judge or an arbitrator over you ?”

nama: Ndenaye fá norayai yéfefaf, “Kor fété! Ménamén féfene méinyotio fénatafénaté kwénjtamén; ménamén féfene ghérsé yau so rokaram tawanjarngé.”
english: He said to them , “ Beware ! Keep yourselves from covetousness , for a man ’s life doesn’ t consist of the abundance of the things which he possesses.”

nama: Féyo Yesum emndayéng yéné rengerenge si: “Kété témorwén ámb wemár ár efe témorwén mband yuré némé kanotawét mer rokar ndawan.
english: He spoke a parable to them , saying , “ The ground of a certain rich man produced abundantly.

nama: Fá nemnayéng, 'Yéndo némé so yafrotan? Tane yau kor sifayé yém ánde tane méinyotio rokar so ewamndan.'
english: He reasoned within himself , saying , ‘ What will I do , because I don’ t have room to store my crops ?’

nama: “Féyo fá norayai, 'Yéné fiyém yéndon némé so yafrotan. Yéndon so yironan tane wamángégh méngo so yérametan ndimbalfifi, a kété yéndon tanjo ndau rokar a tanjo ámb rokar so ewamndan.
english: He said , ‘ This is what I will do . I will pull down my barns, build bigger ones , and there I will store all my grain and my goods.

nama: Yénd so korayangén tandio táf, “Tafaf em tárfár mer rokar ekmang tárfár yunjéf. Afrát ghérsaye so némor; njamke néne kénjún, wéri nu néneyan a so náuwéte.”'
english: I will tell my soul , “ Soul , you have many goods laid up for many years . Take your ease, eat , drink , and be merry .”’

nama: “Wénde Ngánjánam yémndai fá, 'Fém mámáfi árǃ Yéné sémbáran fémon fene ghérsé so téram. Féyo emon so tawanangé méinyotio rokar fémon némé té táfrende fendio?'
english: “ But God said to him , ‘ You foolish one , tonight your soul is required of you . The things which you have prepared — whose will they be ?’

nama: “Yéné fiyém ndernáye so yém ámb ár emon rokar ewamnde yandio wénde yau em wemár ár Ngánján kénjún.” Mámé mwighé yau
english: So is he who lays up treasure for himself , and is not rich toward God .”

nama: Féyo Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár, “Yénémamén yéndon emndetan, mwighayam yau ewawérsota ménamén féfene ghérsamén, némé so enetati; ménamén féfene fifimén a némé so ewawo.
english: He said to his disciples , “ Therefore I tell you , don’ t be anxious for your life , what you will eat , nor yet for your body , what you will wear.

nama: Ghérsé siyém minde tukén, wénde njamke siyém yuwanyo yém, a fifi siyém minde tukén, wénde sáláme siyém yuwanyo yém.
english: Life is more than food , and the body is more than clothing .

nama: Kéretangi kétán kwagh kés em: yémofem yau eitat o etanat, yéfene wamángégh méngo yau ewam; wénde Ngánjánam emanjne fá. Fém em minde tukén Ngánjánene ghékfan a ewaindati yéné men!
english: Consider the ravens : they don’ t sow , they don’ t reap , they have no warehouse or barn, and God feeds them . How much more valuable are you than birds !

nama: Fém so féyotaro em ndené teke sái emor ménáyan mwighé wawérsoghan yénémamén?
english: Which of you by being anxious can add a cubit to his height?

nama: Táf njam yau ewafrotati yéné némbne rokar, fém némamén mámé mwighé so nemnati ámb rokarmén?
english: If then you aren’ t able to do even the least things , why are you anxious about the rest ?

nama: “Kéretangi ndernáye kéghat mbumbu nanotat. Fá yau násáfotat minde yéfenjo sálámet. Wénde yéndon emndetan, yau mer ndené Solomon yáne méinyotio merfifi rokar kénjún fá umaneghkaf témorwén ndené mbumbu kés em.
english: Consider the lilies , how they grow. They don’ t toil , neither do they spin ; yet I tell you , even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these .

nama: Fá Ngánján yém emo ewamanete kéghat yus, árkéméné sénonjo yéna em káye kawaindangi a ewite fését. Nderé minde tukén yémon némamanete fém? Nderé némbne mwighafé fém emǃ
english: But if this is how God clothes the grass in the field, which today exists and tomorrow is cast into the oven, how much more will he clothe you , O you of little faith ?

nama: Fém yau féfenjo tikéfan nuitati njamkemén a numén, fém yau mwighayam ewawérsota yénémamén.
english: “Don’t seek what you will eat or what you will drink ; neither be anxious.

nama: Ménamén téndo ár yéné mband maratan nérsat yéné rokarot, a fene Afé mat yém féfene némé ménde em.
english: For the nations of the world seek after all of these things , but your Father knows that you need these things .

nama: Wénde yéronjati Ngánjánene limánégh, a yéné rokar so tawaramangé féfeyot féyotaro.
english: But seek God ’s Kingdom, and all these things will be added to you .

nama: “Férfér yau em, némbne mérén, ménamén féfene Afé áuwéghan yém so tawaramangé Ngánjánene limánégh.
english: “Don’ t be afraid , little flock , for it is your Father ’s good pleasure to give you the Kingdom.

nama: Fémofem tátérnangi féfene némé ewam a tawaramangi yauyau árét. Néusnati mani yáf féfendio árkéméné yau so kefongi, féfene ngonjél so njafaran yém kété ánde yau so kefongi, ménamén yau oro téfnáram so tawanangi a yau so ghénjam ewasérmbotat.
english: Sell what you have and give gifts to the needy. Make for yourselves purses which don’ t grow old , a treasure in the heavens that doesn’ t fail , where no thief approaches and no moth destroys .

nama: Ménamén ánde féfene ngonjél yém, féfene tikéf kété so em. Kor ghékéf sénté em
english: For where your treasure is , there will your heart be also .

nama: “Túmén afrengéghkaf aem Ngánjánene ásáfoghét méinyotio efogh a sémbár,
english: “ Let your waist be dressed and your lamps burning .

nama: ndenanit ásáfogh ár kawanjetawét ménát yéfene ndimbal ár so nénangote awerégh téméndta, fá njam nénangota a náutána, yémofem sésafne táfandio so tasawérngi yáyot.
english: Be like men watching for their lord when he returns from the wedding feast , that when he comes and knocks , they may immediately open to him .

nama: Nderé áuwégh so yém yéfeyot yéné ásáfogh árét yéfenjo yuram tawinjongé fá táf afrengéghkaf njam em fá sái nénangota. Yéndon féfe ewaufrotan efaloe, yémon so téfitrarngé awerégh sáláme ménát sái efrenda faiofnar ásáfogh ár, so tamndangé ámnjoghét njamke sifayan a sái ewarameta yéfeyot njamke.
english: Blessed are those servants whom the lord will find watching when he comes . Most certainly I tell you that he will dress himself , make them recline, and will come and serve them .

nama: Nderé áuwégh so yém yéfeyot ásáfogh árét yéfene yuram njam tawinjongé afrengéghkaf, fá njam nénangota yéné efghon sémbár néndkéfan o akwan faurghan.
english: They will be blessed if he comes in the second or third watch and finds them so .

nama: Wénde fém so mat em: Méngo yuré mat njam té témorwén árké efogh fénefénean oro téfnár so ténmorwén, yémo yau so tumyongé oro téfnár yáne méngo fétrarghét.
english: But know this , that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming , he would have watched and not allowed his house to be broken into .

nama: Fém so afrengéghkaf em, ménamén Ároene Ambum sái yéném yéné efoghféneféneyan fém yau njam nawanjeta afrengéghofnar.”
english: Therefore be ready also , for the Son of Man is coming in an hour that you don’ t expect him .”

nama: Fitam yémotai, “Yuré, Fémo yéné rengerenge si noraye téfendro o méinyotio árét?”
english: Peter said to him , “ Lord , are you telling this parable to us , or to everybody?”

nama: Yuram yawawangoi, “Efe ndené yém ndimbal mwighafé a mer wanjengégh ár? Fá yéné féyém yánjo yuram so tékmangé méngo wanjengét sái ewarameta njamke efoghféneféneyan?
english: The Lord said , “ Who then is the faithful and wise steward , whom his lord will set over his household , to give them their portion of food at the right times ?

nama: Nderé mer yém yéné ásáfogh téfnárai yáne yuram tinjongé yáne ásáfoghan fá njam nénangota.
english: Blessed is that servant whom his lord will find doing so when he comes .

nama: Yéndon némaufrotan efaloe, yémo so tékmangé fá wanjengét yáne méinyotio rokaran.
english: Truly I tell you that he will set him over all that he has.

nama: Wénde táf faiofnar ásáfogh ár norayai yandio, 'Tane yuram yérse teke efogh angoghét,' a yémon ngi ewafngoi ásáfogh ár a amaf, a njamke nénet a wéri nu nénet ménát wériot.
english: But if that servant says in his heart , ‘ My lord delays his coming ,’ and begins to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink and to be drunken ,

nama: Yéné ásáfogh téfnárane yuré so nénangote yéné efghon njam fá yau yánjeta fá a yau yéné efghon mat yém. Yáne yuram so tékrátan tékmangé kéran a yémon so ewite yémafé áyáréghofnar árfé.
english: then the lord of that servant will come in a day when he isn’ t expecting him and in an hour that he doesn’ t know , and will cut him in two, and place his portion with the unfaithful.

nama: “Ásáfogh téfnár efe mat yém yáne ndimbaloene ménde wénde yau náfrenda o yau yafrota yáne yurane ménde, fá so efmote minde.
english: That servant who knew his lord ’ s will , and didn’ t prepare nor do what he wanted, will be beaten with many stripes,

nama: Wénde ásáfogh téfnár efe yau mat yém a emo yaufi yafrote, fá so efmote némbnare. Kétándmé méinyotio árét endefet ewaramete ndimbal yéfeyot, ndené ndimbalyo so taramangé. A kétándmé emon njam efalo yiyátota ndimbale, ndené ndimbaleyo so taramangé. Yau krufrát yam wénde falogh
english: but he who didn’ t know , and did things worthy of stripes , will be beaten with few stripes . To whomever much is given , of him will much be required ; and to whom much was entrusted , of him more will be asked.

nama: “Yénd wénmormén fés yénérsamén yéné mbandot, yénd nemnan fés té nawaro wénde yau nawaroǃ
english: “ I came to throw fire on the earth . I wish it were already kindled.

nama: Fronde yénd táf so wém ndimbal tékrátan, a nderé kérté yamamé so wiota kétánotio tane tékrát kefongéǃ
english: But I have a baptism to be baptized with , and how distressed I am until it is accomplished !

nama: Fém nemne yénd wénmormén krufrát yénérsamén yéné mbandot? Yau, yau krufrát, wénde falogh.
english: Do you think that I have come to give peace in the earth ? I tell you , no , but rather division .

nama: Yénandmé sénonjo ámbiro mérén 5 so nafalotat mé fáro, nambiota kétándmé sómbio a sómbiota kétándmé nambio.
english: For from now on , there will be five in one house divided , three against two , and two against three .

nama: Fá so nafalotat, afé kétándmé ambum a ambum kétándmé afé, amé kétándmé mérés a mérés kétándmé amé, tanat alé kétándmé nakum alé a nakum alé kétándmé tanat alé.” Mat áyátogh efogh
english: They will be divided , father against son , and son against father ; mother against daughter , and daughter against her mother ; mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law , and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .”

nama: Fá norayai mérénfaf, “Njam fémofem tawinjongi nu kwéf nénarmbotat mbormborta, efaloe fém so korayangi, 'Nuwét yém,' a so nafamnde.
english: He said to the multitudes also , “ When you see a cloud rising from the west , immediately you say , ‘ A shower is coming ,’ and so it happens .

nama: A njam náikái mbormbor yénéfnje, fém so korayangi, 'So yém wafté,' a so nufrote.
english: When a south wind blows , you say , ‘ There will be a scorching heat ,’ and it happens .
