nama: Efe farun mer néretati wénde kénjún yauǃ Fém mat em ndernáye so tayamngongi yam mbandan a njafaran. Fém yau mat em ndernáye so tayamngongi sénonjo efoghmén.
english: You hypocrites ! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky , but how is it that you don’ t interpret this time ?

nama: “Fém fémyo mat em némé yém mbarkánd.
english: “ Why don’ t you judge for yourselves what is right ?

nama: Emon njam tawinjongé fém kérté wafroghan mato kérté efrendati soramé so engém mogh sifayat. Yau njam, so tawerangi mogh sifayat. Féyo nák wanjengégh árém taramangi kétán félismanefaf a félismaném tákmangi kwéfkwéf sifayan.
english: For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him , lest perhaps he drag you to the judge , and the judge deliver you to the officer , and the officer throw you into prison .

nama: Yéndon emndetan, fem yau so kéntorngi kétánotio fém njam méinyotio fai nufrotati.”
english: I tell you , you will by no means get out of there until you have paid the very last penny. ”

nama: Táf yéné efghon ámb ár tamorwén emofem Yesue yaufroyénd ménamén Galili yu árfene férék Failetém térémnau yéfene ghéngénafé.
english: Now there were some present at the same time who told him about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices .

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Fém nderé nemnati yéné Galili yu árfene yaufiyam árké ewafrotat yémofem ewaindat yaufiyam yéfene ámb ár kés em Galili yumén, yéné ende yémofem tékrát téfandawét?
english: Jesus answered them , “ Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans , because they suffered such things ?

nama: Yéndon emndetan, yauǃ Wénde fém yau njam Ngánjánefaf kawengongi, fém méinyotio wéi kér so em.
english: I tell you , no , but unless you repent , you will all perish in the same way .

nama: O efe 18 kér emormén njam tuk méngo Siloam kénjún nuyai yéfefaf, fém némé nemnati yéfene yaufiyamam ewaindayénd yéfene yaufiyam ámb kés tamorangérwén Jerusalemén?
english: Or those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them — do you think that they were worse offenders than all the men who dwell in Jerusalem ?

nama: Yéndon emndetan, yauǃ Wénde fém yau njam Ngánjánefaf kawengongi, fém méinyotio wéi kér so em.”
english: I tell you , no , but , unless you repent , you will all perish in the same way .”

nama: Féyo yémo tamndetau yéné rengerenge si, “Ár témorwén wén mbwe tiyangérwén yáne géreif mbwe ménj ndau kénjún, a fá téngémorwén wén mbwe ereghét, wénde yémon yau ewinjoi.
english: He spoke this parable . “ A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it and found none .

nama: Féyo fá norayai ároefaf emo tanjetau géreif mbwe ménj ndau, 'Yénanotio nambio yunjéf sénonjo yénd té kwénmorwén wén mbwe ereghét yéné wén mbweyot, wénde yéndo yau yinjon. Ténfend! Némamén mé anogh so nanote yéné mbandan?'
english: He said to the vine dresser, ‘ Behold , these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree , and found none . Cut it down ! Why does it waste the soil ?’

nama: “Árom yawangoi, 'Yuré, mé koro ámbiro yunjfot, a yéndon so yétanan téndomé dégwrot.
english: He answered , ‘ Lord , leave it alone this year also , until I dig around it and fertilize it .

nama: Njam kafongé kétamé yunjfan, merǃ Yau njam, téngnéf.'” Aknyégh amafom frengégh yanai Safatan
english: If it bears fruit , fine; but if not , after that , you can cut it down .’”

nama: Ámb Safatan Yesum tawatametau ámb áumengégh méngon,
english: He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath day .

nama: a amaf témorwén kété yaufi mbérmbéram yékeindam 18 yunjéf. Fá témorwén kanémafé a yau tawalinjotau.
english: Behold , there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years . She was bent over and could in no way straighten herself up .

nama: Njam Yesum yinjoi fá, yémo yémotai fá a yémndai, “Amaf, fém ném mer.”
english: When Jesus saw her , he called her and said to her , “ Woman , you are freed from your infirmity.”

nama: Féyo Yesum ngángé efayoi yáfaf, a táfandio nolinjoyénd a Ngánján tuwetau.
english: He laid his hands on her , and immediately she stood up straight and glorified God .

nama: Áumengégh méngo limángégh ár nékw témorwén ménamén Yesum mer táyátotau Safatan, fá korayau árfefaf, “Kété sem 6 efogh ásáfoghét. Féyo eném frengéghét yéné efghon, yau Safatan.”
english: The ruler of the synagogue, being indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath , said to the multitude , “ There are six days in which men ought to work . Therefore come on those days and be healed , and not on the Sabbath day !”

nama: Yuram yawangoi yá, “Fém efe farun mer néretati wénde kénjún yauǃ Fémofem túmén taférngi kau o ndonki kétándmé kwélalta a talimángi nuot Safatan.
english: Therefore the Lord answered him , “ You hypocrites ! Doesn’ t each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath and lead him away to water ?

nama: Féyo yéné amaf, Ebraamene mérés, efe Sáifram yumbai yékeindam 18 yunjéf. Fá fété nawanjar Safatan kétándmé némé yam yékeinda.”
english: Ought not this woman , being a daughter of Abraham whom Satan had bound eighteen long years , be freed from this bondage on the Sabbath day ?”

nama: Yesum njam korayau yéné, yáne mék téfnár wáiwái yam ewifoi, wénde ár káuwetawét némé mer Yesum tawafrotau. Rengerenge si némbne mbwe a yist
english: As he said these things , all his adversaries were disappointed ; and all the multitude rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him .

nama: Féyo Yesum namotai, “Ndernáye nérete Ngánjánene limánégh? Némayafé so yétémndan?
english: He said , “ What is God ’s Kingdom like ? To what shall I compare it ?

nama: Ndenanit némbne mbwe, árom yéngwanai yiyai yánjo ndawan a nanotam wén náyátoi, a menam njén tarametawét yéné kafeyamé.”
english: It is like a grain of mustard seed which a man took and put in his own garden . It grew and became a large tree , and the birds of the sky live in its branches .”

nama: Wéikor Yesu namotai, “Yéndon némayafé so yétémndan Ngánjánene limánégh?
english: Again he said , “ To what shall I compare God ’s Kingdom?

nama: Ndené fété yist amafom yanai a yérémnam nambio flawé baik. Féyo méinyotio flawé néminam ndimbal kofékofé namndai.” Némbne sésafne
english: It is like yeast , which a woman took and hid in three measures of flour, until it was all leavened .”

nama: Yesu njam tiotau Jerusalemét, yémo tangwatametau tesenamé a méngotuamé.
english: He went on his way through cities and villages , teaching , and traveling on to Jerusalem .

nama: Ámb árom yémotai Yesu, “Yuré, féyotaro nambio árro so tawanjar?” Yémon emndai fá,
english: One said to him , “ Lord , are they few who are saved ?” He said to them ,

nama: “Minde násáfotati ménát fém so kélawangi némbne sésafnemé, ménamén yéndon emndetan, tárfár ár yéta so nufotati élaughét a yau so nufrote.
english: “Strive to enter in by the narrow door , for many , I tell you , will seek to enter in and will not be able .

nama: Táf méngo yuré njam so kénangongé a so témnangé sésafne, fém so ewakai sésafne téndota náutánati a norayati, 'Yuré, sésafne tésawér téfe.' “Wénde yémon so tawawangongé, 'Yénd mafina wém fém, o fém árkamé eném.'
english: When once the master of the house has risen up and has shut the door , and you begin to stand outside and to knock at the door , saying , ‘ Lord , Lord , open to us !’ then he will answer and tell you , ‘ I don’ t know you or where you come from .’

nama: “Féyo fém so korayangi, 'Yéndfem njamke a nu tánetawém fémafé, a fémon ténmatametau endmé.'
english: Then you will begin to say , ‘ We ate and drank in your presence , and you taught in our streets .’

nama: “Wénde yémon so tawawangongé, 'Yénd mafina wém fém o fém árkamé eném. Kitrongi ta tambén, fém méinyotio yaufiyam wafrogh téfnárǃ'
english: He will say , ‘ I tell you , I don’t know where you come from . Depart from me , all you workers of iniquity.’

nama: “Kété siyém yengam a sén krarégh, njam fémon tawinjo Ebraam, Aisek a Jeikof a méinyotio Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár efe em Ngánjánene limánéghan, wénde fém té kéméwitat.
english: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac , Jacob , and all the prophets in God ’s Kingdom, and yourselves being thrown outside .

nama: Ár so eném kétándmé wérwérta a mbormborta a náikáita a yambérméta, a so tawanangi yéfenjo sifayé kété téménd kénjún Ngánjánene limánéghan.
english: They will come from the east, west , north , and south , and will sit down in God ’s Kingdom.

nama: Efe soramé em fá so kénáyátongi frondeyot, efe fronde em fá so kénáyátongi soramat.” Yesuene ghérghérsisé Jerusalemét
english: Behold , there are some who are last who will be first , and there are some who are first who will be last .”

nama: Ámb efghon ámb Farasi ár enmormén Yesuefaf a yémndayénd, “Koro yéné yu a kuano ámb sifayat. Erodém ngiot némafngo fém.”
english: On that same day , some Pharisees came , saying to him , “ Get out of here and go away , for Herod wants to kill you .”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Nguwano taufro yéné ághéághai, 'Yéndon so ewitrotan yaufi mbérmbér, a engéfrendan ár sénonjo a káye, a nambio efogh soramé yéndon so tifon tanjo ásáfogh.'
english: He said to them , “ Go and tell that fox, ‘ Behold , I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I complete my mission.

nama: Wénde yénd mé so wéngém sénonjo a káye a ámb efghon, ámb Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár yau so kér em Jerusalem sémta.
english: Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day , for it can’ t be that a prophet would perish outside of Jerusalem .’

nama: “O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, fém emofem esmetat Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a ewafiyátotat wíme efe enwáfáretam féfefaf, yénd ménde wém fene ár so eumendan, ndenéyame amé ghakér kowiam yáne tosétosé yénau tawamongé njambe, wénde fém yau ménde kénémǃ
english: “ Jerusalem , Jerusalem , you who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her ! How often I wanted to gather your children together , like a hen gathers her own brood under her wings , and you refused !

nama: Kéreta, fene tesen so kafrangongé. Yéndon némaufrotan feyot, fémon yau so kwinjo wéi kétánotio fém njam koraya, 'Nderé frengégh yém efe yéném Yurane yétkwénan!'”
english: Behold , your house is left to you desolate. I tell you , you will not see me until you say , ‘ Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord !’”

nama: Ámb Safatan, njam Yesu téngmorwén Farasi árfene utmanégh ároene méngot nénet, árém kor téfandawét Yesu kakafifi.
english: When he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching him .

nama: Ámb ár efe témorwén kafkaf a ngángé mbwefé yénmorméng Yesuefaf.
english: Behold , a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him .

nama: Yesum emotai Farasi ár a nák watamegh téfnár, “Némé nák yém ménát ár frengéghét Safatan o yau?”
english: Jesus , answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees , saying , “ Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath ?”

nama: Wénde fá métar nufrotaménd. Yesum yéngwasroi ár yéfrendai a yéngwatembai.
english: But they were silent . He took him , and healed him , and let him go .

nama: Yesum emotai, “Njam endene ámbiro ambum o kau kuyangé kétán yékwét Safatan, fémo wékeye yau so turto?”
english: He answered them , “ Which of you , if your son or an ox fell into a well , wouldn’ t immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day ?”

nama: A yéfeyot ámb yau si.
english: They couldn’t answer him regarding these things .

nama: Njam fá mat náyátoi wan téfnáram yaferyéng mer sifayé njamke sékian, yémon tawaufrotau yéné rengerenge si:
english: He spoke a parable to those who were invited , when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them ,

nama: “Njam ámb árom kémumyongé fém kétán awerégh téméndfaf, yau tané yéné tuk sifayé. Yau aem ndené yéné sifayé kés yém nawamnde ároe yémo té yumyo endene em tukén yam a kémaindangé fém.
english: “ When you are invited by anyone to a wedding feast , don’ t sit in the best seat, since perhaps someone more honorable than you might be invited by him ,

nama: Njam nufrota, emon njam taumiye fém so kénaflend a tamndend fém, 'Fene sifayé taram yéné ároe.' Nderé wáiwáiafé yém, fémon so tané yuwon sifayé.
english: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you , ‘ Make room for this person .’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place .

nama: Wénde fém njam kémumyongi, yuwon sifayé tané, fene téménd yuré njam yéném, yémon so kénémndangé, 'Fútar, kimnda tuk sifayat.' Fém so némutémndat wan téfnárafene sáfan.
english: But when you are invited , go and sit in the lowest place , so that when he who invited you comes , he may tell you , ‘ Friend , move up higher.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you .

nama: Efe nawarmbotat fáyo so newonat yuwot, efe njam néwonat fá so kayátongi ndimbal.”
english: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled , and whoever humbles himself will be exalted .”

nama: Féyo Yesu norayai yánjo téménd yurafaf, “Fémon njam ewarameta efoghméf néne o séite néne, fémo yau taumyo fe fútaréf a fene anéf a fene mérén o fene wemár fútaréf, fémon njam yafrota, fém so kémumyongi a so némawafángotat.
english: He also said to the one who had invited him , “ When you make a dinner or a supper , don’ t call your friends , nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen , nor rich neighbors , or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.

nama: Wénde fémon njam tawaram njamke, taumyo yauyau ár, aknyégh ár, wawér, ghékéf kwéfkwéf ár.
english: But when you make a feast , ask the poor , the maimed, the lame , or the blind ;

nama: Yémofem yau so fai némawafángotat. Wénde Ngánjánam so néfrende a fai kémawangogé njam yáne ár nénanotat kérta.” Rengerenge ndimbal téméndmén
english: and you will be blessed , because they don’ t have the resources to repay you . For you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous .”

nama: Soramé Yesum si waitogh yifoi, ámb wan ár norayai, “Frengégh yém yáyot emo so enete téménd kénjún Ngánjánene limánéghan!”
english: When one of those who sat at the table with him heard these things , he said to him , “ Blessed is he who will feast in God ’s Kingdom!”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Ámb árom téfrendau ndimbal téménd a eumyoi tárfár wan téfnár.
english: But he said to him , “ A certain man made a great supper , and he invited many people .

nama: Téménd efghon, yémo ewáfáretam yánjo ásáfogh ár tanwaufrongi efe té eumyon, 'Taném, sénonjo méinyotio rokar siyém frengéghkaf yém.'
english: He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited , ‘ Come , for everything is ready now .’

nama: “Wénde yémofem si waufrogh ewafngoyénd. Fronde ár norayai, 'Yéndon mband té yanan, a yénd so wéngém fandaghét. Ghérghérsisé kwawalo tayot.'
english: They all as one began to make excuses . “ The first said to him , ‘ I have bought a field, and I must go and see it . Please have me excused .’

nama: “Ámb norayai, 'Yéndo té ewanan 5 kau, a yénd álighan wém so eufotan. Ghérghérsisé kwawalo tayot.'
english: “ Another said , ‘ I have bought five yoke of oxen , and I must go try them out . Please have me excused .’

nama: “Wéi ámb norayai, 'Yénd somé naweran. Féyo yénd yau so wéném.'
english: “ Another said , ‘ I have married a wife , and therefore I can ’t come .’

nama: “Ásáfogh téfnár nangotam a kafrotau yéné yánjo yurafaf. Féyo méngo yuré nékwafé naféghetam a yáfáretam yánjo ásáfogh téfnár, 'Nguwano kétor wékeye némbne endmé a kéghat endmé yéné tesenan a tanwasro yauyau ár, aknyégh ár, ghékéf kwéfkwéf ár a wawér.'
english: “ That servant came , and told his lord these things . Then the master of the house , being angry , said to his servant , ‘ Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city , and bring in the poor , maimed, blind , and lame .’

nama: “Ásáfogh téfnárom yémndai 'Yuré, fémon némé wémndai té kufrote, wénde kété mé em sifayé.'
english: “ The servant said , ‘ Lord , it is done as you commanded , and there is still room .’

nama: “Féyo yuram yémndai yánjo ásáfogh téfnár, 'Nguwano kétor némbne endmé a kéghat endét a tanwaurémnau, 'Kénaflangi kénjút,' ménát tane méngo so yém afoghare.
english: “ The lord said to the servant , ‘ Go out into the highways and hedges , and compel them to come in , that my house may be filled .

nama: Yéndon némndetan, efe fronde eumyongén yémofem yau so tufongi tane njamkeǃ'” Waitogh ároene yam
english: For I tell you that none of those men who were invited will taste of my supper . For many are called , but few are chosen. ’”

nama: Ndimbal mérén tayotau Yesufé, a nawengoi yéfefaf yémon emndai:
english: Now great multitudes were going with him . He turned and said to them ,

nama: “Efe njam yéném tafaf sái kamndangé tane waitogh ár, yénd so uwete mindefifi, yénamayo so euwete yánjo afé a amé, yánjo amaf a ambumtértér, yáne ghakér a mérésmérés, néméwét, ndenáyo yánjo ghérsé. Yau njam, fá yau so yém tane waitogh ár.
english: “ If anyone comes to me , and doesn’ t disregard his own father , mother , wife , children , brothers, and sisters, yes , and his own life also , he can ’ t be my disciple .

nama: A emo yau njam yérsa yánjo wafatarégh wén a kwénmaitongé yénd fá yau so yém tane waitogh ár.
english: Whoever doesn’ t bear his own cross and come after me , can ’ t be my disciple .

nama: “Njam efe ár ménde yém ndimbal méngo ramghét, fá fronde so kámnjongé a nufénmeta yéfanda njénamb mani yém wifoghét.
english: For which of you , desiring to build a tower , doesn’ t first sit down and count the cost , to see if he has enough to complete it ?

nama: Yémon so tafngongé méngo ramégh, wénde yau so tifongé. Féyo méinyotio árém yéfandat némé nufrote so yurtat fá.
english: Or perhaps , when he has laid a foundation and isn’ t able to finish , everyone who sees begins to mock him ,

nama: Fá korayawét, 'Yéné árom yérnyai ramégh wénde yau yifoi.'
english: saying , ‘ This man began to build and wasn’ t able to finish .’

nama: “Némé so yafrote warsagh árom táf yáne njam em 10,000 fiyaré yandio awamaroghét kétamé ámb warsagh ár emafé so násmét 20,000 fiyarafé? Fronde njam kuwanongé mékot, féyo fá so kámnjongé mwighé so yéfarone waingéghét?
english: Or what king , as he goes to encounter another king in war , will not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand ?

nama: Fá yau njam yém féyotarofnar, yémon so yáfárete si résagh ár táfandio ámb mé esio a fá so kwómnendangé krufrátot.
english: Or else , while the other is yet a great way off, he sends an envoy and asks for conditions of peace .

nama: Yéné endeyo, fémon tafrango méinyotio rokar, fém so ném tane waitogh ár.
english: So therefore , whoever of you who doesn’ t renounce all that he has , he can ’ t be my disciple .

nama: “Sol siyém mer yém, wénde yáne ménde njam kefongé, ndernáye so sol nawafarnjé wéikor?
english: “Salt is good , but if the salt becomes flat and tasteless , with what do you season it ?

nama: Yau mer yém mbandot o ámb rokarot, árém táf yéngwitat. Efe njam ténganafé yém, so káyárangé yéné si.”
english: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile . It is thrown out . He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

nama: Mani kafégh ár a yaufiyam téfnár méinyotio káumendawét áyárghét yáfaf.
english: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him .

nama: Wénde Farasi ár a nák watamegh ár mike nufngoyénd, “Yéné árom eumyote yaufiyam ár ménát yémafe nénet.”
english: The Pharisees and the scribes murmured , saying , “ This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them .”

nama: Féyo Yesum tawaufrotau yéné rengerenge si,
english: He told them this parable :

nama: “Njam ámbiro ároene em 100 sif a kolindangé ámbiro, némé so yafrote? Yémon efaloe so tafrangongé 99 marat sifayan a nguwanongé olingégh sif ronjaghét kétánotio yémo tinjongé.
english: “ Which of you men , if you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them , wouldn’ t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the one that was lost, until he found it ?

nama: Táf yémon njam tinjongé, yémo áuwéghafé tambnangé sif téfayongé yáne fader tukén.
english: When he has found it , he carries it on his shoulders , rejoicing .

nama: Fá so nguwanongé méngot. Féyo yémon emotai yáne fútaréf a ámb kaka ár a norayai yéfefaf, 'Áuwégh yangotam yéndfé. Yéndon té yinjon tane olingégh sif.'
english: When he comes home , he calls together his friends and his neighbors , saying to them , ‘ Rejoice with me , for I have found my sheep which was lost!’

nama: Yéndon emndetan fém kété njafaran ndenéyameyo kété so yém ndimbal áuwégh. Efaloe áuwégh 99 mbarkánd árfenemén efe em yaufiyamofnar, yéné siyém némbnarfifi, wénde minde áuwégh yém ámbiro yaufiyam ár efe njam kawengongé yánjo yaufita. Olingégh mani mbwe
english: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents , than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance .

nama: “Némé so yafrote amafom táf yáne 10 silfé mani mbwe em a kolindangé ámbiro? Yémon táf lambefé tuwarongé a sámburégh méngo tafngongé ronjagh merkéráye kétánotio yémon tinjongé.
english: “ Or what woman , if she had ten drachma coins , if she lost one drachma coin , wouldn’ t light a lamp , sweep the house , and seek diligently until she found it ?

nama: Féyo yémon njam yinjoi, yémon emotai fútaréf a ámb kaka ár a norayai yéfefaf, 'Áuwégh yangotam yéndfé, yéndon té yinjon tane olingégh mani mbwe.'
english: When she has found it , she calls together her friends and neighbors , saying , ‘ Rejoice with me , for I have found the drachma which I had lost!’

nama: Yéndon emndetan fém, ndené endeyo, kété so yém áuwégh Ngánjánene njafar si résagh ár sáfrére ámbiro yaufiyam ár efe njam kawengongé yaufita.” Olingégh ambum
english: Even so , I tell you , there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting.”

nama: Yesum wéikor si yéngwafngoi, “Kété témorwén ár sómbio ambumafé.
english: He said , “ A certain man had two sons .

nama: Kertrom yémndai yárefé, 'Afé, yénd yau so nawanjetan kétán fém kér ném. Sénonjo kwaram fene rokarta tane némé so em.' Féyo yémon ewafaleyai rokar néndkéfan yánjo sómbio ambumat.
english: The younger of them said to his father , ‘ Father , give me my share of your property.’ So he divided his livelihood between them .

nama: “Féyo tekofnaye, kertrom ewanai yáne némé tamorwén, náfrendam nuwanoi nayu yuwot a mani taranégh yéngwafngoi yaufi ghérsayan.
english: Not many days after , the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country . There he wasted his property with riotous living .

nama: Yéné soramé yémon njam ewifoi méinyotio rokar, kété nuyai káukáu yéné yuwon, féyo fá áronjagh nufngoi sioghmén.
english: When he had spent all of it , there arose a severe famine in that country , and he began to be in need .

nama: Féyo fá nuwanoi ásáfoghét nómnendai ámb árfefaf yéné yuwon, a árom yéngwáfáretam fá kimb wanjengét.
english: He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country , and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs .

nama: Fá minde siogh témorwén, fá ménde témorwén kimbafene njamke mbarmbar nénet. Wénde emo yau taramangai yáyot némndafi.
english: He wanted to fill his belly with the pods that the pigs ate , but no one gave him any .

nama: “Fá njam nénangoi mwighat, fá norayai, 'Méinyotio tane afane faiofnar ásáfogh ár féyotaro njamkeyan em, a yéna yénd swém krotro wémǃ
english: But when he came to himself , he said , ‘ How many hired servants of my father ’s have bread enough to spare, and I ’m dying with hunger !

nama: Yénd so nangotan tane afafaf a témndan, “Afé, yéndon yaufiyam té tafrotan njafarot a fefaf.
english: I will get up and go to my father , and will tell him , “ Father , I have sinned against heaven and in your sight .

nama: Yénd yau féyotaro wém sái wémndeta fene ambum. Yénd kuyáto ndenanit fene faiofnar ásáfogh ár.”'
english: I am no more worthy to be called your son . Make me as one of your hired servants.”’

nama: Féyo fá nokayoi a nuwanoi yárefafaf. “Njam kété mé ténmorwén nayu, yárefam yéngwinjoi fá a ghérghérsisé yam yafoi yánemén. Fá néngengoi yánjo ambumoefaf a yambnai a tésnetau.
english: “ He arose and came to his father . But while he was still far off , his father saw him and was moved with compassion , and ran , fell on his neck , and kissed him .

nama: “Ambumom yémndai yárefé, 'Afé, yéndon yaufiyam té tafrotan njafarot a fefaf. Yénd yau wémndeta fene ambum yénd wém.'
english: The son said to him , ‘ Father , I have sinned against heaven and in your sight . I am no longer worthy to be called your son .’

nama: “Wénde yárefam yémndai yáne faiofnar ásáfogh ár, 'Wékeyeǃ Mer totér sáláme ténmawanangi a tawongi. Talawend ghil yáne ngángayan tawend sandol kafkafan.
english: “ But the father said to his servants , ‘ Bring out the best robe and put it on him . Put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet .

nama: Ténmanengi yarsafé kau tésmangi. So téménd yafrotam a áuwégh yangotam,
english: Bring the fattened calf , kill it , and let ’s eat and celebrate ;

nama: ménamén yéné tane ambum efe kér yémormén sénonjo ghérsé yém. Fá té nolindai a sénonjo té yinjongém.' Féyo yémofem áuwégh tangotawét. Olingégh ambum: yárefé nérse kertér ambumoefaf (Luk 15.20)|src="GR-gn33n_noLabel.tif" size="col" copy="© Global Recordings Network, globalrecordings.net." ref="15.20"
english: for this , my son , was dead and is alive again . He was lost and is found .’ Then they began to celebrate .

nama: “Táfandio, fronde sénko ambum témorwén ndawan. Fá njam nénáyátoi méngo kaka, fá náyárayéng wagh ke a ermángégh.
english: “ Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house , he heard music and dancing .

nama: Féyo yémon yanmoi ámb faiofnar ásáfogh ár a yémotai némé yam nufrote.
english: He called one of the servants to him and asked what was going on .

nama: Yémon yawangoi, 'Fene néngén té nufar, a fene afam yarsafé kau té yésém ménamén fá yéna yém a merro yém.'
english: He said to him , ‘ Your brother has come , and your father has killed the fattened calf , because he has received him back safe and healthy.’

nama: “Féyo fronde sénko ambum nékw náwerai a yau nélawai méngo kénjút. Yárefé néntoryéng a yamnendai élaughét.
english: But he was angry and would not go in . Therefore his father came out and begged him .

nama: Wénde yémo yawangoi yárefai, 'Kéretaǃ Méinyotio yunjéf yéndon ásáfogh tafrotan feyot a yau kwawalinde fene si. Wénde fémon némé ámb yau waramai ta tosé gout ménát yéndfem téménd sái tafrotawém tane fútarafé.
english: But he answered his father , ‘ Behold , these many years I have served you , and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours , but you never gave me a goat , that I might celebrate with my friends .

nama: Wénde njam yéné fene ambum emo fene mani tátérnawéng yaufi ghérayan kénangote méngot, fémon yesmang yarésafé kau yáyotǃ'
english: But when this your son came , who has devoured your living with prostitutes , you killed the fattened calf for him .’

nama: “Yárefé norayai, 'Tane ambum, fém túmén yéndfé ténre. Méinyotio rokar tane sem fene em.
english: “ He said to him , ‘ Son , you are always with me , and all that is mine is yours .

nama: Wénde yéndfem ásneghafé a áuwégh tafrotam ménamén fengén kér yémormén a sénonjo ghérsé yém. Fá nolindai a sénonjo yéna yém.'”
english: But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad , for this , your brother, was dead , and is alive again . He was lost, and is found .’”

nama: Yesum emndai yáne waitogh ár, “Ámb wemár árom yékafai ár yáne rokar wanjengét. Wénde wemár ároe yaufroyénd ndené rokar wanjengégh árom fene mani té ewalinda.
english: He also said to his disciples , “ There was a certain rich man who had a manager . An accusation was made to him that this man was wasting his possessions .

nama: Féyo yémo yanmoi fá a yémotai, 'Némé yéné yém yénd náyáran fenemén? Kwaram fene ásáfoghmén mat ndernaye tanjete, ménamén fém yau so ném wanjengégh ár yéné soramé.'
english: He called him , and said to him , ‘ What is this that I hear about you ? Give an accounting of your management , for you can no longer be manager .’

nama: “Wanjengégh ár norayai yáfafétio, 'Yéndon némé so yafrotan sénonjo? Tane yuram tane ásáfoghta wutér. Yénd muyafnar wém targhét, a yénd wáiwái wém ómnengéghét.
english: “ The manager said within himself , ‘ What will I do , seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me ? I don’ t have strength to dig . I am ashamed to beg .

nama: Yénd mat wém némé yéndon so yafrotan ménát árém mé so umyotat yénd yéfene méngomé njam yéndon tanjo ásáfogh koron.'
english: I know what I will do , so that when I am removed from management , they may receive me into their houses .’

nama: “Féyo yémon tamotau ámbiroámbiroe yáne yurane fai wangoghét. Yémo yémotai fronde ár, 'Njénamb fai ekmang tane yurane?'
english: Calling each one of his lord ’ s debtors to him , he said to the first , ‘ How much do you owe to my lord ?’

nama: Yémo yawangoi, '20 dram olif wékwér.' Wanjengégh árom yémndai fá, 'Téngwan fenjo fefé, kámnjo wékeye, a tafarota 10 dram.'
english: He said , ‘ A hundred batos of oil .’ He said to him , ‘ Take your bill , and sit down quickly and write fifty .’

nama: “Wéi yémo yémotai yénamayo ár, 'Fefaf njénamb em?' Yémon yawangoi yá, '1,000 flawé baik.' Yémon yémndai fá, 'Téngwan fenjo fefé. Tafarota kétán 800 flawé baikot.'
english: Then he said to another , ‘ How much do you owe ?’ He said , ‘ A hundred cors of wheat .’ He said to him , ‘ Take your bill , and write eighty .’

nama: “Yuram tuwetau yáne unyégh rokar wanjengégh ár ménamén fá kawarawenau merkéráye. Ár yéné mbandan nawarawenat merkéráye wénde kwayaghane ár yau ndené em.
english: “ His lord commended the dishonest manager because he had done wisely , for the children of this world are , in their own generation , wiser than the children of the light .

nama: Yéndon ewaufrotan féfe, fútar náyátotati fémyo mband rokere, njam tafrango, fém so kémumyongé tuot méngotuot.
english: I tell you , make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon , so that when you fail , they may receive you into the eternal tents .

nama: “Efe njam ndimbal mwighafé yém némbne yaman, ndenáyo wéi so yém ndimbal yaman. Efe njam unyégh ár némbne yaman yém, ndenáyo wéi ndimbal yaman so yém.
english: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much . He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much .

nama: Féyo fém yau njam efalo ném mbandoene rokar wanjengét, ndernáye fém so niyátotat efalo wemáran?
english: If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon , who will commit to your trust the true riches?

nama: A fém yau njam efalo kéném ámb ároene rokar wanjengét, emon so kémaramangé fenjo rokar?
english: If you have not been faithful in that which is another ’s, who will give you that which is your own?

nama: “Ásáfogh ár yau so yém sómbio yurafene limánégh mbanmbanan. Fémon so yékene ámb a so yuwete ámb, o fém so kamnyong ámbfaf a tékena ámb. Yénémamén fém yau so sómbio limánégh mbanmbanan ném, Ngánján a mani.”
english: No servant can serve two masters , for either he will hate the one and love the other ; or else he will hold to one and despise the other . You aren’ t able to serve God and Mammon .”

nama: Farasi ár sem mani méndero ár em. Yémofem njam náyárayénd yéné si a fén yafngoyénd Yesu.
english: The Pharisees , who were lovers of money , also heard all these things , and they scoffed at him .

nama: Yesum emndai fá, “Fém yéné féyém efe mer nitaro kénamndangi árfene ghékfan, wénde Ngánján mat yém féfene tikéf. Némé minde tukén árfene mwighayan fifiofnar yém Ngánjánene ghékfan.
english: He said to them , “ You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men , but God knows your hearts . For that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God .

nama: “Mosesene nák a Ngánjánene mat yátogh ároene watamegh si táitotawét kétánotio Jonene efogh. Kétándmé yéné efghon, Ngánjánene limánéghmén mer si waitogh yérnyai, méinyotio árém end táronjawét kétán Ngánjánene limánéghfaf.
english: “ The law and the prophets were until John . From that time the Good News of God ’s Kingdom is preached , and everyone is forcing his way into it .

nama: Áfráte yém njafar a mband so kawaindangend, wénde némbne si Ngánjánene sian yau so titrém.
english: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one tiny stroke of a pen in the law to fall .

nama: “Emon njam yánjo amaf tatembangé a kawerangé ámb amaf kamndangé amafanyo wanégh ár a emon njam watembégh amaf kawerangé káyátongé áranyo wanégh ár. Wemár ár a Lajarus
english: “ Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery . He who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery .

nama: “Kété témorwén wemár ár efe kumanetau minde tukén sáláme a témorangérwén yánjo meran méinyotio efghon.
english: “ Now there was a certain rich man , and he was clothed in purple and fine linen , living in luxury every day .

nama: Yáne méngo sésafnefaf kété témorwén ómnengégh ár yétkwén Lajarus efe témorwén fénend kér ro
english: A certain beggar , named Lazarus , was taken to his gate , full of sores ,

nama: a ménde témorwén nénet némé kuitawét kétándmé wemár ároene njamke kitarata. Táfandio ághé tanmorwén tasambnawét yáne fénend. Lajarus wémároene sésfnefaf ághafé (Luk 16.20-21)|src="IB-048gr.tif" size="col" copy="by Farid Faadil. © Biblica, Inc. Used with permission. All rights reserved worldwide." ref="16.20-21"
english: and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man ’s table . Yes, even the dogs came and licked his sores .

nama: “Ómnengégh ár kér yémormén a njafar si résagh ár yénmormén a yéngwasroyénd fá kétán Ebraamefaf. Féyo wemár ár kér yémormén a yiyátotaménd.
english: The beggar died , and he was carried away by the angels to Abraham’s bosom . The rich man also died and was buried .

nama: Efoghofnar yékw sifayan, wemár ár tékrátan témorwén, njam fá néretai tukét yinjoi Ebraam nayuta, yémafé Lajarus yáne téndota.
english: In Hades, he lifted up his eyes , being in torment, and saw Abraham far off , and Lazarus at his bosom .

nama: Féyo fá nutnai, 'Afé Ebraam, tayot ghérghérsisé a témnde Lajarus ngángé tauwarngé nuan a tane kánké krufrát tiyáto ménamén yénd fés kénjún tékrátan wém.
english: He cried and said , ‘ Father Abraham, have mercy on me , and send Lazarus , that he may dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue ! For I am in anguish in this flame .’

nama: “Wénde Ebraamém yawangoi, 'Ambum, kawafarota kété fene ghérsé ámnjogh efghon, fém meran kénmorwén, táf Lajarus yauyauan, wénde sénonjo fá enaghan yém yéna a fém tékrátan.
english: “ But Abraham said , ‘ Son , remember that you , in your lifetime , received your good things , and Lazarus , in the same way , bad things . But here he is now comforted and you are in anguish.

nama: Ámb téfén siyém, féyotarofnar yém yéné ménamén yékw ndimbalam yénfalowé, ménát efe ménde éférmanéghét yénandmé fefaf siyém táikér yém.'
english: Besides all this , between us and you there is a great gulf fixed , that those who want to pass from here to you are not able , and that no one may cross over from there to us .’

nama: “Yémo yawangoi, 'Féyo yéndon némamnendan fém, afé, téngwáfáreta Lajarus kétán tane afane méngot,
english: “ He said , ‘ I ask you therefore , father , that you would send him to my father ’s house —

nama: ménamén tane em 5 néngnéf. Mat so táyátongé fá yau so eném yéné tékrát sifayat.'
english: for I have five brothers— that he may testify to them , so they won’ t also come into this place of torment.’

nama: “Ebraamém yawangoi, 'Buk kété em Moses a Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár efarotaménd, fá sái náyáretat yéfendio.'
english: “ But Abraham said to him , ‘ They have Moses and the prophets . Let them listen to them .’

nama: “Fá norayai, 'Yau, afé Ebraam, ámb kér ár njam néngangota kétán yéfefaf, fá so kawengongi.'
english: “ He said , ‘ No , father Abraham, but if one goes to them from the dead , they will repent .’

nama: “Fá norayai yáfaf, 'Yau njam náyáretat Moses a Ngánjánene mat yátogh ároefaf, fá yau so nawengotat wéi ndenéyameyo kér tambén njam kénangongé ghérsat.'”
english: “ He said to him , ‘ If they don’ t listen to Moses and the prophets , neither will they be persuaded if one rises from the dead .’”

nama: Yesu norayai yánjo waitogh árfefaf, “Árké yaman eméinde ár túmén yaufiot, wénde tékrát yém yéné ároe emo eméinde.
english: He said to the disciples , “ It is impossible that no occasions of stumbling should come , but woe to him through whom they come !

nama: Yéné ároefaf ndimbal wím so yumbetat kéman a yitat arakafot, yéné mé té yém némbne kérté. Wénde emo taméinde némbne ambumtértér yaufiot, fene kérté so yém ndimbal.
english: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck , and he were thrown into the sea , rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble .

nama: Féyo kor kafandangi fémyo. “Njam fenam yaufi yafrota, yárna, a fá njam kawengongé, tawalinda.
english: Be careful . If your brother sins against you , rebuke him . If he repents , forgive him .

nama: Njam yémo yaufi yafrota fefaf 7 mbinotmé ámbiro efogh kénjún, a 7 mbinotmé nénangota wéikor fefaf a korayangé, 'Yénd té nawengon,' fémon tawalinda.”
english: If he sins against you seven times in the day , and seven times returns , saying , ‘ I repent ,’ you shall forgive him .”

nama: Yesuene muyafé ár norayayénd Yurafaf, “Yuré, ténmamina téfene ndimbal mwighéǃ”
english: The apostles said to the Lord , “ Increase our faith .”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Njam fene ndimbal mwighé yém ndenanit némbne mbwe, fém féyotaro so koraya yéné ndimbal wénfaf, 'Karufang a kayang arakafan,' a so kényárángé fém.
english: The Lord said , “ If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed , you would tell this sycamore tree , ‘ Be uprooted and be planted in the sea ,’ and it would obey you .

nama: “Njam fene faiofnar ásáfogh ár nénangota tarégh tambén o sif wanjengégh tambén, ndimbal ár so korayangé, 'Kénaf kámnjo njamke nénet'?
english: But who is there among you , having a servant plowing or keeping sheep , that will say when he comes in from the field, ‘ Come immediately and sit down at the table’?

nama: Wénde fá so korayangé, 'Tane njamke kwanegh, kwáfrenda a kwanjeta njam enetan. Yéné soramé fémo so enete.'
english: Wouldn’t he rather tell him , ‘ Prepare my supper , clothe yourself properly, and serve me while I eat and drink . Afterward you shall eat and drink ’?

nama: Kánjo yaramai faiofnar ásáfogh ároe ménamén yáne áyáréghmén némat yémon yémndai wafroghét?
english: Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded ? I think not .

nama: Ndenéyameyo fémofem njam ewafrotati méinyotio némat té emndai wafroghét, norayayénd, 'Yénd siéném faiofnar ásáfogh árro yéném. Yéndfem tanwafrotam téfenjo ásáfoghro.'” 10 ár yaufi ghéngan enjnefé
english: Even so you also , when you have done all the things that are commanded you , say , ‘ We are unworthy servants . We have done our duty.’”

nama: Yesu álighan témorwén Jerusalemét, tiotau Samaria a Galili yu néndkéfan.
english: As he was on his way to Jerusalem , he was passing along the borders of Samaria and Galilee .

nama: Fá njam kufanau méngotuot, 10 ár yaufi ghéngan enjnefé tamorwén yéfángoyénd fá. Fá nénokayoyénd nayu
english: As he entered into a certain village , ten men who were lepers met him , who stood at a distance .

nama: a nutnayénd minde tukén, “Yesu, Yuré, ghérghérsisé téfeyotǃ”
english: They lifted up their voices , saying , “ Jesus , Master , have mercy on us !”

nama: Yesum ewinjoi fá a norayai, “Nguanongi, ngauyafongi fémyo ghéngén wárogh árfefaf.” A njamandio néngwanoyénd, fá mer néngamndayénd.
english: When he saw them , he said to them , “ Go and show yourselves to the priests .” As they went , they were cleansed .

nama: Ámbiroam, njam yinjoi frengégham yanai, nénangoi, Ngánján tuwetawét minde tukén.
english: One of them , when he saw that he was healed , turned back , glorifying God with a loud voice .

nama: Fá budme niyai Yesuene kafkaffaf, kánjo yaramai yáyot. Fá témorwén Samariamén ár.
english: He fell on his face at Jesus ’ feet , giving him thanks ; and he was a Samaritan .

nama: Yesum yémotai, “Némé 10 ár méinyotio frengéghkaf em? Ánde ámb 9 ár em?
english: Jesus answered , “Weren’ t the ten cleansed ? But where are the nine ?

nama: Némamén yéné Téndo árro nénangoi Ngánján frengéghét?”
english: Were there none found who returned to give glory to God , except this foreigner?”

nama: Féyo Yesum yémndai fá, “Kokai a nguwano. Fenjo ndimbal mwighayam mer té niyáto.” Ngánjánene limánégh
english: Then he said to him , “ Get up , and go your way . Your faith has healed you .”

nama: Ámb efghon Farasi árém yémotai Yesu, “Njam so Ngánjánene limánégh yéném?” Yesum ewawangoi, “Ngánjánene limánégh yau yéném yéné ende fémon so tinjo fenjo ghékfe.
english: Being asked by the Pharisees when God ’s Kingdom would come , he answered them , “ God ’s Kingdom doesn’ t come with observation ;

nama: Fém yau so norayati, 'Siyém' o 'Kété yém' ménamén Ngánjánene limánégh siyém féfene kénjún yém.”
english: neither will they say , ‘ Look , here !’ or , ‘ Look , there !’ for behold , God ’s Kingdom is within you .”

nama: Féyo Yesu norayai yánjo waitogh árfefaf, “Efogh yéna yéném fém njam so ménde em winjoghét ámb efghon Ároene Ambumoenemén, wénde fémofem yau so tinjongi.
english: He said to the disciples , “ The days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man , and you will not see it .

nama: Árém so tawaufrongi féfe, 'Kété fá yém,' o 'Yéna fá yém.' Yau néwaitotati yéfe soramé yéfefaf.
english: They will tell you , ‘ Look , here !’ or ‘ Look , there !’ Don’t go away or follow after them ,

nama: Ménamén Ároene Ambum njam nénangota, méinyotio árém so yéfandat ndenanit toyé kwayaghanit so télálángé witmé njafar kwéndkwéndta kétán witmé njafar kwéndkwénd.
english: for as the lightning, when it flashes out of one part under the sky , shines to another part under the sky , so will the Son of Man be in his day .

nama: Wénde fronde yémon so tárfár tékrát efande a so yékenat yéné saté ár mérnam.
english: But first , he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation .

nama: “Ndenaye fété Noane efghon kés tamorwén, ndenéyameyo so yém Ároene Ambumoene efghon.
english: As it was in the days of Noah , even so it will also be in the days of the Son of Man .

nama: Árém njamke a nu tánetawét, amaf ár kaweretawét kétán efogh nawerai Noa nélawai minde ndimbal ngárndot. Féyo táf kéfér nakmai a yérambaryéng méinyotio ár.
english: They ate , they drank , they married , and they were given in marriage until the day that Noah entered into the ship, and the flood came and destroyed them all .

nama: “Ndenéyameyo tamorwén Lotene efghon. Árém njamke a nu tánetawét, kéufnamotawét rokar, njamke yéghan a méngo raméghan.
english: Likewise , even as it was in the days of Lot : they ate , they drank , they bought, they sold , they planted , they built ;

nama: Wénde yéné efghon Lotém yéfrangoi Sodom, fés a fésafé wím kénuitawét njafarta nunit kafamndau, a yérambaryéng méinyotio ár.
english: but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom , it rained fire and sulfur from the sky and destroyed them all .

nama: “Ndenéyameyo Ároene Ambumoene efghon so nufrote.
english: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

nama: Yéné efghon efe méngo téndon em yau so kényawangi méngo kénjút rokar résaghét. Ndenéyameyo, efe ndawan em yau so néngangotat rokar résaghét.
english: In that day , he who will be on the housetop and his goods in the house , let him not go down to take them away. Let him who is in the field likewise not turn back .

nama: Yafarotati Lotene amafǃ
english: Remember Lot ’s wife !

nama: Emo njam yéta yánjo ghérsé yambeta so yétarne, emo njam yétarna yánjo ghérsé so kawanjarngé.
english: Whoever seeks to save his life loses it , but whoever loses his life preserves it .

nama: Yéndon némaufrotan fe, yéné sémbáran sómbio ár so ekmare ámbiro kémégh sifayan, ámb Ngánjánam so téngwanangé ámb so téfrangongé.
english: I tell you , in that night there will be two people in one bed . One will be taken and the other will be left.

nama: Sómbio amaf so néusénanét njamke mérne, ámb so yémon téngwanangé, ámb so téfrangongé.”
english: There will be two grinding grain together . One will be taken and the other will be left.”

nama: Yémofem yémotayénd, “Ándan, Yuré?” Yesum ewawangoi, “Ánde kér fú yém, ndikáké kété so náumendat.”
english: They , answering, asked him , “ Where , Lord ?” He said to them , “ Where the body is , there the vultures will also be gathered together .”

nama: Yesum tawaufrotau yánjo waitogh árét a rengerenge si ewauyafoi yéfe fá so túmén Ngánjánafé norayat a yau so kékmbongi.
english: He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray and not give up ,

nama: Yesu norayai, “Ámb tesenan kété témorwén mwighé farorégh ár efe Ngánján férférofnar témorwén utmanéghofnar árfefaf.
english: saying , “ There was a judge in a certain city who didn’ t fear God and didn’ t respect man .

nama: Kété témorwén ámb kwiré yéné tesen kénjún efe katérnau túmén yáfaf siafé, 'Ta kwaram mbarkánd yéné si éwighmén mék téfnárafé siyénmare.'
english: A widow was in that city , and she often came to him , saying , ‘Defend me from my adversary!’

nama: “Nák sénko árom yékenai ámb efghot, wénde ambinomén norayai yánjomén, 'Yénd férférofnar wém Ngánjánefaf a utmanéghofnar árfefaf,
english: He wouldn’ t for a while ; but afterward he said to himself , ‘ Though I neither fear God nor respect man ,

nama: wénde yéné kwiram túmén wawérsote yénd, yéndon so tinjon yáfaf mbarkánd, ménát yau so nénatérne tafaf túménǃ'”
english: yet because this widow bothers me , I will defend her , or else she will wear me out by her continual coming .’”

nama: Féyo Yuré norayai, “Káyárangi kétán némé yéné mbarkándofnar mwighé farorégh ár norayai.
english: The Lord said , “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says .

nama: Ngánjánam so fété enwarse mbarkánd yánjo waferégh árét, efe nendat yáfaf efogh a sémbár. Yémon yau so túmén ekarote fá.
english: Won’ t God avenge his chosen ones who are crying out to him day and night , and yet he exercises patience with them ?

nama: Yéndon némaufrotan fe, yémon so tinjongé nde yémafém mbarkánd yanangi wékeye. Wénde njam Ároene Ambum nénangota, yémon so tawinjongé efe em ndimbal mwighafé yéné mbandan?” Farasi ár a mani kafégh ár
english: I tell you that he will avenge them quickly . Nevertheless , when the Son of Man comes , will he find faith on the earth ?”

nama: Yesum tawaufrotau yéné rengerenge si ámb ároefaf efe tawewonawét ndené kemnawét ndené yénd yéném mbarkánd ár a fá yau:
english: He also spoke this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others :

nama: “Sómbio ár nuwaneyayénd kétán wáwefnogh méngot Ngánjánafé oraighét, ámb témorwén Farasi ár a ámb témorwén mani kafégh ár.
english: “ Two men went up into the temple to pray ; one was a Pharisee , and the other was a tax collector .

nama: Farasi ár nokayoi Ngánjánafé koraiwét, 'Ngánján, kánjo fe ndené yénd yau wém ndené ámb ár kés em, oro téfnár, yaufi wafrogh ár, amaf wanégh ár, o ndenanit yéné mani kafégh ár kés em.
english: The Pharisee stood and prayed by himself like this : ‘ God , I thank you that I am not like the rest of men : extortionists, unrighteous, adulterers , or even like this tax collector .

nama: Yénd nawafarmetan sómbio efogh ámbiro Safatan a yéndon túmén ramégh yam so yafrotam Mosesene nákam noraye Ngánjáneyot.'
english: I fast twice a week . I give tithes of all that I get .’

nama: “Wénde mani kafégh ár takayongérwén nayu. Fá yau néwewoi ndenáyo njafarot, wénde yánjo kafan nasmai a norayai, 'Ngánján, ghérghérsisé tayot, yénd yaufi ár wém.'
english: But the tax collector , standing far away , wouldn’ t even lift up his eyes to heaven , but beat his chest , saying , ‘ God , be merciful to me , a sinner!’

nama: “Yéndon némndetan fém, yéné yaufi ár, yau ndené Farasi ár, fá kangotau méngot áuwéghafé Ngánjánene sáfrére. Fém njam nawarmbota ámb árfefaf, fém so kémeworngi, a fém njam yuwan ném, fém so ném tukén.” Ambumtértér a Yesu
english: I tell you , this man went down to his house justified rather than the other ; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled , but he who humbles himself will be exalted .”

nama: Árém tanérsawét ambumtértér Yesuefaf ménát frengéghét. Njam waitogh árém yinjoyénd yéné, yémofem ekmboyénd yéfefaf.
english: They were also bringing their babies to him , that he might touch them . But when the disciples saw it , they rebuked them .

nama: Wénde Yesum emotai ambumtértér yáfaftio norayai, “Tanumyongi ambumtértér kénaflangi tafaf, yau ekmbotati ambumtértér ménamén Ngánjánene limánégh siyém ndené árét ambumtértér kés em.
english: Jesus summoned them , saying , “Allow the little children to come to me , and don’ t hinder them , for God ’s Kingdom belongs to such as these .

nama: Yéndon efalo si ewaufrotan, ámb árom emo yau tanangé Ngánján limánégh ndenanit ambumtértér sem yau so kényawangi.” Wemár ár
english: Most certainly , I tell you , whoever doesn’ t receive God ’s Kingdom like a little child , he will in no way enter into it .”

nama: Ámb Ju sénko árom yémotai fá, “Mer watamegh ár, némé yéndon so yafrotan tuot ghérsé résaghét?”
english: A certain ruler asked him , saying , “ Good Teacher , what shall I do to inherit eternal life ?”

nama: Yesum yawangoi, “Némamén wémndete yénd mer? Efe yau mer yém fá Ngánjánro.
english: Jesus asked him , “ Why do you call me good ? No one is good , except one : God .

nama: Fém mat ném náknák, 'Amafanyo wanégh yau, ár ngi yau, oro yau, unyégh yau, eutmanwé afé a amé.'”
english: You know the commandments : ‘Don’ t commit adultery ,’ ‘Don’ t murder ,’ ‘Don’ t steal ,’ ‘Don’ t give false testimony ,’ ‘ Honor your father and your mother .’”

nama: Yémo yémndai, “Kétándmayo yénd tosé kwémorwén, yéndon yéné méinyotio nák tawarawénawén.”
english: He said , “ I have observed all these things from my youth up .”

nama: Yesum njam náyárai yéné, fá norayai yáfaf, “Fefaf mé náláite ámb yam wafroghét. Etérna maniot fene némé em méinyotio, ewarameta yéfeyot yauyau árét, a fém so ném wemár njafaran. Féyo kénaf, kwénmaito yénd.”
english: When Jesus heard these things , he said to him , “ You still lack one thing . Sell all that you have and distribute it to the poor . Then you will have treasure in heaven ; then come , follow me .”

nama: Yémon njam náráyai yéné si, fá nawinjoi ghérghérsisafé ménamén fá témorwén minde wemár ár.
english: But when he heard these things , he became very sad , for he was very rich .

nama: Yesum yéfandai a yémndai, “Nderé táikér yém ndené wemár árét Ngánjánene limánégh élaughétǃ
english: Jesus , seeing that he became very sad, said , “ How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God ’ s Kingdom!

nama: Taikre kemole élawégh nidul fénemé siyém ndimbal, wénde taikre wemár ároe élaughét Ngánjánene limánéghét yéné yam yáinde.”
english: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God ’s Kingdom.”

nama: Fá efe náyárayénd yéné ámoghan, “Efe so wét kunjarngé?”
english: Those who heard it said , “ Then who can be saved ?”

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, “Némé táikér yém árét Ngánjáneyot siyém áfrat.”
english: But he said , “ The things which are impossible with men are possible with God .”

nama: Fité norayai yáfaf, “Yéndfem enfrangongém méinyotio téfene némé tam fém némaitongémǃ”
english: Peter said , “ Look , we have left everything and followed you .”

nama: Yesu norayai yéfefaf, “Yéndon ewaufrotan féfe efaloe, yéndon mat eyátotan méinyotio ár emo efrango méngo, amaf, yanaf, yárefaf, ambumtértér Ngánjánene limánégh téfnan
english: He said to them , “ Most certainly I tell you , there is no one who has left house , or wife , or brothers, or parents , or children , for God ’s Kingdom’s sake ,

nama: so kor ewinjotati tárfár mbinotmé sénonjo yéné ghérayan, a so akegh ghérsé tinjongi Ngánjáneta soramé yuwon.” Yesu wéikor korayau yánjo kérmén
english: who will not receive many times more in this time , and in the world to come , eternal life .”

nama: Yesum ewasroi 12 waitogh ár téndot a emndai fá, “Yénd kuwanongém kétán Jerusalemét, a méinyotio yéné yéfarotaménd Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém Ároene Ambumoenemén so nufrote.
english: He took the twelve aside and said to them , “ Behold , we are going up to Jerusalem , and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed .

nama: Fá so téramangi Téndo árfefaf, a yémofem so si yawafrotat, yasalitat fá, mbérme yéfarndat.
english: For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles , will be mocked , treated shamefully, and spit on .

nama: Yémofem so yéfmotat a yésmetat krot. Nambio efogh soramé fá wéikor so kanongé.”
english: They will scourge and kill him . On the third day , he will rise again .”

nama: Wénde waitogh ár yau mat náyátoyénd yénémamén. Yénayoene fifi tétnongérwén kétándmé yéfe tambne, a fá yau mat náyátoyénd némémén fá korayau. Yesum mer yiyátoi ghékéf kwéfkwéf ómnengégh ár
english: They understood none of these things . This saying was hidden from them , and they didn’ t understand the things that were said .

nama: Njamandio Yesum tánetau Jeriko, ghékéf kwéfkwéf ár témorangérwén end téndon kómnendau.
english: As he came near Jericho , a certain blind man sat by the road , begging .

nama: Njam yémon eyárai ár mérén álighan kawaindawét, fá namotai némé yam nufrote.
english: Hearing a multitude going by , he asked what this meant .

nama: Yémofem yaufroyénd yá, “Yesu Najaretmén siyém nawainde.”
english: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by .

nama: Fá nutnayéng, “Yesu, Deifidene ambum, so ghérghérsisé ném tafafǃ”
english: He cried out , “ Jesus , you son of David , have mercy on me !”

nama: Fá efe tangmorwén endmé yarnayénd fá a yémndayénd, “Métar kékmboǃ” Wénde fá yéméindayénd minde keyot, “Deifidene ambum, so ghérghérsisé ném tafafǃ”
english: Those who led the way rebuked him , that he should be quiet ; but he cried out all the more , “ You son of David , have mercy on me !”

nama: Yesu nékmboi a tamndangé ár yáfaf ménjaghét. Njam nénaflai kaka, Yesum témotangé fá,
english: Standing still , Jesus commanded him to be brought to him . When he had come near , he asked him ,

nama: “Némé wafroghét ménde ném tafaf?” Yémon yawangoi, “Yuré, yénd ménde wém éreghét.”
english: “ What do you want me to do ?” He said , “ Lord , that I may see again.”

nama: A Yesum yémndai fá, “Féyotaro so kéretaǃ Fenjo ndimbal mwighayam némanjar fém.”
english: Jesus said to him , “ Receive your sight . Your faith has healed you .”

nama: Táf fété fá ghékéf néretai a yémon yáitoi Yesu, tuwetau Ngánján. Njam méinyotio árém yinjoyénd, yémofem wéi ndenáyo Ngánján tuwetawét.
english: Immediately he received his sight and followed him , glorifying God . All the people , when they saw it , praised God .

nama: Yesu téngmorwén Jerikomé.
english: He entered and was passing through Jericho .

nama: Ár yétkwén kété témorwén Jekies, fá témorwén mani kafégh sénko ár efe témorwén wemár.
english: There was a man named Zacchaeus . He was a chief tax collector, and he was rich .

nama: Fá ménde témorwén Yesue fandaghét, wénde ndémbndémb ármén yau yinyoyéng ménamén ár mérén kénjún takayongérwén.
english: He was trying to see who Jesus was , and couldn’ t because of the crowd , because he was short .

nama: Féyo fá frondeyo nengoi a narmboi ndimbal wénan ndenanit wanj wén Yesue winjoghét, táf Yesu mé téngmorwén yéné endmé.
english: He ran on ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him , for he was going to pass that way .

nama: Njam Yesu nufaryéng yéné sifayan, Yesu néwewoi tukét a yémndai fá, “Jekies, kénewor wékeye yuwot. Fene méngon sénonjo so wémor.”
english: When Jesus came to the place , he looked up and saw him , and said to him , “ Zacchaeus , hurry and come down , for today I must stay at your house .”

nama: Féyo fá néneworyéng wékeye a Yesu áuwéghafé yumyoi yánjo méngot.
english: He hurried , came down , and received him joyfully .

nama: Méinyotio árém yinjoyénd yéné yam a mikemike nufngoyénd, “Fá néngwanoi wan téfnár náyátoi yaufi ároefaf.”
english: When they saw it , they all murmured , saying , “ He has gone in to lodge with a man who is a sinner.”

nama: Wénde Jekies nokayoi a norayai Yurafaf, “Kéreta, Yuréǃ Sénonjo yénandmé yéndon efalotan néndkéfan tane rokar yauyau árét, a yénd njam té kuindan árfefaf rokarmén, so ewangotan yéfene fai fo mbinotma.”
english: Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord , “ Behold , Lord , half of my goods I give to the poor . If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone , I restore four times as much .”

nama: Yesu norayai yáfaf, “Sénonjo wanjarégh yém yéné méngot, ménamén yéné ár siyém Ebraamene ákimén.
english: Jesus said to him , “ Today , salvation has come to this house , because he also is a son of Abraham.

nama: Ménamén Ároene Ambum yénmormén ronjaghét olingégh ár wanjarghét.” Rengerenge si ten faiofnar ásáfogh árfenemén
english: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.”

nama: Njam fá káyáretawét yénémamén, yémon tawawaitotau rengerenge si ménamén fá témorwén Jerusalem kaka a ár kemnawét ndenénde Ngánjánene limánégh kar sélngwéle so kufarngai.
english: As they heard these things , he went on and told a parable , because he was near Jerusalem , and they supposed that God ’s Kingdom would be revealed immediately .

nama: Fá norayai, “Ámb sénko ár nuwanoi nayu yuwot ménát fáyo náyátoi warsagh ár a so wéi nénangote.
english: He said therefore , “ A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom and to return .

nama: Féyo yémon emotai ten faiofnar ásáfogh ár a yéfeyot ewarametam ámb rérero ndimbal mani. Fá norayai, 'Takmangi yéné mani ásáfoghét kétánotio yénd nénangotan.'
english: He called ten servants of his and gave them ten mina coins, and told them , ‘Conduct business until I come .’

nama: “Wénde yánjo árém méndméndat tafandawét a ewáfáretam fénaté ásáfogh mérén soramé fá norayayénd, 'Yénd méndefnar yéném yéné ár sái kamndangé téfene warsagh ár.'
english: But his citizens hated him , and sent an envoy after him , saying , ‘ We don’ t want this man to reign over us .’

nama: “Fá náyátoi warsagh ár féyo fá nénangotam méngotuot. Féyo yémon ewáfáretam faiofnar ásáfogh ár kétán endefet mani ewarametam ménát matot njénamb mani ewafrotaménd.
english: “ When he had come back again , having received the kingdom, he commanded these servants , to whom he had given the money, to be called to him , that he might know what they had gained by conducting business .

nama: “Fronde ámbiro ár yénmormén a norayai, 'Yuré, fene maniam té efuna ámb ten.'
english: The first came before him , saying , ‘ Lord , your mina has made ten more minas .’

nama: “Yáne yuram yawangoi, 'Mer yém. Tane mer faiofnar ásáfogh árǃ Ménamén fém té efalo náyáto némbne mani funaghan, fémon so ewanjete ten ndimbal tesen.'
english: “ He said to him , ‘ Well done , you good servant ! Because you were found faithful with very little , you shall have authority over ten cities .’

nama: “Sómbiomén yénmormén a norayai, 'Yuré, fene maniam té efuna ámb 5.'
english: “ The second came , saying , ‘ Your mina , Lord , has made five minas .’

nama: “Yáne yuram yawangoi, 'Fémon so ewanjete 5 ndimbal tesen.'
english: “ So he said to him , ‘ And you are to be over five cities .’

nama: “Nambiomén faiofnar ásáfogh ár yénmormén a norayai, 'Yuré, fene mani siyém. Yéndon fene mani tamndan a etnoyén sáláme sutafé.
english: Another came , saying , ‘ Lord , behold , your mina , which I kept laid away in a handkerchief ,

nama: Yénd fenemén férfér kwémorwén ménamén fém ném táikér ár. Fémon totér némé yau yélawai a yétane némé yau yiyáyéng.'
english: for I feared you , because you are an exacting man . You take up that which you didn’t lay down , and reap that which you didn’t sow .’

nama: “Yánjo yuram yawangoi, 'Yéndon so kémawitérmbon fe fenjo sieyo, fém yaufi faiofnar ásáfogh árǃ Fém mat ném ndené yénd wém táikér ár a yéndon ewangotan árkéméné yau em tane a etanan yéné árkéméné yéndon yau eitamén.
english: “ He said to him , ‘ Out of your own mouth I will judge you , you wicked servant ! You knew that I am an exacting man , taking up that which I didn’ t lay down and reaping that which I didn’ t sow .

nama: Némamén wét tane mani yau yélawai mani funagh sifayan ménát yénd njam nénangotan, yéndon so tinjongén minaghkaf?'
english: Then why didn’ t you deposit my money in the bank , and at my coming , I might have earned interest on it ?’

nama: “Féyo yémon fá emndai efe tawakayongérwén kaka, 'Takawangi yáne mani yá tambén a taramangi yá efe ten maniafé yém.'
english: He said to those who stood by , ‘ Take the mina away from him and give it to him who has the ten minas .’

nama: “Fá norayayénd, 'Yuré, té náyátongi ten mani yáneǃ'
english: “ They said to him , ‘ Lord , he has ten minas !’

nama: “Yémon tawangongé, 'Yéndon némndetan ndenáye méinyotio endene tárfár em, yá so taramangi tárfár, wénde endene yau em, so takawangi.
english: ‘ For I tell you that to everyone who has , will more be given ; but from him who doesn’ t have , even that which he has will be taken away from him .

nama: Wénde tane mék téfnár efe em méndefnar fá em yénd sái wém yéfene warsagh ár, tanwasrongi a tásmetangi tane sáfrére.'” Yesu warsagh ár nélawete Jerusalemét
english: But bring those enemies of mine who didn’ t want me to reign over them here , and kill them before me .’”

nama: Yéné si soramé Yesu téngmorwén frondeyo Jerusalemét.
english: Having said these things , he went on ahead , going up to Jerusalem .

nama: Njam kaka náyátoi Betefes a Betani mband tunakéf yu yétkwén olifén, yémon ewáfáretangeyai yánjo sómbio waitogh ár, emndeyai fá,
english: When he came near to Bethsphage and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples ,

nama: “Kuwanend méngotuot fronde, njam kélawend, so tinjend ndonki rényéghkaf, efe yau yéfayongérmén. Ténferend a yénémnjét yénan.
english: saying , “ Go your way into the village on the other side, in which , as you enter , you will find a colt tied , which no man has ever sat upon . Untie it and bring it .

nama: Ámb árom njam tamotangé, 'Némat fémofem yéferét?' taufrend yá, 'Yuré ménde yém.'”
english: If anyone asks you , ‘ Why are you untying it ?’ say to him : ‘ The Lord needs it .’”

nama: Yéné efe ewafareméng nuwaneyayénd a yinjeyayénd ndernáye té engémndeyai fá.
english: Those who were sent went away and found things just as he had told them .

nama: Njamandio yémofem téferwét ndonki, yéné yuram emotangeyai fá, “Némamén fémofem yéferét ndonki?”
english: As they were untying the colt , its owners said to them , “ Why are you untying the colt ?”

nama: Yémofem yawangeyayénd, “Yurane ménde yém.”
english: They said , “ The Lord needs it .”

nama: Yémofem yénémnjeménd Yesuefaf, yéfenjo sáláme efayeyayénd ndonkiyan a Yesu nafayoi yéné tukén.
english: Then they brought it to Jesus . They threw their cloaks on the colt and sat Jesus on them .

nama: Njamandio Yesu téngmorwén, árém yéfenjo sáláme tangéfyangotawét endmé.
english: As he went , they spread their cloaks on the road .

nama: Fá njam téngmorwén kaka néngamndai yéné sifayan ánde end ngewonau Olif Mband tunakfot, méinyotio Yesuene waitogh ár nufngoyénd wagh áuwéghafé Ngánjánefaf marat kéme, téfén ménamén sélngwélafé ásáfogh téfandawét Yesuefaf:
english: As he was now getting near , at the descent of the Mount of Olives , the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen ,

nama: “Frengégh ném warsagh ár efe néném Yurane yétkwénanǃ Krufrat njafar tukén a ndimbal kwayaghé minde tukén!”
english: saying , “ Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord ! Peace in heaven , and glory in the highest !”

nama: Ámb Farasi ár kénjún norayayénd Yesuefaf, “Watamegh ár, táwárna fene waitogh árǃ”
english: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him , “ Teacher , rebuke your disciples !”

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, “Yéndon emndetan fém, njam fá métar ekmbo, wím so nutnatǃ”
english: He answered them , “ I tell you that if these were silent , the stones would cry out .”

nama: Njam Yesu kaka ufarghétro témorwén Jerusalemét, yéngwinjoi yéné tesen, Yesu táf ye nufngoi yénémamén
english: When he came near , he saw the city and wept over it ,

nama: a norayai, “Jerusalemén ár, yénd ménde wém fém mat kamndangi sénonjo némé yam krufrát yénérse féfefaf, wénde yau mat em. Ménamén ténoghe yém kétándmé féfene ghékéfta.
english: saying , “ If you , even you , had known today the things which belong to your peace ! But now , they are hidden from your eyes .

nama: Efogh yéna eném féfeyot njam féfene mék téfnáram so tawarendangi fém a tawanawangi féfe méinyotio téndomé.
english: For the days will come on you when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you , surround you , hem you in on every side ,

nama: Yémofem so tawalindangi fém kétán mbandot, fém a ambumtértér fene sifayan. Yémofem ámb yau so taurongi wím kwéndkwéndan yaufifi, ménamén fémofem yau yáyamngongi Ngánjánene wanjarégh féfeyot.” Yesu wáwefnogh méngon
english: and will dash you and your children within you to the ground . They will not leave in you one stone on another , because you didn’ t know the time of your visitation .”

nama: Féyo Yesu nélawai wáwefnogh méngo sifayan a wifarégh ewafngoi emofem rokar téwáfnamotawét.
english: He entered into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in it ,

nama: Fá norayai yéfefaf, “Buk sian yékai, 'Tane méngo so yém Ngánjánafé orayégh méngo,' wénde fémofem yiyátongi oro téfnárafene átnogh yékw.”
english: saying to them , “ It is written , ‘ My house is a house of prayer ,’ but you have made it a ‘ den of robbers ’!”

nama: Méinyotio efghon fá témorwén watameghan wáwefnogh méngon. Wénde limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár, nák watamegh ár a limánégh ár ménde tamorwén Yesue ngiot.
english: He was teaching daily in the temple , but the chief priests, the scribes , and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him .

nama: Wénde yémofem yau end ewinjoyénd yá ngiot, ménamén méinyotio árfene mwighé tarnyangérwén yáne sifaf.
english: They couldn’ t find what they might do , for all the people hung on to every word that he said .

nama: Ámb efghon yémon njam tawatametau ár wáwefnogh méngo sifayan a táitotau mer si, limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a nák watamegh ár nafghayénd yémafe ámb sénko árfé, enmormén yáfaf.
english: On one of those days , as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News , the priests and scribes came to him with the elders .

nama: Yémofem norayayénd, “Ténmaufro némé muyaye fémon ewafrote yéné ásáfogh. Emon fe yéné muyé némaramai?”
english: They asked him , “ Tell us : by what authority do you do these things ? Or who is giving you this authority ?”

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, “Yéndon so ndenéyameyo fém témotangén ámogh si. Ta kwaufrongi,
english: He answered them , “ I also will ask you one question . Tell me :

nama: 'Jonene walitogh yénmormén njafarta o árta?'”
english: the baptism of John , was it from heaven , or from men ?”

nama: Yémofem táruindawét yéfe kénjúnro a korayawét nde, “Njam yénd korayangém 'Kétándmé njafarta,' fá so kamotangé, 'Némamén yau wét fá efalo yiyátongi?'
english: They reasoned with themselves , saying , “ If we say , ‘ From heaven ,’ he will say , ‘ Why didn’ t you believe him ?’

nama: Wénde njam yénd korayangém, 'Kétándmé árta,' méinyotio árém so yénamndat yénd wíme ménamén fá em mat nde Jon témorwén Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár.”
english: But if we say , ‘ From men ,’ all the people will stone us , for they are persuaded that John was a prophet .”

nama: Féyo yémofem yawangoyénd, “Yénd mafina yéném fá árkémamén yém.”
english: They answered that they didn’t know where it was from .

nama: Yesu norayai, “Yéndon wét yau so tawaufron féfe némé muyaye yéndon ewafrotan yéné ásáfogh.” Rengerenge si ndau wanjengégh téfnárafenemén
english: Jesus said to them , “ Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things .”

nama: Fá téngmorwén tawawaitotau árét rengerenge si, “Árém yeitam géreif mbwe ménj ndawan, noroi faiofnar ásáfogh árfefaf a nuwanoi teke.
english: He began to tell the people this parable : “ A man planted a vineyard and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time .

nama: Wau yuwon yémon yáfaretam faiofnar ásáfogh ár kétán ndau wanjengégh árfefaf ménát yémofem so téramangi yáyot ámb géreif mbwe ménj mbwe yéné ndaumén, wénde wanjengégh árém tésmetawét fá a yátembayénd firo rokarofnar.
english: At the proper season , he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him and sent him away empty .

nama: Yémon yáfáretam yénamayo faiofnar ásáfogh ár, wénde yéné árém ndenéyameyo tésmetawét, mámé wáiwáiafé yátembayénd firo rokarofnar.
english: He sent yet another servant , and they also beat him and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty .

nama: Yémon yáfáretam nambiomén a tésmetawét férkafé yitaménd fá.
english: He sent yet a third , and they also wounded him and threw him out .

nama: “Géreif mbwe ménj ndau yuré norayai, 'Yéndon némé so yafrotan? Yéndon so yéngwáfáretan tane ambum efe yéndon yuwetan. Efalo yiyátoi yémofem so yutémndat.'
english: The lord of the vineyard said , ‘ What shall I do ? I will send my beloved son . It may be that seeing him , they will respect him .’

nama: “Wénde njam ndau wanjengégh árém yinjoyénd fá, yémofem korayawét yéfenjo kénjúnro, 'Yéné siyém ndau yurane ambum yém. Wét yésmetam krotio fá, a ndau rokar so kénamndangi téfene.'
english: “ But when the farmers saw him , they reasoned among themselves , saying , ‘ This is the heir . Come , let ’s kill him , that the inheritance may be ours .’

nama: Féyo yémofem yitaménd géreif mbwe ménj ndau kénjúta a yesmayénd krotio fá. “Némé so yafrote géreif mbwe ménj ndau yuram yéfefaf?
english: Then they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him . What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them ?

nama: Fá so yéném a engésmete krotio yéné ár mérén a tawaramangé géreif mbwe ménj ndau ámb árét.” Njam árém náyárayénd yénémamen, fá norayayénd, “Yénandmé yaufifi so yémǃ”
english: He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others .” When they heard that , they said , “ May that never be !”

nama: Yesu néngretai yéfefaf mbarkándaye a emotai, “Féyo némé fifi yéné yém árkéméné yéfarotaménd, 'Sérasér árké méngo ramégh téfnáram yékenayénd a namndayéng néndkéf sérasér.'
english: But he looked at them and said , “ Then what is this that is written , ‘ The stone which the builders rejected was made the chief cornerstone ’? + 20:17 Psalms 118:22

nama: Méinyotio ár efe njam nénuitat wíman so karéfkaréf nufrotat, a njam kénuyangé árfefaf fá so tásénangé.”
english: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces , but it will crush whomever it falls on to dust .”

nama: Nák watamegh ár a limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár end táronjawét werghét wékeye, ménamén fá mat náyátoyénd nde fá korayau yéné rengerenge si yéfenemén. Wénde fá férfér tamorwén árfenemén. Kaisare mani kémégh
english: The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour , but they feared the people — for they knew he had spoken this parable against them .

nama: Yémofem ghékéf tafayotawét yáyot, a ewáfáretaménd ufénmanégh téfnár efe mermer kuindawét. Fá yéta kemnawét Yesue werghét ménát ámb si so korayangai, ménát yémofem so tramangayénd gafanafaf.
english: They watched him and sent out spies , who pretended to be righteous , that they might trap him in something he said , so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor .

nama: Ufénmanégh téfnáram yémotayénd fá, “Watamegh ár, yénd mat yéném ndené fém noraye a ewatamete némé mbarkánd yém a fém yau namnyote árfene mwighafaf, wénde ewatamete Ngánjánene ende efaloyero.
english: They asked him , “ Teacher , we know that you say and teach what is right , and aren’ t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God .

nama: Téfeyot mbarkánd yém mani sái ekmetam Kaisare o yau?”
english: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar , or not ?”

nama: Yesum ewawinjoi yéfene unyégh a norayayéng yéfefaf,
english: But he perceived their craftiness , and said to them , “Why do you test me ?

nama: “Kwauyafongi yáne mani. Endene ár sáf a yétkwén ekayong?” Yémofem yawangoyénd, “Kaisarene.”
english: Show me a denarius . Whose image and inscription are on it ?” They answered , “ Caesar ’ s .”

nama: Yesum emndai, “Taramangi Kaisare yáne némé yém, a Ngánjáne taramangi némé yém yáne.”
english: He said to them , “ Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar ’s, and to God the things that are God ’s.”

nama: Fá endofnar namndayénd Yesue werghét némé yaufimén korayau kété árfene sáfrére. Sélngwél emormén yáne uwangoghan, a fá métar namndayénd. Anogh a awerégh
english: They weren’ t able to trap him in his words before the people . They marveled at his answer and were silent .

nama: Ámb Saduki ár enmormén efe norayayénd ndené kété ámb yau anogh yém. Fá enmormén Yesuefaf moghét.
english: Some of the Sadducees came to him , those who deny that there is a resurrection.

nama: Fá norayayénd, “Watamegh ár, Mosesém yéfarotam nák téfeyot ndené njam yánngénoene yané kér yém tatembangé amaf wénde ambumofnar, yánngnom fété so kwiré tékayongé a ambum eunjeta yanai.
english: They asked him , “ Teacher , Moses wrote to us that if a man ’ s brother dies having a wife , and he is childless, his brother should take the wife and raise up children for his brother.

nama: Kété tamorwén sefen ghakér ambum. Fronde sénkom nawerai a kér yémormén ambumofnar.
english: There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife , and died childless.

nama: Yénamayom kwiré nawerai, wénde fá ndenéyameyo kér yémormén, yéfrangoyéng ambumofnar.
english: The second took her as wife, and he died childless.

nama: A nambiomén nawerai kwiré, ndenéyamanro sefen kér emormén, yéfrangoyéng ambumofnar.
english: The third took her , and likewise the seven all left no children , and died .

nama: Soramayo amaf kér yémormén.
english: Afterward the woman also died .

nama: Anogh efghon endene amaf so yém? Ménamén yéné sefenam té naweretaménd.”
english: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be ? For the seven had her as a wife .”

nama: Yesum ewawangoi yéfe, “Árém amaf so fété naweretat yéné mbandan.
english: Jesus said to them , “ The children of this age marry and are given in marriage .

nama: Wénde soramé efghon, fá efe so tawanongé kérta yau so nawerétat.
english: But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage .

nama: A fá yau kér so em. Fá so ndené kamndangi njafar si résagh árnit. Fá so em Ngánjánene ambumtértér ménamén Ngánjánam so tawanongé kérta.
english: For they can ’ t die any more , for they are like the angels and are children of God , being children of the resurrection.

nama: Wénde Mosesém njam yufarotaméng kéghat fés awároghan, yémon yuyafoi ndené kér so nanotat ghérsat. Yémon témotau Yuré 'Ebraamene Ngánján, Aisekene Ngánján a Jeikofene Ngánján.'
english: But that the dead are raised , even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord ‘ The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac , and the God of Jacob .’

nama: Fá yau yém kér árfene Ngánján, wénde ghérsé árfene Ngánján, ménamén fá méinyotio ghérsé em yáfaf.”
english: Now he is not the God of the dead , but of the living , for all are alive to him.”

nama: Ámb nák watamegh ár namnyoyénd, “Mer norayayénd, watamegh árǃ”
english: Some of the scribes answered , “ Teacher , you speak well .”

nama: A árém yau yémotayénd fá ámb ámogh si kor. Endene ambum yém Keriso?
english: They didn’t dare to ask him any more questions .

nama: Yesum emotai fá, “Némamén ndené árém norayat nde Keriso yém Deifidene ambum?
english: He said to them , “ Why do they say that the Christ is David ’s son ?

nama: Deifid fáyo safén nakmai Sams bukan a norayai, 'Yuré noyarai kétán tane Yurafaf: “Kámnjo yénandmé tane tambén ngángata.
english: David himself says in the book of Psalms , ‘ The Lord said to my Lord , “ Sit at my right hand ,

nama: Kétánotio yéndon tákman fene mék téfnár fene kafkaf mbanmbanan.”'
english: until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet .”’

nama: Deifidém tanmotau fá 'Yuré.' Ndernáye fá yáne ambum so yém?”
english: “ David therefore calls him Lord , so how is he his son ?”

nama: Njamandio méinyotio árém tiyáretawét, Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár,
english: In the hearing of all the people , he said to his disciples ,

nama: “Kor nawarawenati nák watamegh árfenemén. Fá ménde em tombé sálámefé arengét a sái eutémndat térarégh sifayan, a yéfene mwighé témorwén minde mer sifayan ámnjoghét áumengégh méngo kénjún a yémofem méndefé tafandawét limánégh ámnjogh sifayé témndan.
english: “ Beware of those scribes who like to walk in long robes , and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues , and the best places at feasts ;

nama: Yémofem unyéghe kwirafene rokar tawarsawét a fá mermer kuindawét tombé sie Ngánjánafé orayégh ár sáfan. Ndené árém tékrát so yérsat minde tukén.”
english: who devour widows ’ houses , and for a pretense make long prayers . These will receive greater condemnation .”

nama: Njamandio Yesu néngretai, yémon ewinjoi wemár árém takmetawét ramégh rokar wáwefnogh méngon.
english: He looked up and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury .

nama: Yémon yinjoi yauyau kwiré sómbio némbne mani mbwe kémghan.
english: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins .

nama: Yémon emndai, “Yéndon efalo ewaufrotan, yéné yauyau kwiram ewaindai fá emofem mani takmetawét.
english: He said , “Truly I tell you , this poor widow put in more than all of them ,

nama: Méinyotio yéné árém takmetawét némbnare tárfár rokarta, wénde kwiram ekmeyai méinyotio yánjo ghérsé ámnjoghmén.” Kerker efoghene ufarogh yam
english: for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance , but she , out of her poverty , put in all that she had to live on .”

nama: Ámb yáne waitogh ár tamorwén sian ménamén ndernáye wáwefnogh méngo yamanetaménd mer wíme a ramégh yam wateghkaf Ngánjáne. Wénde Yesu norayai,
english: As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said ,

nama: “Méinyotio fémo némé yéfande yéna, efogh yéna yéném ánde ámbiro wím yau so kafrangongé kétándmé ámbta, méinyotio so ewite yuwot.”
english: “ As for these things which you see , the days will come in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down .”

nama: Fá namotayénd, “Watamegh ár, njam so yéné yam nufrotat? A némé yamam so tufarotangé ménát yéné yam ufroghétro em?”
english: They asked him , “ Teacher , so when will these things be ? What is the sign that these things are about to happen ?”

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, “Kor fété aem fém warsoghmén. Mwighé sénté ménamén tárfár ár so eném tane yétkwénan, ndené siafé, 'Yénd wém Yesu,' a 'Efogh siyém kaka yém.' Yau ewaitotati fá.
