english: Now in these days , prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.

nama: Ámb ár yétkwén Agabus nokayoyéng Ngánján Mbérmbér kénjúmé norayai ndené minde káukáu so marataye yém Rom yuwon. (Yéné nufrotam táf Klaudiusene limánégh efghon)
english: One of them named Agabus stood up and indicated by the Spirit that there should be a great famine all over the world , which also happened in the days of Claudius .

nama: Waitogh árém mwighé yéfaronaménd wáutárghét yéfe fútarét efe tamorangérwén Judia yuwon.
english: As any of the disciples had plenty, each determined to send relief to the brothers who lived in Judea ;

nama: Yémofem eumendaménd mani a eramayénd kétán Banabas a Sol, emofem engérsaménd kétán sénko árfefaf Judia yuwon.
english: which they also did , sending it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul .

nama: Yéné efghon warsagh ár Erodém ewerai ár Yesuene mérénmén kemnau yéfe ngiot.
english: Now about that time , King Herod stretched out his hands to oppress some of the assembly .

nama: Erodém fiyaré ewafáretai Jone yánngén Jemse ngiot witmafé sénté naifaye krot.
english: He killed James , the brother of John , with the sword .

nama: Njam Erodém Jems kérotio yésmetam káuwétawét Ju ár, féyo néngwanoi Fité werghét wéikor. Yéné kufrotau táf Minaghofnar flawé témndan.
english: When he saw that it pleased the Jews , he proceeded to seize Peter also . This was during the days of unleavened bread.

nama: Werégh soramé, Erodém yénglawai Fité kwéfkwéf méngot, yéngwafnamoi 4 fiyaré mérén kénjúmé ámbiro mérnan 4 fiyaré tamorwén, méinyotio 16 fiyaré. Erod nemnai yá utorghét moghét yéné Waingégh téménd soramé.
english: When he had arrested him , he put him in prison and delivered him to four squads of four soldiers each to guard him , intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover .

nama: Féyo Fité kwéfkwéf méngon télawangérwén, wénde Yesuene mérén ndimbal kéráye oraighan Ngánjánefaf tamorwén yánemén.
english: Peter therefore was kept in the prison , but constant prayer was made by the assembly to God for him .

nama: Yéné sémbáranyo Erodém mwighé yéfaronam yáfaf sái ténémnjawét moghét, Fité tékmangérwén sómbio fiyarafene néndkéfan, sómbio seinafé, ámb ghékéf fayogh ár tawakayarwén sésafneyan.
english: The same night when Herod was about to bring him out , Peter was sleeping between two soldiers , bound with two chains . Guards in front of the door kept the prison .

nama: Sunsune Yurane njafar si résagh ár nufaryéng kwayaghé natremngoi kwéfkwéf méngo kénjún. Yémo yésmai Fité kwasayan a yanoyéng a témndangé “Kano wékeyeǃ” a sein nuyáyénd Fitane ngángata.
english: And behold , an angel of the Lord stood by him , and a light shone in the cell . He struck Peter on the side and woke him up , saying , “ Stand up quickly !” His chains fell off his hands .

nama: Féyo njafar si résagh árom témndangé fá, “Kawo fenjo sáláme a sandol.” A Fitam ewafrotam yéné yam. Njafar si résagh árom yémndai, “Fenjo sálámeye káfyar a kwénmaito.”
english: The angel said to him , “ Get dressed and put on your sandals .” He did so . He said to him , “ Put on your cloak and follow me .”

nama: Fitam yéngwaitoi néngtoreyayénd kwéfkwéf méngota, wénde fá yau mat témorwén némé njafar si résagh árom tafrotau wénde fété kufrotau; fá kéma kemnau nde ghékéf waingégh kar témorwén.
english: And he went out and followed him . He didn’ t know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he saw a vision .

nama: Yémofem engwaindangeyayénd frondemén a yénamayomén ghékéf fayogh ár mérén a nénafleyayénd tot téfén sésafnefaf árké end témorwén ndimbal tesenot. Fáyo nasawéryéng yéfeyot, a néngwaneyayénd endmé. Fá njam tangérwén yéné endmé, sélngwéle njafar si résagh árom yéfrangoi.
english: When they were past the first and the second guard , they came to the iron gate that leads into the city , which opened to them by itself. They went out and went down one street , and immediately the angel departed from him .

nama: Fité mwighat nangoi a norayai, “Sénonjo mat náyáton atambaréghofnar nde Yuram ténmáfárete njafar si résagh ár ta wanjarghét kétándmé Erodene ngángata a kétándmé méinyotio Ju árfene kamnangta.”
english: When Peter had come to himself , he said , “ Now I truly know that the Lord has sent out his angel and delivered me out of the hand of Herod , and from everything the Jewish people were expecting .”

nama: Njam yémon yéné yáyamngoi, fá nuwanoi Meriene méngot Jon Make yátmé, ánde tárfár ár káumendawét Ngánjánafé orayéghét.
english: Thinking about that , he came to the house of Mary , the mother of John who was called Mark , where many were gathered together and were praying .

nama: Fité áutárégh kufngongé sésafneyan, a ásáfogh mérés yétkwén Roda néngaflai sésafnefaf uwangoghét.
english: When Peter knocked at the door of the gate , a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer .

nama: Njam mérsom yáyamngoi Fitane kém, mérés yémormén áuwégham yáindai, fá néngangoi wérseye sésafne sauréghofnaye a néngorayai, “Fité sésafnefaf yémǃ”
english: When she recognized Peter ’s voice , she didn’ t open the gate for joy , but ran in and reported that Peter was standing in front of the gate .

nama: Yémofem témndangi, “Fe mwighayam némauwéinde!” Njam fá mé kafrotau ndené efalo fété yém, fá norayayénd, “Mété táf njafar si résagh ár yém yá wáutárghét.”
english: They said to her , “ You are crazy!” But she insisted that it was so . They said , “ It is his angel.”

nama: Wénde Fité ménjtaye mé káutanau. Njam yémofem sésafne yésawéryénd a yinjoyénd Fité, fá sélngwél emormén.
english: But Peter continued knocking . When they had opened, they saw him and were amazed .

nama: Fitam ngángaye engwaunyai métar a tawawalinjotau ndernáye Yuram yénmutoryéng kwéfkwéf méngota. Fá norayai, “Tawaufrongi Jems a ámb fútarét yénémamén.” A féyo fá táf nuwanoi ámb yuwot.
english: But he , beckoning to them with his hand to be silent , declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison . He said , “ Tell these things to James and to the brothers.” Then he departed and went to another place .

nama: Ámb akwan kété ndimbal atambarégh témorwén fiyarafefaf némé nufrotam Fitafaf.
english: Now as soon as it was day , there was no small stir among the soldiers about what had become of Peter .

nama: Soramé Erodém yéta ronjagh yafngoi wénde yau yinjoi Fité, yémon mogh ghékéf fayogh ár ewafngoi a krot takmetau. Erod kér yémormén Féyo Erod néwanoi kétándmé Judia yuta Kaisaria tesenot féyo kété témorangérwén.
english: When Herod had sought for him and didn’ t find him , he examined the guards , then commanded that they should be put to death. He went down from Judea to Caesarea , and stayed there .

nama: Erod nékw témorwén Taire a Sidon Yumén árfefaf, yénémamén fá nuwanoyénd mérne Erode fandaghét. Fronde yémofem yamnendayénd Blastus, Erodene méngo wanjéngégh sénko ár ménát yéfe wáutárghét. Féyo fá nuwanoyénd Erodefaf a yamnendayénd yau so ménjtaye tékmbotau njamkemén ménamén túmén Taire a Sidon Yu tarsawét njamke warsagh ár Erodene ndimbal yuta.
english: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon . They came with one accord to him and , having made Blastus, the king ’s personal aide, their friend , they asked for peace , because their country depended on the king ’s country for food.

nama: Ufoghmén kwéfon Erod numanetaméng yáne yétkwén sáláme, námnjoi yánjo limánégh ámnjogh sifayan a marataye si térametau árfefaf.
english: On an appointed day , Herod dressed himself in royal clothing , sat on the throne, and gave a speech to them .

nama: Fá nutnayénd, “Yéné siyém ngánjánene si kém, yau ároene.”
english: The people shouted , “ The voice of a god , and not of a man !”

nama: Táfandio, ménamén Erodém áuwégh yau tawangotau Ngánjáne, féyo Yurane njafar si résagh árom yésmai fá, féyo Erod ndéndam néne yafngoyénd a kér yémormén.
english: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him , because he didn’ t give God the glory . Then he was eaten by worms and died .

nama: Wénde Ngánjánene si mulmulafé kanotau a marat káyátotau.
english: But the word of God grew and multiplied .

nama: Njam Banabas a Sol yéfenjo ásáfogh yifeyayénd, fá kénangowét kétándmé Jerusalemta kétán Antiokét yémafé Jon Mak.
english: Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem when they had fulfilled their service , also taking with them John who was called Mark .

nama: Antiokén Yesuene mérén kénjún kété tamorwén Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a watamegh ár. Fá tamorwén Banabas, Saimon kwéfité ár, Luses Sairia tesenmén, Manayen(efe Erodene kaka fútar témorwén), a Sol.
english: Now in the assembly that was at Antioch there were some prophets and teachers : Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger , Lucius of Cyrene , Manaen the foster brother of Herod the tetrarch , and Saul .

nama: Yémofem njam táwefnotawét Yurai awafarmanéghafé, Ngánján Mbérmbér norayai, “Tafalend tayot Banabas a Sol ménát ásáfoghét yéndon árké ménát ewafereyayén.”
english: As they served the Lord and fasted , the Holy Spirit said , “Separate Barnabas and Saul for me , for the work to which I have called them .”

nama: Féyo yéfene awafarmanégh a Ngánjánafé orayégh soramé, yémofem ngángé efayoyénd yéfefaf a nuwaneyayénd. Fol a Banabasfem mer si taitowét Saifrusén
english: Then , when they had fasted and prayed and laid their hands on them , they sent them away.

nama: Fá sómbio nuwaneyayénd yéfenjo endmé Ngánján Mbérmbéran, nuwaneyayénd kétán Selusia tesen a karaiwét kétándmé kétán Saifrus tesenot.
english: So , being sent out by the Holy Spirit , they went down to Seleucia . From there they sailed to Cyprus .

nama: Njam fá nufareyayénd kétán Salamis tesenot, yémofem Ngánjánene si waitogh yafngeyayénd Ju árfene áumengégh méngomé. Jonfé tamorwén yéfene wáutárégh ár. Folene fronde áligh (Acts 13.4—14.28)|src="nmx_195wbt - Paul journey 1 [bwf-light] @en_v3.tif" size="col" copy="© 2025 Wycliffe Bible Translators, Inc." ref="13.4—14.28"
english: When they were at Salamis , they proclaimed God ’ s word in the Jewish synagogues . They also had John as their attendant .

nama: Fá karendawét méinyotio yuwomé kétánotio nénufaryénd Fafos méngotuot. Kété yémafé nafángoyénd Bar-Yesu. Fá témorwén Ju ghar téfnár a unyégh si afrogh ár.
english: When they had gone through the island to Paphos , they found a certain sorcerer , a false prophet , a Jew whose name was Bar Jesus,

nama: Fá yáyot kásáfotau Sargius Faulus, efe témorwén minde matyamafé a témorwén Rom fiyarafene sénko ár yéné yuwon. Sargius Faulus ménde témorwén Ngánjánene si áyárghét a yémon ewáfáretam yéfefaf Banabas a Sol.
english: who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus , a man of understanding. This man summoned Barnabas and Saul , and sought to hear the word of God .

nama: Wénde ghar téfnár Elimas (yéné siyém Bar-Yesuene yétkwén fifi yém) taufotau a yéta tawengotau gafanane ndimbal mwighé.
english: But Elymas the sorcerer ( for so is his name by interpretation ) withstood them , seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith .

nama: Féyo Sol, efe tanmotawét Fol, Ngánján Mbérmbér wafoghkaf, néretai mbarkánde yáfaf Elimas a korayangé,
english: But Saul , who is also called Paul , filled with the Holy Spirit , fastened his eyes on him

nama: “Fém sném Sáiférene ambum a méinyotio mbarkándoene mék téfnárǃ Fém sném méinyotio yaman warsogh ár a unyégh ár. Kékmbo, Yurane mbarkánd yamta yau ewasemna ár unyéghe!
english: and said , “ You son of the devil , full of all deceit and all cunning, you enemy of all righteousness , will you not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord ?

nama: Sénonjo Yurane nékwam so kémanangé. Fém so ném ghékéf kwéfkwéf, a yéné efghétro fémon yau so yéfande efghoene kwayaghé.” Táfandio esam a kwéfkwéfam yafaryéng fá, a fá korsotau yénémamén káronjau árot ménjághét.
english: Now , behold , the hand of the Lord is on you , and you will be blind , not seeing the sun for a season !” Immediately a mist and darkness fell on him . He went around seeking someone to lead him by the hand .

nama: Njam Rom fiyarafene sénko árom yinjoi némé kufrotau, fá sélngwél yémormén Ngánjánene si watameghan, yémon táf efalo yiyátoi Yuré. Fol a Banabasfem mer si taitowét Antiok tesenan Fisidia ndimbal yuwon
english: Then the proconsul, when he saw what was done , believed , being astonished at the teaching of the Lord .

nama: Kétándmé Fafosta, Fol a yáne mérnafé tayotawét ndimbal boute kétán Fargat Famfilia kénjún, wénde Joném efrangoi a kangotau kétán Jerusalemét.
english: Now Paul and his company set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia . John departed from them and returned to Jerusalem .

nama: Kétamé Fargata fá nuwanoyénd Antiok tesenot Fisidia ndimbal yuwon. Safatan fá nélawayénd áumengégh méngot a námnjoyénd.
english: But they , passing on from Perga , came to Antioch of Pisidia . They went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day and sat down .

nama: Mosesene nák a Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfene si wafégh soramé, Ju árfene áumengégh méngo limángégh árém si yéngwatembayénd yéfefaf, “Fútaréf, njam féfefaf wawéngsogh si yém yéné árét, féyotaro korayangi.”
english: After the reading of the law and the prophets , the rulers of the synagogue sent to them , saying , “Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people , speak .”

nama: Fol nokayoyéng, ngángé euyoi a tamndangé, “Israilmén ár a fém Téndo ár emofem Ngánjáne yáwefnotati, kénáyárangi tafafǃ
english: Paul stood up , and gesturing with his hand said , “ Men of Israel , and you who fear God , listen.

nama: Israil árfene Ngánjánam ewaferyéng téfene ákif a marat ár eyátoyéng táf fá stamorangérwén Ijif kénjún, a Ngánjánam yánjo waftaye eutoryéng, elimai fá yéné yuta,
english: The God of this people chose our fathers , and exalted the people when they stayed as aliens in the land of Egypt, and with an uplifted arm , he led them out of it .

nama: fá mé kawamarotau yéfene yaufiyaman kétánotio 40 yunjéf fanfne yuwon,
english: For a period of about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness .

nama: yémon ewasérmbotam sefen ndimbal yu Kananén a mband ewaramai yuré náyátoyénd.
english: When he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan , he gave them their land for an inheritance for about four hundred fifty years.

nama: Méinyotio yéné yamam yénérsam 450 yunjéf. “Yéné soramé, Ngánjánam ewaramai yéfe mogh sénko ár kétánotio Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár Samuelene efogh nawerai.
english: After these things , he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet .

nama: Fá nómnendayénd warsagh árot, a yémon ewaramai Kisene ambum Sol, Benjamin mérénmén, emon elimndam 40 yunjéf.
english: Afterward they asked for a king , and God gave to them Saul the son of Kish , a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years .

nama: Wénde Ngánjánam Sol yitérmboi a yémon yasoi Deifid yéfene warsagh árot. Fá norayai yánemén, 'Yéndon yinjoyén Deifid Jesiene ambum, yéné ár efe tane tikéfmén yém, yémon so ewafrote méinyotio yam tane ménde némat yém.'
english: When he had removed him , he raised up David to be their king , to whom he also testified , ‘ I have found David the son of Jesse , a man after my heart , who will do all my will .’

nama: “Kétándmé yéné ár mérénta Ngánjánam enwarsam Israil árét Ghérsé urtogh ár Yesu, ndené fété fá nénorayai unyéghofnar si.
english: From this man ’s offspring, God has brought salvation to Israel according to his promise ,

nama: Soramé Yesuene ufarégh, Joném fronde si táitotawéng yaufiyam awengoghmén a nu walitoghmén méinyotio Israil árfefaf.
english: before his coming , when John had first preached the baptism of repentance to Israel .

nama: Joném njam yáne ásáfogh tifotau, yémon tamndangé, 'Fém ndernáye nemnati yénd efe wém? Yénd yau wém Keriso. Wénde fá siyém ta soramé yéném, endene sandol ferghét yénd féyotarofnar wém.'
english: As John was fulfilling his course , he said , ‘ What do you suppose that I am ? I am not he . But behold , one comes after me , the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie .’

nama: “Fútaréf, Ebraamene ambumtértér, a fém Ngánján férférafé Téndo ár, téfeyot yéné wanjarégh si ténmáfáretat.
english: “Brothers, children of the stock of Abraham, and those among you who fear God , the word of this salvation is sent out to you .

nama: Jerusalemén ár a yéfene limánégh árém yau yáyámngoyénd Yesu endenemén Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár kénorayawét. Wénde yémofem ngi yafngoyénd Yesu, yéné si efalo káyátotau némé Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém kénorayawét árké taftawét méinyotio Safatan.
english: For those who dwell in Jerusalem , and their rulers, because they didn’ t know him , nor the voices of the prophets which are read every Sabbath , fulfilled them by condemning him .

nama: Yémofem yau yinjoyénd téfén yáne krot, wénde yémofem yamnendayénd Failet yá ngiot.
english: Though they found no cause for death , they still asked Pilate to have him killed.

nama: “Njam yémofem ásáfogh yafrotaménd méinyotio yéné bukan árké tékayarwén yánemén, yémofem yeworyénd wafatarégh wénta a yélawayénd mbéwé méngon.
english: When they had fulfilled all things that were written about him , they took him down from the tree and laid him in a tomb .

nama: Wénde Ngánjánam yanoi fá kétándmé kérta,
english: But God raised him from the dead ,

nama: a tárfár efogh fá nufaryéng yéfefaf emafé tayotau kétándmé Galili yu kétán Jerusalem. Fá fem sénonjo yáne ghékéf fandagh ár Israil árét.
english: and he was seen for many days by those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem , who are his witnesses to the people .

nama: “Yéndfem ewaufrotam féfe mer si, Ngánjánam némé nénorayam unyéghofnar si téfene ákifefaf
english: We bring you good news of the promise made to the fathers ,

nama: Ngánjánam té tawafrote téfeyot, yéfene ákiambumat, ménaye Yesu kés yanoi. Ndené fété kés yékai yénamayo Sams bukan, “'Fém ném tane Ambum, sénonjo yénd nénáyáton fene Afé.'
english: that God has fulfilled this to us , their children , in that he raised up Jesus . As it is also written in the second psalm , ‘ You are my Son . Today I have become your father .’ + 13:33 Psalms 2:7

nama: Ngánjánam fá yanoi kérta, yénémamén yau so nornde, némé noraye yéné sian, “'Yéndon so tawaraman njúnnjúnofnar a efalo frengégh némé té nénorayam Deifidefaf.'
english: “ Concerning that he raised him up from the dead , now no more to return to corruption , he has spoken thus : ‘ I will give you the holy and sure blessings of David .’

nama: Ámb Sams bukan yéné yékai, “'Fémon yau so tumyo fenjo Njúnnjúnofnar Ambum kérkfot.'
english: Therefore he says also in another psalm, ‘ You will not allow your Holy One to see decay .’

nama: “Njam Deifidém Ngánjánene ásáfogh tawafrotau yánjo ár satafaf, fá táf kémégh nakmai, fá esareyayénd yákifé a yáne fifi norndam.
english: For David , after he had in his own generation served the counsel of God , fell asleep , was laid with his fathers , and saw decay .

nama: Wénde ámbiro ár efe Ngánjánam yanoi kérta yau norndam.
english: But he whom God raised up saw no decay .

nama: “Yénémaménnde, tane fútaréf, yénd ménde wém fém mat kamndangi ndené Yesuene kénjúmé wawalingégh yém féfene yaufimén.
english: Be it known to you therefore , brothers, that through this man is proclaimed to you remission of sins;

nama: Emo efalo yiyátota Yesu, Ngánjánam so tanmongé fá siyém mbarkánd ár, yau Mosesene náke so yém.
english: and by him everyone who believes is justified from all things , from which you could not be justified by the law of Moses .

nama: Kor nawarawenati fémǃ Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém árké yammén korayawét yau so féfefaf nufrote:
english: Beware therefore , lest that come on you which is spoken in the prophets :

nama: “'Fém Ngánján karogh ár, sunsunan kér so em, némamén yéndon so ewafrotan ámb yam féfene efghon fémofem efalo yau so eyátotati, ndemamén ámb árom so kémaufrongé feyot.'”
english: ‘ Behold , you scoffers ! Wonder and perish, for I work a work in your days , a work which you will in no way believe , if one declares it to you .’” + 13:41 Habakkuk 1:5

nama: Njamandio Fol a Banabasfem téfrangowét Ju árfene áumengégh méngo, árém eumieyayénd fá kor ámb Safatot siot.
english: So when the Jews went out of the synagogue, the Gentiles begged that these words might be preached to them the next Sabbath .

nama: Njam ár mérén kófarnawét, tárfár Ju ár a Téndo árém ewáiteyayénd Fol a Banabas, efe korayawét yémafé a ewamnendangeyayénd fá ménát mé sái engérnyang Ngánjánene ngárám kénjún.
english: Now when the synagogue broke up , many of the Jews and of the devout proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas; who , speaking to them , urged them to continue in the grace of God .

nama: Ámb Safatan ndenanit témorwén méinyotio tesen kar káumendawét Yurane si áyárghét.
english: The next Sabbath , almost the whole city was gathered together to hear the word of God .

nama: Njam Ju árém ewinjoyénd ár mérén, fá waramnjagh mwighayam ewafoi a si karogh yafngoyénd némé Fol korayau.
english: But when the Jews saw the multitudes , they were filled with jealousy , and contradicted the things which were spoken by Paul , and blasphemed.

nama: Féyo Fol a Banabasfem ewawangeyayénd yéfe férférofnaye, “Yénd so fété noraiwam Ngánjánene si féfefaf Ju árfefaf fronde. Ménamén fémofem yékenati a nawafénmetati fémyo féyotarofnar em tuot ghérsé résaghét, yéndfem fém so tafrangem a kuwanem Téndo árfefaf.
english: Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly , and said , “ It was necessary that God ’s word should be spoken to you first . Since indeed you thrust it from yourselves , and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life , behold , we turn to the Gentiles .

nama: Yénat yénd ténmafarewé Yuram, “'Yéndon fém té eyáteyayén kwayaghé yéfe téndo árét, ménát fémofem so yérset wanjarégh kétánotio mband ánde yiyato.'”
english: For so has the Lord commanded us , saying, ‘ I have set you as a light for the Gentiles , that you should bring salvation to the uttermost parts of the earth .’” + 13:47 Isaiah 49:6

nama: Njam Téndo árém náyárayénd yéné si, fá áuwéghan tamorwén a kánjo tarametawét Yurane simén; a efe méinyotio ewaferyéng tuot ghérsat efalo yiyátoyénd.
english: As the Gentiles heard this , they were glad and glorified the word of God . As many as were appointed to eternal life believed .

nama: Yurane si marat náyátoi méinyotio yuwomé.
english: The Lord ’ s word was spread abroad throughout all the region .

nama: Wénde Ju árém sénanégh ewafngoyénd tesenmén limánégh ár a Ngánján férférafé ndimbal amaf. Yémofem Fol a Banabas ngi ewafngeyayénd a ewitreyayénd yéfene yuta.
english: But the Jews stirred up the devout and prominent women and the chief men of the city , and stirred up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their borders .

nama: Féyo yéné téfnan yémofem kafkaf ngwérméng esenangeyayénd matyamot yéfefaf a nuwaneyayénd Ikonium méngotuot.
english: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them , and came to Iconium .

nama: A waitogh ár áuwégham ewafoi a Ngánján Mbérmbéram.
english: The disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit .

nama: Ikonium yu kénjún Fol a Banabas túmén tangérwén Ju árfene áumengégh méngot. Kété fá koraiwét sotafé Jumén a téndo yumén ndimbal ár mérnafefaf Ngánján efalo yiyátoyénd.
english: In Iconium , they entered together into the synagogue of the Jews , and so spoke that a great multitude both of Jews and of Greeks believed .

nama: Wénde yéné Ju ár efe nakenayénd Ngánjáne efalo yátoghét norayayénd Téndo árfefaf a tawasérmbotawét yéfene mwighé sénanégh ewafngeyayénd Fol a Banabas.
english: But the disbelieving Jews stirred up and embittered the souls of the Gentiles against the brothers.

nama: Féyo Fol a Banabas teke kété tárwén, koraiwét férférofnar Yurai, a Yuramyo ngárámmén si tékmetau safén ndené efalo yém ménáyan tawafrowét ufarogh yam a sélngwélafé yam.
english: Therefore they stayed there a long time , speaking boldly in the Lord , who testified to the word of his grace , granting signs and wonders to be done by their hands .

nama: Tesenmén ár nafaloyénd; ámb Ju árfeta, ámb Yesuene muyafé árfeta.
english: But the multitude of the city was divided . Part sided with the Jews and part with the apostles.

nama: Féyo Téndo ár a Ju ár mérne nafghayénd yéfenjo sénko árfé ménát Fol a Banabasfe ngiot.
english: When some of both the Gentiles and the Jews , with their rulers, made a violent attempt to mistreat and stone them ,

nama: Njam Yesuene muyafé ár mat náyáteyayénd yénémamén, fá nitreyayénd kétán Likonia yuwot Listran a Derbe tesen a ámb kaka yuwomé,
english: they became aware of it and fled to the cities of Lycaonia , Lystra , Derbe , and the surrounding region .

nama: kété wéikor yéngérnyeyayénd mer si waitogh. Fol si táitotau Ju árfene áumengégh méngon (Acts 14.1)|src="CN01961B.tif" size="col" copy="© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission." ref="14.1" Fol a Banabas taitowét mer si Listran a Derbe tesenan
english: There they preached the Good News .

nama: Listra tesenan kété ár témorangérwén aknyégh ár, efe tékmangérwén wawér kétándmayo áráfégh yau tiyotawéng.
english: At Lystra a certain man sat , impotent in his feet , a cripple from his mother ’s womb , who never had walked .

nama: Yémon tiyáretau Fol sian. Fol néngretai mbarkánde yáfaf, a yawinjoi yáne ndimbal mwighé mer yátoghét
english: He was listening to Paul speaking , who , fastening eyes on him and seeing that he had faith to be made whole ,

nama: a témotangé, “Kokai mbarkánde fenjo kafkafeǃ” Táf fété ár natnam tukét a áligh nufngoyéng.
english: said with a loud voice , “Stand upright on your feet !” He leaped up and walked .

nama: Njam ár mérnam yinjoyénd sélngwélafé yam némé Folém yafrotam, fá kutnawét Likonia sie, “Efalo sómbio ngánján té néneworend téfefaf árnitǃ”
english: When the multitude saw what Paul had done , they lifted up their voice , saying in the language of Lycaonia , “ The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men !”

nama: Banabas témndangi Jeus, yáne fifi Grik sie ndimbal ngánján a Fol témndangi Ermes yáne fifi Grik sie si sénko ár.
english: They called Barnabas “ Jupiter ”, and Paul “Mercury”, because he was the chief speaker .

nama: Jeusene wáwefnogh méngo témorwén tesen téndota, ghéngén wárogh ár enwanai kau a mbumbu kétán tesen sésafnefaf ménamén fá a ár mérén ménde tamorwén ghéngén ramghét yéfeyot.
english: The priest of Jupiter , whose temple was in front of their city , brought oxen and garlands to the gates , and would have made a sacrifice along with the multitudes .

nama: Wénde njam Yesuene muyafé ár Banabas a Fol náyáreyayénd yénémamén, yémofem eftráreyayénd yéfenjo sáláme a néngenngeyayénd ár mérnafefaf, kutnawét,
english: But when the apostles, Barnabas and Paul , heard of it , they tore their clothes and sprang into the multitude , crying out ,

nama: “Fútaréf, némamén fémofem yéné yafrotati? Yénd yénre ár, ndené fété fém sem. Yéndfem enwarsuwam féfeyot mer si, ménát awengoghét kétándmé fifiofnar yamta kétán akegh Ngánjánefaf, emo ewafrotam njafar, mband, arakaf a méinyotio rokar yéné kénjún.
english: “ Men , why are you doing these things ? We also are men of the same nature as you , and bring you good news , that you should turn from these vain things to the living God , who made the sky , the earth , the sea , and all that is in them ;

nama: Tándái yémon engéumyoi méinyotio ár yéfenjo méndeye néngwanoyénd,
english: who in the generations gone by allowed all the nations to walk in their own ways .

nama: wénde yémon yau efrangoi ambino wénde yémon mé tawauyafotau meryam ménaye nu raméghe kétándmé njafarta a njamke raméghe yéfenjo wérwéran a yémon mé nouséne yéfeyot tárfár njamke a yéfene tikéf yafote áuwéghét.”
english: Yet he didn’ t leave himself without witness, in that he did good and gave you rains from the sky and fruitful seasons , filling our hearts with food and gladness .”

nama: Ndemamén yéné sie, Fol a Banabas emnyangménd ár mérén yau so ghéngén tarametawét yéfeyot, wénde yau témorwén afrát yéfeyot ménát sái tawanauwét kétamé sái tafrowét.
english: Even saying these things , they hardly stopped the multitudes from making a sacrifice to them .

nama: Féyo ámb Ju ár enmormén kétándmé Antiok a Ikonium tesenta a ár ewasemnaménd Fol a Banabasfé ngiot. Yémofem wíme tafiyátotawét Fol a téroghawét fá tesenta yutoryénd, kéma kemnawét fá kér kar yémormén.
english: But some Jews from Antioch and Iconium came there , and having persuaded the multitudes , they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city , supposing that he was dead .

nama: Wénde yéné soramé waitogh árém yarendayénd Fol, fá nurtotam a néngwanoi tesen kénjút. Ámb efghon Fol a Banabas néwaneyayénd Derbe tesenot. Fol a Banabas kangowét Antiokét Sirian
english: But as the disciples stood around him , he rose up , and entered into the city . On the next day he went out with Barnabas to Derbe .

nama: Yémofem taitowét mer si Derbe tesenan a tárfár ár náyátoyénd waitogh ár. Féyo fá kangowét Listra, Ikonium a Antiokét,
english: When they had preached the Good News to that city and had made many disciples , they returned to Lystra , Iconium , and Antioch,

nama: muyé tawaramwét waitogh árét a tawawéngsowét ménát fá mé sái efalo em ndimbal mwighayan. Yémofem tamndend, “Yénd mé so yénngém tárfár táikér yam kénjúmé ménát Ngánján limánégh élaughét.”
english: strengthening the souls of the disciples , exhorting them to continue in the faith , and that through many afflictions we must enter into God ’s Kingdom.

nama: Fol a Banabasfem ekafeyayénd Yesuene mérén wáutárégh ár yéfeyot fénatayero Yesuene mérnot. Orayéghe a awafarmanéghe, fá ewateménd Yurafaf endefaf yéfene méghmégh témorwén.
english: When they had appointed elders for them in every assembly , and had prayed with fasting , they commended them to the Lord on whom they had believed .

nama: Soramé tangérwén Fisidia yuwomé, fá enérmén kétán Famfilia yuwot,
english: They passed through Pisidia and came to Pamphylia .

nama: a yémofem si waitogh yafngeyayénd Ferga yuwon, yéné soramé fá néngwaneyayénd Atalia tesenot.
english: When they had spoken the word in Perga , they went down to Attalia.

nama: Kétándmé Ataliata fá ndimbal boute kangowét Antiokét. Antiok yéné sifayé témorwén ánde fronde efalo yátogh ár karamangayénd fá ménát Ngánjánene ngárám ngángayam so tawafrotau Ngánjánene ásáfogh. Yéné sómbio árém ewifeyayénd ásáfogh némé té ewarameyayénd.
english: From there they sailed to Antioch, from where they had been committed to the grace of God for the work which they had fulfilled .

nama: Antiok nufareyayénd, yémofem eumendangeyayénd Yesuene mérén a tawaufrowét méinyotio Ngánjánam némé tawafrotau yéfe kénjúmé a ndernáye Ngánjánam yésawéryéng ndimbal mwighé sésafne Téndo árét.
english: When they had arrived and had gathered the assembly together , they reported all the things that God had done with them , and that he had opened a door of faith to the nations .

nama: A fá kété teke emorarmén waitogh árfé.
english: They stayed there with the disciples for a long time .

nama: Ámb ár enmormén Judiata Antiokét a efalo yátogh ár tawatametawét: “Táf féfene ghéngan yau njam tánfangi, ndené Mosesene nákam yénémndete, yau so kawanjar.”
english: Some men came down from Judea and taught the brothers, “ Unless you are circumcised after the custom of Moses , you can ’ t be saved .”

nama: Yéné watamegh yamam eméindangeyai Fol a Banabas éwighét yémafé yéné watameghmén. Fol a Banabas ekafeyayénd a ámb efalo yátogh árfé, angoghét Jerusalemét Yesuene muyafé ár fandaghét a ámb sénko árfé yéné watameghmén.
english: Therefore when Paul and Barnabas had no small discord and discussion with them , they appointed Paul , Barnabas, and some others of them to go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and elders about this question .

nama: Yesuene mérénam engwatembayénd Fol a Banabas tangmorwén yénamé Fonisiya a Samariamé, a yémofem ewaufroyénd ndernáye Téndo ár té kawengotat. Yéné siam eméindayéng méinyotio efalo yátogh ár áuwéghét.
english: They , being sent on their way by the assembly , passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria , declaring the conversion of the Gentiles . They caused great joy to all the brothers.

nama: Fá njam nufaryénd Jerusalemét, Yesuene mérénam a Yesuene muyafé ár a ámb sénko árém eumieyayénd Fol a Banabas, endefet yémofem tawaufrowét méinyotio yammén Ngánjánam némé tafrotau yéfe kénjúmé.
english: When they had come to Jerusalem , they were received by the assembly and the apostles and the elders , and they reported everything that God had done with them .

nama: Féyo ámb efalo yátogh ár efe tawafnyangérwén Farasi mérénmén nokayoi a tamndangé, “Téndo ár so fété ghéngan enfetat ndenáye Mosesene nákam kés noraye.”
english: But some of the sect of the Pharisees who believed rose up , saying , “ It is necessary to circumcise them , and to command them to keep the law of Moses .”

nama: Yesuene muyafé ár a ámb sénko ár náumendayénd yéné watamegh simén.
english: The apostles and the elders were gathered together to see about this matter .

nama: Yéné watamegh si tombé warunyégh soramé, Fité nokayoi a korayangé yéfefaf: “Fútaréf, fém mat em ndené tándái Ngánjánam waferyéng féfene kénjún ménát téndo árém sái káyárangi tane mbírmbírta mer si a efalo tiyátongi.
english: When there had been much discussion , Peter rose up and said to them , “Brothers, you know that a good while ago God made a choice among you that by my mouth the nations should hear the word of the Good News and believe .

nama: Ngánján, efe mat yém árfene tikéf, ewauyafote ndené yémon eyátote téndo ár ménaye Ngánján Mbérmbér raméghe ndené fété téfe kés yénmaramai.
english: God , who knows the heart , testified about them , giving them the Holy Spirit , just like he did to us .

nama: Yémon mwighé yéfaronam ménát ámbiro so tényátonga téndo árfé, ménamén yémon ewáwáine yéfene tikéf ménaye ndimbal mwighaye.
english: He made no distinction between us and them , cleansing their hearts by faith .

nama: Féyo fémofem yau so ufoghan Ngánján yékmetati sénonjo ménaye kérté fayoghe téndo efalo yátogh árét ndenéyameyo téfene ákifet a téfeyot árkéméné yau so tanangém.
english: Now therefore why do you tempt God , that you should put a yoke on the neck of the disciples which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?

nama: Yéndfem efalo yiyátotam ndené téfene Yuré Yesuene ngárám kénjúmé yénd nawanjanam, ndenéyame fá kés em.”
english: But we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus , just as they are .”

nama: Ár mérén métar nufrotaménd fá káyáretawét yéfefaf Banabas a Fol kafrowét ménamén ufarogh yam a sélngwélafé yam Ngánjánam tawafrotau Téndo árfefaf yéfe kénjúmé.
english: All the multitude kept silence , and they listened to Barnabas and Paul reporting what signs and wonders God had done among the nations through them .

nama: Njam Banabas a Fité yifeyayénd si, Jems nokayoi siafé: “Fútaréf, yénd kwiyárangi.
english: After they were silent , James answered , “Brothers, listen to me .

nama: Saimon Fitam tafenau téfefaf ndernáye Ngánjánam fronde yuyafotam yánjo mwighé ménaye ndernáye Téndo ár té eyátoi yáyot.
english: Simeon has reported how God first visited the nations to take out of them a people for his name .

nama: A yéné téndo árfene awengogh siyém yénayanfifi némé Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár kénorayawét. Ndené kés yékai:
english: This agrees with the words of the prophets . As it is written ,

nama: 'Yéné soramé yénd so nénangotan, a kor so yafarnjan Deifidene méngotu. Yéndon kor so yérametan wasérmboghkaf yém, a yéndon so kor yafrotan,
english: ‘ After these things I will return. I will again build the tabernacle of David , which has fallen . I will again build its ruins . I will set it up

nama: ménát méinyotio árém so yéronjat Yuré, so tanéfghangé Téndo ár emofem tane yétkwén so yuyotat, Yuré noraye, emo ewafrote yéné yam
english: that the rest of men may seek after the Lord : all the Gentiles who are called by my name , says the Lord , who does all these things .’ + 15:17 Amos 9:11-12

nama: tándái árkéméné mat náyátoyénd.'
english: “All of God’s works are known to him from eternity.

nama: “Yénémaménnde tane mwighé yém yéndfem yau so táikér yiyátotam ménát ghéngan néfghét yéfe Téndo árét efe nawengotat Ngánjánefaf.
english: Therefore my judgment is that we don’ t trouble those from among the Gentiles who turn to God ,

nama: Wénde yéndfem férén so yéfarotam yéfeyot a engwaufrotam ménát yau so enetat njamke árkéméné mewang sifayamén em, yau táf téndo wal, yau táf enetat férék eréghofnar wérár a férék yau táf enetati.
english: but that we write to them that they abstain from the pollution of idols , from sexual immorality , from what is strangled , and from blood.

nama: Ménamén Mosesene nák si téngwaitotawét méinyotio tesenan fronde efghon a taftawét Ju árfene áumengégh méngon méinyotio Safatan.” Férén yéfeyot téndo efalo yátogh árfefaf
english: For Moses from generations of old has in every city those who preach him , being read in the synagogues every Sabbath .”

nama: Féyo Yesuene muyafé ár a sénko ár yémafé méinyotio Yesuene mérén mwighé yéfaronaménd yéfenjo ár kafghét álighét kétán Antiok yémafé Fol a Banabas. Yémofem ekafayénd Judas efe tanmotawét Barsabas a Sailes, yéné ár efe tárwén limánégh ár efalo yátogh árfene kénjún.
english: Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders , with the whole assembly , to choose men out of their company, and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas: Judas called Barsabbas, and Silas , chief men among the brothers.

nama: Yéfe kénjúmé yémofem yatembayénd yénamayo férén: “Yesuene muyafé ár a sénko ár, fene fútaréf Jerusalemén, Kétán téndo efalo yátogh árfefaf kétán Antiok, Siria a Silisia: Áuwégh si.
english: They wrote these things by their hand : “ The apostles, the elders , and the brothers, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria , and Cilicia : greetings .

nama: Yénd té náyárangém ndené ámb té nétorngi téfe tambén téfene matofnaye a fene mwighé kémawasérmbotat, ménaye fá némé korayat.
english: Because we have heard that some who went out from us have troubled you with words , unsettling your souls , saying , ‘ You must be circumcised and keep the law,’ to whom we gave no commandment ;

nama: Yénémamén yéndfem méinyotio efalo yiyátongém ekafangém ámb ár a engwatembangém féfefaf yémafé téfene tikfafé fútar Banabas a Fol,
english: it seemed good to us , having come to one accord , to choose out men and send them to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul ,

nama: ár emofem yéfenjo ghérsé kértat ekmend Yuré Yesu Kerisoene yétkwénot.
english: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ .

nama: Yénémaménnde yéndfem Judas a Sailes engwáfárewam féfefaf ménát yémofem yénayo si so efalo yiyátot mbírmbíre némé yéndfem yéngwafnamotam.
english: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas , who themselves will also tell you the same things by word of mouth .

nama: Ngánján Mbérmbér a yénd efalo té yiyátongém ndené yéndfem yau so kérté ewarametam táikér yame wénde mé nambio yamero:
english: For it seemed good to the Holy Spirit , and to us , to lay no greater burden on you than these necessary things :

nama: Fém so nafrangotati kétándmé njamke árkéméné mewang sifayamén em, férékta, férék eréghofnar wérárta a téndo walta. Merfifi yém yéné yamta kékmbongi. Yénaro fai.”
english: that you abstain from things sacrificed to idols , from blood, from things strangled , and from sexual immorality , from which if you keep yourselves , it will be well with you . Farewell .”

nama: Ár ewatembayénd a tayotau Antiok yuwot. Kété fá káumendawét Yesuene mérén mérne a kété férén yéramayénd.
english: So , when they were sent off, they came to Antioch. Having gathered the multitude together , they delivered the letter .

nama: Árém njam taftawét áuwéghan tamorwén yéné wawéngsogh simén.
english: When they had read it , they rejoiced over the encouragement .

nama: Judas a Sailes, efe tárwén Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár, ndimbal wawéngsogh a muyé si koraiwét efalo yátogh árfefaf.
english: Judas and Silas , also being prophets themselves , encouraged the brothers with many words and strengthened them .

nama: Ámb efogh soramé kété, Judas a Sailes ewatembeyayénd fútarfem frengéghafé a áuwéghafé kangowét yéfefaf emofem enwáfáreménd.
english: After they had spent some time there , they were dismissed in peace from the brothers to the apostles.

nama: -35 Wénde Fol a Banabas mé tamorarwén kété Antiokén, ánde fá a tárfár árfé Yurane si táitotawét a tawatametawét. Fol a Banabas nafaleyayénd

english: But Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord , with many others also .

nama: Ámb efogh soramé Folém yémndayéng Banabas, “So nangowam kétán a eyafowam efalo yátogh ár méinyotio tesenan ánde yéndfem Yurane si taitowém a tanwinjem némé yémofem ewafrotat.”
english: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, “ Let ’ s return now and visit our brothers in every city in which we proclaimed the word of the Lord , to see how they are doing.”

nama: Banabas ménde témorwén Jon Make wasroghét yémafé,
english: Barnabas planned to take John , who was called Mark , with them also .

nama: wénde Fol yau ndené kemnau sái tasrongai fá, ménamén Jon Makém efrangoi kété Famfilia yuwon a nékmboyéng kétamé ásáfogh tambén.
english: But Paul didn’ t think that it was a good idea to take with them someone who had withdrawn from them in Pamphylia , and didn’ t go with them to do the work .

nama: Kété ewarmén ndimbal éwigh a nafalotaménd. Banabasém yasroi Mak a tayowéng ndimbal boute kétán Sáifras,
english: Then the contention grew so sharp that they separated from each other . Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus ,

nama: wénde Folém yaferyéng Sailes a efalo yátogh árém erameyayénd Fol a Sailes Yurane ngárám kénjút. Féyo sómbio nuwaneyayénd.
english: but Paul chose Silas and went out , being commended by the brothers to the grace of God .

nama: Fá nuwaneyayénd yénamé Siria a Silisia yu, yémofem taitoghwét si ménát muyé tawaramwét Yesuene mérnat. Folene yénamayo áligh (Acts 15.36—18.22)|src="nmx_205wbt - Paul journey 2 [bwf-light] @en_v4.tif" size="col" copy="© 2025 Wycliffe Bible Translators, Inc." ref="15.36—18.22"
english: He went through Syria and Cilicia , strengthening the assemblies .

nama: Fol yénmormén Derbe tesenot a yénamé kétán Listra tesen, ánde waitogh ár yétkwén Timoti témorangérwén. Yátmé témorwén Ju amaf a efalo yátogh ár, wénde yárefé témorwén Grikmén ár.
english: He came to Derbe and Lystra ; and behold , a certain disciple was there , named Timothy , the son of a Jewess who believed , but his father was a Greek .

nama: Efalo yátogh ár mer si korayawét Timotienemén Listran a Ikoniummén.
english: The brothers who were at Lystra and Iconium gave a good testimony about him .

nama: Fol ménde témorwén yémafé álighét, féyo yémo ghéngan yanfai ménamén Ju árfenemén efe tamorangérwén yéné yuwon, ménamén fá méinyotio mat tamorwén ndené yárefé témorwén Grikmén ár.
english: Paul wanted to have him go out with him , and he took and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in those parts , for they all knew that his father was a Greek .

nama: Yéfene áligh yam kétándmé ámb tesen kétán ámb tesen, yémofem tawawéngsotawét efalo yátogh ár ménát so táitotawét yéné férén árké yéfarotaménd Yesuene muyafé árém a ámb sénko árém Jerusalemén.
english: As they went on their way through the cities , they delivered the decrees to them to keep which had been ordained by the apostles and elders who were at Jerusalem .

nama: Féyo Yesuene mérén táf káyátotawét muyafé ndimbal mwighayan a kanotawét méinyotio efghon. Folene ghékéf waingégh Makedonia ároenemén
english: So the assemblies were strengthened in the faith , and increased in number daily .

nama: Fol yánjo mérnafé tayotau yéné yuwomé Frigia a Galasia, Ngánján Mbérmbéram yau eumyoi si waitoghét Esia yuwon.
english: When they had gone through the region of Phrygia and Galatia , they were forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia .

nama: Fá engmormén Misia mbandot néngufaryénd, féyo fá ménde tamorwén sái kényawangai Biténiat, wénde Yesuene Mbémbéram yau eumyoi élaughét.
english: When they had come opposite Mysia , they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit didn’ t allow them .

nama: Féyo yémofem yéngwaindayénd Misia a néngaflayénd kétán Troasot.
english: Passing by Mysia , they came down to Troas .

nama: Yéné sémbáran Folém téfandau ghékéf waingéghe Makedoniamén ár takayongérwén tamnendau fá, “Kénaf Makedonia ndimbal yuwot téfe wáutárghét.”
english: A vision appeared to Paul in the night . There was a man of Macedonia standing , begging him and saying , “ Come over into Macedonia and help us .”

nama: Folene ghékéf waingégh fandagh soramé, yénd wékeye káfrendawém álighét kétán Makedoniat. Ménamén mat náyátoyém ndené Ngánjánam yénmanmoi yénd mer si waitoghét kétán yéfefaf. Lidiane awengogh yaufita Filifiyan
english: When he had seen the vision , immediately we sought to go out to Macedonia , concluding that the Lord had called us to preach the Good News to them .

nama: Yénd ndimbal boute tényotau Troasta kétán Samotrasét, a ámb efghon néngufaryém Neafolisét.
english: Setting sail therefore from Troas , we made a straight course to Samothrace , and the day following to Neapolis ;

nama: Kétamé yénd néngwanoyém kétán Filifi, Rom mwighafé yu a limánégh tesen Makedonia ndimbal yuwot. Kété yénmorangérmén tárfár efogh.
english: and from there to Philippi , which is a city of Macedonia , the foremost of the district , a Roman colony . We were staying some days in this city .

nama: Ámb Safatan yénd néngtoryém tesen sésafneta kétán wanjet, yéné mwighafé Ngánjánafé orayégh sifayé kété so yém. Yénd námnjoyém a si yérnyayém yéné amafafefaf efe nénáumendayénd kété.
english: On the Sabbath day we went outside of the city by a riverside , where we supposed there was a place of prayer , and we sat down and spoke to the women who had come together .

nama: Ámb amaf efe káyáretau yáne yétkwén Lidia, efe témorwén Tayatira tesenmén. Yémon ndéndák ngarot sáláme faian yékmai a fá témorwén Ngánjánene wáwefnogh amaf. Yuram yasawéryéng yáne tikéf áyárghét kétán Folene sifaf.
english: A certain woman named Lydia , a seller of purple , of the city of Thyatira , one who worshiped God , heard us. The Lord opened her heart to listen to the things which were spoken by Paul .

nama: Amaf a yáne mérén ewalitotam, yéné soramé amafom yénumyoi yánjo méngot. Amafom témndangé, “Njam fém nemnati ndené yénd wém efalo yátogh ár Yurane kénjún, kénaflangi tane méngon so emor.” Féyo amafom yénmamnendai yénd, féyo yénd kété yénmorangérmén. Fol a Sailes kwéfkwéf méngot elaweyayénd
english: When she and her household were baptized, she begged us , saying , “ If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord , come into my house and stay .” So she persuaded us .

nama: Ámb káye yénd ténngérwén Ngánjánafé orayégh sifayat, yénd yénfangeyai faiofnar ásáfogh mérsom efe yaufi mbérmbéroene waftaye korayau soramé yumén. Yémon tawinjotau ndimbal mani árfe tambén ménamén korayau soramé yumén. Yémon tawarametau mani yánjo wanjengégh téfnárat.
english: As we were going to prayer , a certain girl having a spirit of divination met us , who brought her masters much gain by fortune telling.

nama: Yéné mérsom Fol a yénd yénmaitoyéng ndené keyafé, “Yéné ár fem Minde Tukén Ngánjánene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, emofem ewafrotat end féfe wanjarghét.”
english: Following Paul and us , she cried out , “ These men are servants of the Most High God , who proclaim to us a way of salvation !”

nama: Mérés ménj mé térnyangérwén tárfár efogh yéné sian. Kerkeran Fol nékwafé náindam nawengoi a témndangé mbérmbér, “Yesu Kerisoene yétkwénan fém kétor yá tambénǃ” Táf fété mbérmbéram mérés yéfrangoi.
english: She was doing this for many days . But Paul , becoming greatly annoyed , turned and said to the spirit , “ I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her !” It came out that very hour .

nama: Njam faiofnar ásáfogh mérésoene yuram yáyámngoyénd ndené mani winjo end té nefo, yémofem Fol a Sailes ngi ewafngeyayénd taroghwét fá marat sifayamé kétán sénko árfefaf.
english: But when her masters saw that the hope of their gain was gone , they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the rulers.

nama: Yémofem enémnjéménd yéné yumén gafanane sáfrére a norayayénd, “Yéné ár sere Jumén, yémofem ár mwighayan ewanjmengét,
english: When they had brought them to the magistrates , they said , “ These men , being Jews , are agitating our city

nama: yémofem ár ewatamet fénaté si yéné mbarkánd yau árké nérete téfe Rom árét wafroghét.”
english: and advocate customs which it is not lawful for us to accept or to observe , being Romans .”

nama: Ár mérén nafghayénd Fol a Sailes yéfe ngiot, a yéné ndimbal yumén mogh árfene wáfáreghan so yéfene sáláme tawaftérarwét a tafmowét.
english: The multitude rose up together against them and the magistrates tore their clothes from them , then commanded them to be beaten with rods .

nama: Féyo yaufi fémogh efmoménd, a fá ewiménd kétán kwéfkwéf méngot, a yéné sifayé wanjengégh ár yémndayénd merkéráye wanjengéghét.
english: When they had laid many stripes on them , they threw them into prison , charging the jailer to keep them safely.

nama: Njam fá náyárai wáfaregh si, yémon ewármbneyai sómbio ár a ewangseyayénd wéne yéfene ghambér. Fol a Sailes tarwén kwéfkwéf méngon (Acts 16.24)|src="IB04207gr.tif" size="col" copy="Farid Faadil. © Biblica, Inc. Used with permission. All rights reserved worldwide." ref="16.24"
english: Having received such a command , he threw them into the inner prison and secured their feet in the stocks .

nama: Sémbár néndkéfan Fol a Sailes Ngánjánjafé korayawét a wagh kermangwét Ngánjánefaf, a ámb ár kwéfkwéf méngon talawangérwén táyárwét waghan.
english: But about midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God , and the prisoners were listening to them .

nama: Sunsune ndimbal sokrereyam kwéfkwéf méngo sérasér esámbnetam. Táf fété méinyotio kwéfkwéf méngo sésafne kasaunawét, a méinyotio árfene sein naferyénd.
english: Suddenly there was a great earthquake , so that the foundations of the prison were shaken ; and immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone ’s bonds were loosened.

nama: Kwéfkwéf méngo wanjengégh ár nanoi, a njam néngretai kwéfkwéf méngo sésafne sauréghkaf tamorwén, fá witmafé sénté naifaye akunyéghét féf nufngoi krotio ménamén fá kéma kemnau nde kwéfkwéf méngo ár kar té nétoryénd.
english: The jailer , being roused out of sleep and seeing the prison doors open, drew his sword and was about to kill himself , supposing that the prisoners had escaped .

nama: Wénde Fol nutnai, “Yau nawaghna fémyoǃ Yénd méinyotio yéna mé yénémǃ”
english: But Paul cried with a loud voice , saying , “Don’t harm yourself , for we are all here !”

nama: Kwéfkwéf méngo wanjengégh ár néngutnai kwayaghat a soramé ewawanayénd kwayaghé, néngénngoi kwéfkwéf méngo kénjút a nénguyai ngerngerafé Fol a Sailesfene sáfrére.
english: He called for lights , sprang in , fell down trembling before Paul and Silas ,

nama: Féyo yémon enutoreyai kénjúta a emotangeyai, “Ndimbal, yéndon némé so yafrotan awanjarghét?”
english: brought them out , and said , “ Sirs , what must I do to be saved ?”

nama: Yémofem yawangeyayénd, “Yuré Yesu efalo tiyáto, a táf fém so kawanjar fene mérnafé.”
english: They said , “ Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ , and you will be saved , you and your household .”

nama: Féyo fá kafrowét Yurane si yáfaf a méinyotio ámb árfefaf yáne méngon.
english: They spoke the word of the Lord to him , and to all who were in his house .

nama: Yéné sémbáranyo kwéfkwéf méngo wanjengégh árom emnjéméng a yéfene fénend tawaumbarwéng; féyo táf fété fá yánjo mérnafayo nuan ewalitoménd.
english: He took them the same hour of the night and washed their stripes , and was immediately baptized, he and all his household.

nama: Kwéfkwéf méngo wanjengégh árom emnjéméng yáne méngot a ewémnjeyai nénet; fá áuwégham yafoi ménamén yémon Ngánján efalo yiyátoi yánjo méinyotio mérnafé.
english: He brought them up into his house and set food before them , and rejoiced greatly with all his household , having believed in God .

nama: Njam akw yéfauwéryéng, Rom mogh árém ewáfáretayénd yéfene ásáfogh ár kwéfkwéf méngo wanjengégh ároefaf: “Tautorend yéné ár nguwanend.”
english: But when it was day , the magistrates sent the sergeants, saying , “ Let those men go.”

nama: Kwéfkwéf méngo wanjengégh árom taufrongé Fole, “Rom mogh árém nénorayangi ndené fém a Sailes so tautorend. Sénonjo fémofem yéné sifayé téfrangend kuwanend krufráte.”
english: The jailer reported these words to Paul , saying , “ The magistrates have sent to let you go ; now therefore come out and go in peace .”

nama: Wénde Folém korayangé ásáfogh árfefaf: “Yémofem yénd ténsémét marataye moghofnar yuwon, ndemamén yénd yénre Rom yumén ár, a yénmiménd kwéfkwéf méngo kénjút. Sénonjo yénd yémofem métare yénmutorend? Yauǃ Fá fáyo táf kénaflangi a téngémnjend yénd.”
english: But Paul said to them , “ They have beaten us publicly without a trial, men who are Romans , and have cast us into prison ! Do they now release us secretly ? No , most certainly , but let them come themselves and bring us out !”

nama: Ásáfogh árém njam ewaufroyénd Rom mogh árfet ndené Fol a Sailes tárwén Rom yumén ár, fá férféram ewifoi.
english: The sergeants reported these words to the magistrates , and they were afraid when they heard that they were Romans ,

nama: Fá nénaflayénd yéfefaf ghérghérsisé siot a enémnjeyayénd kétándmé kwéfkwéf méngota, ewamnendangeyayénd tesen frangoghét.
english: and they came and begged them . When they had brought them out , they asked them to depart from the city .

nama: Soramé Fol a Sailes néntoreyayénd kwéfkwéf méngota, fá néngwaneyayénd Lidiane méngot, ánde nafángoyénd efalo yátogh árfé tawawéngsowét fá. Féyo yémofem táf efrangeyayénd.
english: They went out of the prison and entered into Lydia ’s house. When they had seen the brothers, they encouraged them , then departed .

nama: Njam Fol yáne fútarfé nawaindayénd Amfifolis a Afoloniamé, fá enmormén Tesalonikat, kété témorwén Ju árfene áumengégh méngo.
english: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica , where there was a Jewish synagogue .

nama: Yáne túmén yam témorwén, Fol téngmorwén Ju árfene áumengégh méngo kénjút, a nambio Safat efghon yémon yérnyai warunyégh árfé,
english: Paul , as was his custom , went in to them ; and for three Sabbath days reasoned with them from the Scriptures ,

nama: a Buk si tawawafenau ndené Keriso tékrátan so yém a kérta kanongé. Fá norayai, “Yéné Yesu siyém Keriso yéndon endenemén yaitotan féfeyot.”
english: explaining and demonstrating that the Christ had to suffer and rise again from the dead , and saying , “ This Jesus , whom I proclaim to you , is the Christ .”

nama: Ámb Ju árém efalo yiyátoyénd a nufnyayénd Fol a Sailesfefaf. Kété tamorwén tárfár Téndo ár Ngánján férférafé a tárfár sénko amaf.
english: Some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas : of the devout Greeks a great multitude , and not a few of the chief women .

nama: Wénde ámb Ju ár kawaramnjawét, yénémamén yémofem eumendayénd kérté wafrogh ár mérén marat sifayan, awifarégh yérnyayénd tesenan. Fá néngenngoyénd Jasonene méngot Fol a Sailes yéfe ronjaghét a wasroghét ár mérénfaf.
english: But the unpersuaded Jews took along some wicked men from the marketplace and gathering a crowd, set the city in an uproar . Assaulting the house of Jason , they sought to bring them out to the people .

nama: Wénde njam yémofem yau ewinjeyayénd Fol a Sailes, yémofem Jason a ámb efalo yátogh ár tesenmén tawafaretawét gafanane sáfrére, yéné keyafé, “Yéné ár fem emofem yéfunat kérté méinyotio mband maratan sénonjo yéna em,
english: When they didn’ t find them , they dragged Jason and certain brothers before the rulers of the city , crying, “ These who have turned the world upside down have come here also ,

nama: a Jasoném eumyoi fá yáne méngot. Fá fem emofem méinyotio Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisarene nák erafnat ndené siafé Yesu siyém ámb warsagh ár.”
english: whom Jason has received . These all act contrary to the decrees of Caesar , saying that there is another king , Jesus !”

nama: Njam fá náyárayénd yéné si, ár mérén a tesenmén gafana fá nékw nawerayénd.
english: The multitude and the rulers of the city were troubled when they heard these things .

nama: Féyo yémofem fai ewafrotaménd Jason a ámb efalo yátogh ár utorghét. Fol a Sailesfem mer si taitowét Bereya kénjún
english: When they had taken security from Jason and the rest , they let them go .

nama: Yéné sémbáranyo, ámb efalo yátogh árém engwatembeyayénd Fol a Sailes Bereya tesenot. Ufarghan Fol a Sailes, fá néngwaneyayénd Ju árfene áumengégh méngot.
english: The brothers immediately sent Paul and Silas away by night to Beroea. When they arrived , they went into the Jewish synagogue .

nama: Ménamén Bereyamén Ju ár tamorwén minde mer ár, yéfene wafroghyamam ewaindayéng Tesalonika yumén ár, yémofem yumyoyénd si áuwéghafé a tafénmetawét Buk si túmén efghon téfandawét némé Fol efalo korayau.
english: Now these were more noble than those in Thessalonica , in that they received the word with all readiness of mind , examining the Scriptures daily to see whether these things were so .

nama: Tárfár Ju árém efalo yiyátoyénd si a ámb ndimbal yétkwén amafam a ámb Grikmén árém.
english: Many of them therefore believed ; also of the prominent Greek women , and not a few men .

nama: Njam Ju ár mat náyátoyénd Tesalonika kénjún ndené Folém táitotau Ngánjánene si Bereyan, ámb ár wéi kétán néngwanoyénd a amrorégh yérnyayénd ár mérén tawanjmetawét.
english: But when the Jews of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was proclaimed by Paul at Beroea also , they came there likewise , agitating the multitudes .

nama: Táf fété efalo yátogh árém Fol yáfáretaménd arakaf tambén, wénde Saileséf Timotifé tamorarwén Bereyan.
english: Then the brothers immediately sent out Paul to go as far as to the sea , and Silas and Timothy still stayed there .

nama: Njam yéné árém yénémnjaménd Fol Atenét, Fol norayai yéfefaf ndené, “Sailes a Timoti tafaf táf enre wékeye.” Féyo táf néngwanoyénd. Folém mer si táitotau Aten kénjún
english: But those who escorted Paul brought him as far as Athens. Receiving a commandment to Silas and Timothy that they should come to him very quickly , they departed .

nama: Njam Fol kawanjetau yéfeyot Sailes a Timoti Aten tesenan, fá áuwéghofnar témorwén ménamén yinjoi yéné tesen mewang afoghare.
english: Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was provoked within him as he saw the city full of idols .

nama: Yémon éwigh yérnyai yémafé Ju árfene áumengégh méngon Ju ár a Ngánján férférafé Téndo ár. Fá wéi táf téngmorwén marat sifayat méinyotio efghon a ár kété tamndetau.
english: So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons , and in the marketplace every day with those who met him .

nama: Mérén tárwén sómbio, ámb Efikuria mérén a ámb Stoik mérén ndimbal matyamafé ár éwigh yérnyayénd Folfé. Ámb kamotangi, “Némé siot yéné waréwaré nufngo?” Ámb korayangi, “Fá mété noraya ámb ngánjánmén.” Fá korayawét yéné si ménamén Folém táitotau mer si Yesuenemén a yáne anoghmén.
english: Some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also were conversing with him . Some said , “ What does this babbler want to say ?” Others said , “ He seems to be advocating foreign deities ,” because he preached Jesus and the resurrection.

nama: Yémofem yéngwasroyénd fá sénko árfene áumengégh sifayé yétkwén Areyofagus, ánde yémofem fá témndetawét, “Féyotaro fémo so ténmaufro matot némé totér watamegh fémo yaitote?
english: They took hold of him and brought him to the Areopagus, saying , “ May we know what this new teaching is , which you are speaking about ?

nama: Fémon téfe yénérse fénataye watamegh náyáretam, a yénd ménde yéném mat sái káyátongém némé yáne fifi yém.”
english: For you bring certain strange things to our ears. We want to know therefore what these things mean .”

nama: (Méinyotio Atenmén ár a ámb yuta ár efe tamorangérwén kété efogh tifotawét ámnjoghanro wénde korayawét a káyáretawét soramamén siot)
english: Now all the Athenians and the strangers living there spent their time in nothing else , but either to tell or to hear some new thing .

nama: Féyo Fol nokayoi sénko árfene áumengégh sifayé yétkwén Areyofagus a tamndangé, “Atenmén árǃ Yéndon tawafandan fémofem ewáwefnotati tárfár ngánjánafet mindefifi.
english: Paul stood in the middle of the Areopagus and said , “ You men of Athens, I perceive that you are very religious in all things .

nama: Ménamén yénd njam karendan táf kor ewawinjon féfene wáwefnogh rokar. Yéndon tawafandan kitarékitaré yéné yétkwénafé: Kétán Mat Yátoghofnar Ngánján. Fém mat yau em némayoe fémofem yáwefnotati, sénonjo yéndon so ewaufrotan féfeyot yánemén.
english: For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship , I also found an altar with this inscription : ‘ TO AN UNKNOWN GOD .’ What therefore you worship in ignorance, I announce to you .

nama: “Ngánján emo yafrotam mband marat a méinyotio rokar yéné kénjún. Ménamén yémo elimnde méinyotio rokar njafaran a mbandan, fá yau yémor ár ngángé raméghmén wáwefnogh méngon.
english: The God who made the world and all things in it , he , being Lord of heaven and earth , doesn’ t dwell in temples made with hands .

nama: A fá yau náronje yéné rokarot árém árké ewafrotat yáyot, ménamén fá fáyo méinyotio árfene ghérsé ramégh ár yém a méinyotio rokar kés.
english: He isn’ t served by men ’s hands , as though he needed anything , seeing he himself gives to all life and breath and all things .

nama: Rényéghan Ngánjánam yafrotam ámbiro ár, Yá tambén Ngánjánam ewafrotam méinyotio ár mérén efe sénonjo emorang méinyotio mband maratan. A yémon wéi mwighé yéfaronam ánde fénatayero ár mérén so emor a nderé teke fá so emor.
english: He made from one blood every nation of men to dwell on all the surface of the earth , having determined appointed seasons and the boundaries of their dwellings,

nama: Ngánján ménde yém árém sái yéronjat. Ménamén fá yau nayu yém téfeta, féyo yémofem mété táf yéronjat fá a tinjongi.
english: that they should seek the Lord , if perhaps they might reach out for him and find him , though he is not far from each one of us .

nama: 'Ménamén yáne waftayan yénd ghérsé yénmor a nimndam a naketam.' Ndenanit féfenjo ár efe mat em férénafene fifi, fá norayat ndenanit, 'Yénd yéném yáne ambumtértér.'
english: ‘ For in him we live , move , and have our being .’ As some of your own poets have said , ‘ For we are also his offspring .’

nama: “Ménamén yénd Ngánjánene ambumtértér kés yéném, yénd yau so nemnam ndené árém so ewafrotat Ngánján kétamé gol o silfé o wím yéfenjo matyame.
english: Being then the offspring of God , we ought not to think that the Divine Nature is like gold , or silver, or stone , engraved by art and design of man .

nama: Tándái ár njam yau mat tamorwén némé Ngánján ménde témorwén yéfefaf wafroghét, Ngánjánam yau kértayan tákmangai ménamén yéfene matyam yau, wénde sénonjo Ngánjánam emndete méinyotio ár méinyotio yuwomé yaufita awengoghét.
english: The times of ignorance therefore God overlooked . But now he commands that all people everywhere should repent ,

nama: Efogh té yaferyéng njam mband so moghan tékmangé. Yémo té yékafai ár emo so moghan ténékmangé mbarkánde. Ngánjánam té yuyafoi yéné ár, efe Ngánjánam té yékafai ménát sái emota mband marat, yémo so moghan tékmangé mband marat ménamén Ngánjánam yanoi kérta.”
english: because he has appointed a day in which he will judge the world in righteousness by the man whom he has ordained ; of which he has given assurance to all men , in that he has raised him from the dead .”

nama: Njam fá náyárayénd ároene kér anoghmén si, ámb árém turtawét, wénde ámb korayawét, “Yénd ménde yéném fém sái kor koraya téfefaf yéné si téfén.”
english: Now when they heard of the resurrection of the dead , some mocked ; but others said , “ We want to hear you again concerning this .”

nama: Yéné áumengégh soramé Folém efrangoi.
english: Thus Paul went out from among them .

nama: Ámb ár namndayénd Folene waitogh ár a efalo yiyátoyénd. Yéfene kénjún tafnyangérwén Dionisus, efe témorwén Areyofagus mérénmén, ndenáyo amaf yétkwén Damaris, a ámb ár kés.
english: But certain men joined with him and believed , including Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris , and others with them .

nama: Yéné soramé, Folém yéfrangoi Aten tesen a néngwanoi Korint tesenot.
english: After these things Paul departed from Athens and came to Corinth .

nama: Kété yémon yéfángoi Ju ár yétkwén Akwila Fontusmén. Fá yénmormén kétándmé Italia yánjo amafafé yétkwén Frisila, ménamén Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisar Klaudiasém emndai “Fém Ju ár, fémofem so téfrangongi Rom.” Fol néngaflai Akwila a Frisila fandaghét,
english: He found a certain Jew named Aquila, a man of Pontus by race , who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla , because Claudius had commanded all the Jews to depart from Rome . He came to them ,

nama: a ménamén Fol témorwén farai méngo ramégh ár ndené fá kés tamorwén, Fol námnjoyéng a kásáfotawét yémafé.
english: and because he practiced the same trade , he lived with them and worked , for by trade they were tent makers.

nama: Méinyotio Safatan Folfem taruindawét Ju árfene áumengégh méngon, ménát sái tikéf áfrat táyátongayénd Ju ár a Téndo ár.
english: He reasoned in the synagogue every Sabbath and persuaded Jews and Greeks .

nama: Njam Sailes a Timoti nufareyayénd kétándmé Makedoniata, yéna Folém yéta Ju ár matot tarametau ndené Yesu siyém Keriso.
english: When Silas and Timothy came down from Macedonia , Paul was compelled by the Spirit , testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ .

nama: Wénde Ju árém tékarotawét Fol a yaufi si témndetawét. Féyo yénémamén yémon ngwérméng yétnendai yánjo sálámeta a tamndangé fá, “Njam Ngánjánam tékratan tákmangé, so yém féfenjo téfnan, yau so yém tane téfén. Yénd nawafenan féfefaf yénd yau kérté so wém. Yénandmé yénd so kuwanon sénonjo Téndo árfefaf.”
english: When they opposed him and blasphemed, he shook out his clothing and said to them , “ Your blood be on your own heads ! I am clean . From now on , I will go to the Gentiles !”

nama: Féyo Folém yéngéfrangoi Ju árfene áumengégh méngo a néngaflai kaka méngot, Taitus Jéstusene méngot efe témorwén Ngánján wáwefnogh ár.
english: He departed there and went into the house of a certain man named Justus , one who worshiped God , whose house was next door to the synagogue .

nama: Krisfus, Ju árfene áumengégh méngo limánégh ár, a yánjo mérnam Yuré efalo yiyátoyénd a tárfár Korintmén ár efe káyáretawét, efalo yiyátoyénd a nuwan ewalitotam.
english: Crispus , the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord with all his house . Many of the Corinthians , when they heard, believed and were baptized.

nama: Ámb sémbáran Yuré norayai Folefaf ghékéf waingégh kénjún, “Yau férfér ném, sian miya néngém, yau métar ném a nékmbo.
english: The Lord said to Paul in the night by a vision , “Don’ t be afraid , but speak and don’ t be silent ;

nama: Ménamén yénd fémafé yénre, a emo yau so nékmbote a némasérmbote fém, ménamén tane tárfár ár em yéné tesenan.”
english: for I am with you , and no one will attack you to harm you , for I have many people in this city .”

nama: Yéné téfnan Fol yémorangérmén Korintén ámbiro yunjéf ámb yunjéf rér yéramai fá tawatametau Ngánjánene si.
english: He lived there a year and six months , teaching the word of God among them .

nama: Njam Galio témorwén gafana Akaya ndimbal yumén, ámb Ju ár nafghayénd Fole kékmboghét a yénmasroyénd moghét.
english: But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews with one accord rose up against Paul and brought him before the judgment seat,

nama: Yémofem yémndayénd, “Yéné árom elimnde ár Ngánján wáwefnoghét yéné ende téfene nák eráfne.”
english: saying , “ This man persuades men to worship God contrary to the law .”

nama: Fol siotro témorwén, Galio norayai Ju árfefaf, “Fém Ju ár njam nawatarnati yéné kértamén tayot mer nérete, fém so eyáretan sian.
english: But when Paul was about to open his mouth , Gallio said to the Jews , “ If indeed it were a matter of wrong or of wicked crime , you Jews , it would be reasonable that I should bear with you ;

nama: Wénde yéna ewafnyang ámogh ménamén orayaghmén a yétkwénmén a féfenjo nákmén, táfrendangi yéné yam fémofemyo. Yénd yau so yéna wém féfene moghan yéné yammén.”
english: but if they are questions about words and names and your own law , look to it yourselves . For I don’ t want to be a judge of these matters .”

nama: Féyo yémon engéutoryéng moghta.
english: So he drove them from the judgment seat.

nama: Féyo méinyotioyam yerayénd Sostenes Ju árfene áumengégh méngo limánégh ároefaf ngi yafngoi mogh méngo safén. Wénde Galiom yau kemnau yénémamén. Frisila, Akwila a Afolos
english: Then all the Greeks seized Sosthenes , the ruler of the synagogue, and beat him before the judgment seat. Gallio didn’ t care about any of these things .

nama: Fol yémorangérmén Korintén tárfár efogh. Féyo yémon engéfrangoi fútaréf kété, a nuwanoyénd yémafé Frisila a yánjar Akwila. Fá néngwanoyénd kétán Senséri. Kété Fol séf natértam ménamén yane unyéghofnar simén Ngánjánefaf. Fá ndimbal boute nénguwanoyénd Siria yuwot.
english: Paul , having stayed after this many more days , took his leave of the brothers, and sailed from there for Syria , together with Priscilla and Aquila. He shaved his head in Cenchreae, for he had a vow .

nama: Fá nékmboyénd Efesos tesenan, Folém efrangeyai Frisila a Akwila. Fá yambia néngwanoi Ju árfene áumengégh méngot a tarundawét Ju árfé.
english: He came to Ephesus , and he left them there ; but he himself entered into the synagogue and reasoned with the Jews .

nama: Njam yémofem yaunendayénd ámb efogh ámnjoghét yémafé, yémon yékenai.
english: When they asked him to stay with them a longer time , he declined;

nama: Wénde njam Folém engéfrangoi, yémon unyéghofnar si emndai, “Yénd so kénangon Ngánjánene méndeyan.” Féyo yémon yéfrangoi Efesos a mé tanmorwén Kaisariat.
english: but taking his leave of them , he said , “ I must by all means keep this coming feast in Jerusalem, but I will return again to you if God wills .” Then he set sail from Ephesus .

nama: Njam fá nufaryéng Kaisaria yuwot, fá néngwanoi Jerusalemét a áuwégh si ewaramai Yesuene mérnat, a néngangoi kétán Antiok yuwot.
english: When he had landed at Caesarea , he went up and greeted the assembly , and went down to Antioch.

nama: Ámb efogh ámnjogh soramé, Antiokta kétamé Fol néngwanoi yéné yuwomé karendau Galasia yu a Frigia yu, muyé tawarametau méinyotio waitogh árét. Folene nambiomén áligh (Acts 18.23—21.17)|src="nmx_215wbt - Paul journey 3 [bwf-light] @en_v4.tif" size="col" copy="© 2025 Wycliffe Bible Translators, Inc." ref="18.23—21.17"
english: Having spent some time there , he departed and went through the region of Galatia and Phrygia , in order , establishing all the disciples .

nama: Jumén ár yétkwén Afolos Aleksandria yumén yénmormén Efesos. Fá témorwén Buk si matyamafé.
english: Now a certain Jew named Apollos, an Alexandrian by race , an eloquent man , came to Ephesus . He was mighty in the Scriptures .

nama: Fá yatametaménd Yurane ende, korayau tikéf fésafé a tawatametau Yesuenemén merkéráye, wénde fá mat témorwén Jonene nu walitoghménro.
english: This man had been instructed in the way of the Lord ; and being fervent in spirit , he spoke and taught accurately the things concerning Jesus , although he knew only the baptism of John .

nama: Yémon si yafngoi férférofnaye Ju árfene áumengégh méngo kénjún. Njam Frisila a Akwilafem yiyáreyayénd fá, yémofem yumyeyayénd kétán yéfenjo méngot a tawaferwét Afolose mbarkánde Ngánjánene end.
english: He began to speak boldly in the synagogue . But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him , they took him aside , and explained to him the way of God more accurately.

nama: Njam Afolos ménde témorwén Akaya ndimbal yuwot álighét, fútarém yawéngsotaménd fá a yéfarotaménd férén waitogh árfefaf Akaya ndimbal yuwon yá umyoghét. Yáne ufarghan, fá náyátoi ndimbal wáutárégh yéfeyot emofem efalo yiyátoyénd ménaye Ngánjánene ngáráme.
english: When he had determined to pass over into Achaia, the brothers encouraged him ; and wrote to the disciples to receive him . When he had come , he greatly helped those who had believed through grace ;

nama: Fá kéwitawét marataye Ju árfé, yémon yéta safét tawakmetau Buk sita ndené Yesu témorwén Keriso.
english: for he powerfully refuted the Jews , publicly showing by the Scriptures that Jesus was the Christ .

nama: Njam Afolos témorwén Korint tesenan, Fol tiotau mband tunamé kétán Efesos tesen néngufaryéng. Kété yémon ewinjoi ámb waitogh ár
english: While Apollos was at Corinth , Paul , having passed through the upper country , came to Ephesus and found certain disciples .

nama: a yémon emotai, “Fémofem Ngánján Mbérmbér té yanayénd njam fémofem efalo yiyátoyénd Yesu?” Yémofem yawangoyénd, “Yau, yénd yau náyárayém ndené yéna yém Ngánján Mbérmbér.”
english: He said to them , “ Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed ?” They said to him , “ No , we haven’ t even heard that there is a Holy Spirit .”

nama: Féyo Folém emotai, “Endene walitogh fémofem yanayénd?” Yémofem yawangoyénd, “Jonene nu walitogh.”
english: He said , “ Into what then were you baptized?” They said , “ Into John ’s baptism.”

nama: Folém norayai, “Jonene nu walitogh yémormén yaufita awengoghét. Joném tamndetau ár efalo yátoghét Yesu efe so yéném yá soramé.”
english: Paul said , “ John indeed baptized with the baptism of repentance , saying to the people that they should believe in the one who would come after him , that is , in Christ Jesus .”

nama: Yéné si áyárghan, Folém ewalitotam Yuré Yesuene yétkwénan.
english: When they heard this , they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus .

nama: Njam Folém ngángé efayoi yéfefaf, Ngánján Mbérmbér táf yénmormén yéfefaf, a ámb sie korayawét a táitotawét Ngánjánene si.
english: When Paul had laid his hands on them , the Holy Spirit came on them and they spoke with other languages and prophesied .

nama: Kété mété tamorwén 12 ár.
english: They were about twelve men in all .

nama: Fol nénglawai Ju árfene áumengégh méngot a férférofnar korayau kété nambio ghérare. Yémo taruindau yémafé a yéta taméindau ménát sái efalo tiyátongayénd Ngánján limánéghmén.
english: He entered into the synagogue and spoke boldly for a period of three months , reasoning and persuading about the things concerning God ’s Kingdom.

nama: Wénde ámb ár namndayénd tikéf táikér; fá kakenawét efalo yátoghét a marataye yaufi si korayawét Ngánjánene endmén. Féyo yéné yammén Folém efrangoi fá. Yémon engwasroi waitogh árfé téngwaruindawét si méinyotio efghon yétkwén Taranusoene watamegh méngon.
english: But when some were hardened and disobedient, speaking evil of the Way before the multitude , he departed from them and separated the disciples , reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus .

nama: Fá yérsam sómbio yunjéf sian, féyo méinyotio Ju ár a Téndo ár Esia kénjún efe tamorwén náyárayénd Yurane si.
english: This continued for two years , so that all those who lived in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus , both Jews and Greeks .

nama: Ngánjánam yéramai Fole wafté sélngwélafé ásáfoghét.
english: God worked special miracles by the hands of Paul ,

nama: Árém muku o sáláme Folém árké tiyánjangai tafayotawét enjne téfnárefaf, táf mer káyátotawét a yaufi mbérmbér kitérmbotawét.
english: so that even handkerchiefs or aprons were carried away from his body to the sick, and the diseases departed from them , and the evil spirits went out .

nama: Ámb Ju ár nuwanoyénd yéta yaufi mbérmbér tawifnawét Yuré Yesuene yétkwénan fá efe yaufi mbérmbéram tawasérmbotau. Ámb árém norayai, “Yesuene yétkwénan, endenemén Folém yaitote, yéndo némndetan, 'Kéntor.'”
english: But some of the itinerant Jews , exorcists , took on themselves to invoke over those who had the evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus , saying , “ We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preaches .”

nama: Jumén limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár Skefane sefen ambumam yéné ásáfogh tafrotawét.
english: There were seven sons of one Sceva , a Jewish chief priest, who did this .

nama: Ámb efghon yaufi mbérmbéram ewawangoi yéfe, “Yénd mat wém Yesu a yénd mat wém Folenemén, wénde fém efe em?”
english: The evil spirit answered , “ Jesus I know , and Paul I know , but who are you ?”

nama: Féyo yéné yaufi mbérmbérafé ár néngatnam yéfefaf a ewifnai méinyotio tásmetau. Fá méngota néngenngoyénd firo kérsawét férkafé.
english: The man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them , overpowered them , and prevailed against them , so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded .

nama: Kété Efesosén njam Ju ár a Téndo árém náyárayénd yénémamén, fá férfér tamorwén, yémofem tuwetawét Yuré Yesuene yétkwén.
english: This became known to all , both Jews and Greeks , who lived at Ephesus . Fear fell on them all , and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified .

nama: Tárfár efe efalo yátogh ár náyátoyénd afrogh nufngoyénd yéfenjo yaufi wafroghmén.
english: Many also of those who had believed came , confessing and declaring their deeds .

nama: Ámb ár efe kotametawét ghar yémofem tanérsawét yéfenjo ghar buk a tawarotawét maratan. Njam yémofem taftawét árfene bukmén fai kétán narsam tárfár milion kinat.
english: Many of those who practiced magical arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all . They counted their price , and found it to be fifty thousand pieces of silver.

nama: Féyo táf Yurane si marat káyátotau a kamndetau waftafé. Safén warogh ghar si fefé (Acts 19.19)|src="CN02003B.tif" size="col" copy="© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission." ref="19.19"
english: So the word of the Lord was growing and becoming mighty .

nama: Méinyotio yéné yam njam kufrotau soramé, Folém mwighé yaitotam yafoghét Makedonia a Akaya a Jerusalemét. Fol norayai, “Yénandmé yénd so kuwanon kétán Rom.”
english: Now after these things had ended , Paul determined in the Spirit , when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem , saying , “ After I have been there , I must also see Rome .”

nama: Féyo yémon ewáfáreméng yáne sómbio wáutárégh ár, Timoti a Erastus, kétán Makedoniat, wénde fá mé témorangérwén Esian. Kérté Efesos kénjún
english: Having sent into Macedonia two of those who served him , Timothy and Erastus , he himself stayed in Asia for a while .

nama: Yéné efghon Efesos yuwon kérté yéfunayénd Ngánjánene Endmén.
english: About that time there arose no small disturbance concerning the Way .

nama: Kété témorwén ár yétkwén Demetrios, emo tawafrotau silfé wáwefnogh méngo Artemis mewangoe. Fá efe kásáfotawét yémofem tawinjotawét tárfár mani.
english: For a certain man named Demetrius , a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the craftsmen ,

nama: Demetriosém eumendai yánjo ásáfogh ár a tamndangé, “Fútaréf, káyárangi, fém mat em yéndfem tawinjotam tárfár mani yéné ásáfoghmén.
english: whom he gathered together with the workmen of like occupation , and said , “Sirs, you know that by this business we have our wealth .

nama: Fémofem té yinjongi a yáyamngongi ndernáye Folém áfrat yényátote a ámb watameghe tárfár ár elimnde Efesos tesenan a méinyotio Esia yuwomé. Yémon noraye ndené ár wafroghmén ngánján yau em ngánján.
english: You see and hear that not at Ephesus alone , but almost throughout all Asia , this Paul has persuaded and turned away many people , saying that they are no gods that are made with hands .
