nama: Yéné yam siyém áuwéghofnar yém ménamén téfene mani winjogh end so kefongé. Ndenéyameyo ndimbal amaf ngánján Atemisene wáwefnogh méngo so kamndangé fifiofnar, endeyot táwefnotat Esia a mband maratan. Árém yau so nemnat ndené amaf ngánján Atemis siyém ngánján.”
english: Not only is there danger that this our trade come into disrepute, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be counted as nothing and her majesty destroyed , whom all Asia and the world worships.”

nama: Njam fá náyárayénd yéné si, fá nékw tamorwén a ke kutnawét ndené siafé, “Ndimbal ngánján amaf siyém Atemis Efesosén.”
english: When they heard this they were filled with anger , and cried out , saying , “ Great is Artemis of the Ephesians !”

nama: Ndimbal tesen nufnyai a ke kutnawét ménamén ámbiro mwighayan tamorwén. Árém ewanetangeyayénd Gayus a Aristakus, Folene arengégh fútaréf kétamé Makedonia yuta, a árém tawafarewét kétán áumengégh sifayat.
english: The whole city was filled with confusion , and they rushed with one accord into the theater , having seized Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia , Paul ’s companions in travel .

nama: Fol ménde témorwén álighét áumengégh sifayat, wénde waitogh árém yau yéngéumyoyénd kétán álighét.
english: When Paul wanted to enter in to the people , the disciples didn’ t allow him .

nama: Ámb gafanane ásáfogh ár efe tamorwén Folene fútar, si wékeye yénmawatembayénd yau álighét kétán áumengégh sifayat.
english: Certain also of the Asiarchs, being his friends , sent to him and begged him not to venture into the theater .

nama: Ár mérén kéwérsotawét ménamén fénatayero ke kutnawét. Tárfár ár yau mat tamorwén némamén fá kété tamorwén.
english: Some therefore cried one thing , and some another , for the assembly was in confusion . Most of them didn’ t know why they had come together .

nama: Ju árém yéngwáfáretayénd Aleksenda yéfefaf siot. Yémon árét ngángaye ewaunyai métar ekmboghét. Fá ménde témorwén ménát sái tamndangai fá ndené Ju ár yau kérté yéfunayénd.
english: They brought Alexander out of the multitude , the Jews putting him forward . Alexander beckoned with his hand , and would have made a defense to the people .

nama: Wénde njam yémofem yáyámngoyénd nde fá témorwén Ju ár, fá kutnawét sómbio efoghféneféne ndené keyafé, “Ndimbal yém Atemis Efesosménǃ”
english: But when they perceived that he was a Jew , all with one voice for a time of about two hours cried out , “ Great is Artemis of the Ephesians !”

nama: Tesenmén gafanane ásáfogh árom ekmboi a norayai, “Efesosmén ár, fém mat em ndené Efesos tesen siyém wanjengégh ár ndimbal amaf ngánján Artamisene wáwefnogh méngo a yéné amafoene sáf mbanmban árké néneworyéng njafarta.
english: When the town clerk had quieted the multitude , he said , “ You men of Ephesus , what man is there who doesn’ t know that the city of the Ephesians is temple keeper of the great goddess Artemis, and of the image which fell down from Zeus?

nama: Efe yau so korayangé fá yau nénui njafarta. Fém métar. Némé yau yafrotati namname.
english: Seeing then that these things can’t be denied, you ought to be quiet and to do nothing rash .

nama: Fémofem yéné ár tánémnjati yénan, yémofem yau yaufi si korayat téfene ngánjánefaf a yau némndafi oroe tarsat téfene ngánjánene wáwefnogh méngota.
english: For you have brought these men here , who are neither robbers of temples nor blasphemers of your goddess .

nama: Féyo njam Demetrios a yáne ásáfogh árfé kérté ewam, mogh siyém sauréghkaf yém a yéna em mogh ár. Féyotaro fá so norayat yéné yammén.
english: If therefore Demetrius and the craftsmen who are with him have a matter against anyone , the courts are open and there are proconsuls. Let them press charges against one another .

nama: Njam kérté mé em, so tanwanangi kétán moghfaf.
english: But if you seek anything about other matters , it will be settled in the regular assembly .

nama: A féyo mat ayéném yéné, ndené njam sénko ár káyárangi némé yéndfem tafrotam sénonjo, yémofem mété yénémndetat ndimbal kérté funaghmén. Yéndfem yéné tafrotam fifiofnar, féyo némé yénd so norayam njam yénd yénmotat yénémamén.”
english: For indeed we are in danger of being accused concerning today ’s riot , there being no cause . Concerning it , we wouldn’ t be able to give an account of this commotion.”

nama: Féyo yéné si soramé, yémon ár ewáfárnayéng.
english: When he had thus spoken , he dismissed the assembly .

nama: Njam tesenan sarake nefoi, Fol néngwanoi kétán waitogh árfefaf, a tawawéngsotau yéfe. Féyo fá emndayéng “Yao” a táf néwanoi Makedonia yuwot.
english: After the uproar had ceased , Paul sent for the disciples , took leave of them , and departed to go into Macedonia .

nama: Fá ngrendau yéné yuwomé, ngorayau tárfár wawéngsogh si árfefaf soramé Fol néngufaryéng Grik yuwot,
english: When he had gone through those parts and had encouraged them with many words , he came into Greece .

nama: kété fá yémorangérmén nambio ghérare. Féyo mwighé yaitotam Siria yuwot angoghét ndimbal boute, wénde fá náyárai ndené ámb Ju árém yá ngiot mwighé táitotawét. Féyo yémon mwighé yaitotam tunamé angoghét Makedoniamé.
english: When he had spent three months there , and a plot was made against him by Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria , he determined to return through Macedonia .

nama: Sofata Firusene ambum kétándmamén Bereya yumén, Aristakus a Sekundus kétándmamén Tesalonika yumén, Gaius kétándmamén Derbe yumén, Timoti ndenéyameyo, Tukikus a Trofimus kétándmamén Esia marat yumén yémafé Folfé tayotau.
english: These accompanied him as far as Asia: Sopater of Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians , Gaius of Derbe , Timothy , and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.

nama: Yéné ár engmormén frondeyo ténmanjetawét yénd Troas tesenan.
english: But these had gone ahead , and were waiting for us at Troas .

nama: Wénde yénd ndimbal boute ténngmorwén kétamé Filifi tesenta Minaghofnar flawé téménd soramé. Ámb 5 efogh soramé yénd nufnyayém kété Troas tesenan, ánde yénd yémorangérmén sefen efogh. Yutikus kérta nanoi Troas tesenan
english: We sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and came to them at Troas in five days , where we stayed seven days .

nama: Sande efghon yénd yénngmormén mérne ménát féskaf flawé nénet. Folém korayau árfefaf a sian mé téngmorwén kétánotio sémbár néndkéf, ménamén ámb efghon álighét karametawét.
english: On the first day of the week , when the disciples were gathered together to break bread, Paul talked with them , intending to depart on the next day ; and continued his speech until midnight .

nama: Yéné méngo kénjún kété kánetawét tárfár kwayaghé ánde yénd káumendawém.
english: There were many lights in the upper room where we were gathered together .

nama: Kété ámb totér ár yétkwén Yutikus témorangérwén mbormbor sésafneyan, njamandio Fol mé korayau teke, Yutikus kémégham térametau. Kerkeran, fá kémégh nakmai nambio kitaré satayan kétándmé nénuyai mbandot. Yémofem yékafayénd kérwén.
english: A certain young man named Eutychus sat in the window , weighed down with deep sleep . As Paul spoke still longer , being weighed down by his sleep , he fell down from the third floor and was taken up dead .

nama: Fol néngeworyéng yuwot, néngáwefnoi ngángé efayoi yáfaf. Féyo Folém tamndangé, “Yau mámé mwighé em, fá mé ghérsé yémǃ”
english: Paul went down and fell upon him , and embracing him said , “Don’ t be troubled , for his life is in him .”

nama: Féyo Fol wéikor néngarmboi tukét, a ténetau féskaf flawé. Fol mé korayau kétánotio akw yéfawéryéng soramé yémo efrangoi.
english: When he had gone up , had broken bread and eaten , and had talked with them a long while , even until break of day , he departed .

nama: Árém totér ár yéngwanayénd méngot a yéfeyot ndimbal áuwéghafé témorwén ménamén kérta nanoi. Folfé nafángoyénd Efesos sénko árfé
english: They brought the boy in alive , and were greatly comforted .

nama: Fol té norayai ndené yémo so ténfángongai kété Asosén, ménamén fá tiyotau álighe. Féyo fronde yénd nénguwanoyém Boutfaf a ngérsawém kétán Asos tesenot. Kété yénd ménde ténmormén sái téfángongayém fá a téngwanangayém bout.
english: But we , going ahead to the ship , set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there ; for he had so arranged , intending himself to go by land .

nama: Njam yémon yénfángoyéng Asosén, yéndfem fá yéngwaflayém a néngwanoyém Mitilan tesenot.
english: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and came to Mitylene .

nama: Ámb efghon yéndfem yéfrangoyém yéné a néngwanoyém ndimbal boute kétán Kios tesenot. Yéné efogh soramé wéi néngurtoyém Samos tesenot, sómbio efogh soramé néngufaryém Miletus tesenot.
english: Sailing from there , we came the following day opposite Chios . The next day we touched at Samos and stayed at Trogyllium, and the day after we came to Miletus .

nama: Fol yau ménde témorwén ménát sái témorangérwén Esia marat yuwon, féyo mwighé yéfaronam yau so kékmbongai Efesosén. Ménamén fá ménde témorwén wékeye ufarghét Jerusalemét wafarogh téménd yétkwén Fentikost
english: For Paul had determined to sail past Ephesus , that he might not have to spend time in Asia ; for he was hastening , if it were possible for him , to be in Jerusalem on the day of Pentecost .

nama: Kétándmé Miletus tesenta, Folém si yatembai kétán Efesos yumén Yesuene mérén sénko árét álighét yémafé siot.
english: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus and called to himself the elders of the assembly .

nama: Njam sénko ár nufaryénd, Fol norayai yéfefaf, “Fém mat em ndernáye yénd kásáfotan féfe kénjún kétándmayo frondefifi efghon yénd njam nufaryén Esia marat yuwon.
english: When they had come to him , he said to them , “ You yourselves know , from the first day that I set foot in Asia , how I was with you all the time ,

nama: Yénd kásáfotan ndimbal yuwan mwighaye a yengamafé. Yénd tékrátan kawamarotan ménamén Ju árém ngiot kwérametawét.
english: serving the Lord with all humility , with many tears , and with trials which happened to me by the plots of the Jews ;

nama: Fém mat em ndené yénd yau férfér kwém si waitoghét árkéméné féfe wáutárghafé tém wénde tawatametawén fém marataye méinyotio méngomé.
english: how I didn’ t shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable , teaching you publicly and from house to house ,

nama: Yéndon táitotawén si yéfe Ju ár a Grikmén árét ménát fá so nawengotat yaufita Ngánjánefaf a efalo so yiyátotat téfene Yuré Yesu.
english: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks repentance toward God and faith toward our Lord Jesus .

nama: “Sénonjo Ngánján Mbérmbéroene sian, yénd so kuwanon Jerusalemét, mat yau wém némé yam so nufrote kété tafaf.
english: Now , behold , I go bound by the Spirit to Jerusalem , not knowing what will happen to me there ;

nama: Wénde yénd yénaro mat wém méinyotio tesenan Ngánján Mbérmbéram waufrote ndené Jerusalemén árém so kwényawangi yénd kwéfkwéf méngon a yénd so tékratan kwékmangi.
english: except that the Holy Spirit testifies in every city , saying that bonds and afflictions wait for me .

nama: Wénde tane fifie ghérsé siyém fifiofnar yém, wénde yéné fifiyafé tafaf yém: ndené yéndo so tifon wérse Yuré Yesum árké waramai, mer si waitogh ásáfoghét Ngánjánene ngárámmén
english: But these things don’ t count; nor do I hold my life dear to myself , so that I may finish my race with joy , and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus , to fully testify to the Good News of the grace of God .

nama: “Yénd mat wém féfefafmé té karendan Ngánján limánéghmén si táitotan. Wénde sénonjo yénd mat wém ndené fémofem yau so wéikor kwinjongi.
english: “ Now , behold , I know that you all , among whom I went about preaching God ’s Kingdom, will see my face no more .

nama: Yénémamén sénonjo yénd sotafé si norayan. Njam fém ámb kér ném Yesu efalo yátoghofnar, fémofem yau so kwéfayongi yénd yénémamén.
english: Therefore I testify to you today that I am clean from the blood of all men ,

nama: Yénd yau férfér kwém méinyotio Ngánjánene ménde waitoghét féfefaf.
english: for I didn’ t shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God .

nama: Fém kor nawarawenati fémyo a méinyotio Yesuene mérén árké Ngánján Mbérmbéram té ewaferyéng ár wanjengét. Ngánjánene Yesuene mérén wanjengégh ár aem, yémon yánjo férke té yénmanai.
english: Take heed , therefore , to yourselves and to all the flock , in which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers , to shepherd the assembly of the Lord and God which he purchased with his own blood.

nama: Yénd ndené mat wém, yénd njam kuwanon munjan ághé so kufarngi féfene kénjúnyo a yau so ewarawenat wénde so esmetat krot.
english: For I know that after my departure, vicious wolves will enter in among you , not sparing the flock .

nama: Yénémamén féfenjo mérén kénjúmén ár so kénokayongi a elimndat efalo yátogh ár ménát so ewaitotat yéfene unyégh watamegh.
english: Men will arise from among your own selves , speaking perverse things , to draw away the disciples after them .

nama: Aya kor fém nawarawenatiǃ Té nawafarotati ndené nambio yunjéf yéndon tawawéngsotawén ekmboghofnar fém ámbiroámbiroe yengamafé méinyotio efogh a sémbár.
english: Therefore watch , remembering that for a period of three years I didn’t cease to admonish everyone night and day with tears .

nama: “Yénémamén sénonjo yéndon ewatetan fém kétán Ngánjánefaf a kétán ngárám sifaf, árké siam fém so ewanote a efrende méinyotio efe té efaloi yandio.
english: Now , brothers, I entrust you to God and to the word of his grace , which is able to build up and to give you the inheritance among all those who are sanctified .

nama: Yéndon yau féfene sáláme o mani mwighaye takwnan tayot.
english: I coveted no one ’s silver, gold , or clothing .

nama: Fém méinyo mat em ndené yénd tanjo ngángaye kásáfotawén tanjo sálámet a maniot a ndenéyameyo tane fútarat.
english: You yourselves know that these hands served my necessities , and those who were with me .

nama: Yéné ende yéndo té tawauyafotan ndernáye fém so emor. Fém so násáfotati táikrataye ménát yauyau ár so ewáutánati, so nawafarotam Yuré Yesuene si fá árké korayau: 'Ramégh ároene áuwégham yáinde yáne áuwégh emo rokar tanangé.'”
english: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus , that he himself said , ‘ It is more blessed to give than to receive .’ ”

nama: Njam Folém si ewifoi, fá méinyotio budme neitaménd Ngánjánafé orayéghét.
english: When he had spoken these things , he knelt down and prayed with them all .

nama: Féyo táf yémofem yengamafé yambnayénd a Fol tésnetawét.
english: They all wept freely , and fell on Paul ’s neck and kissed him ,

nama: Fá áuwéghofnar tamorwén ménamén Fol árké norayai ndené ámb káye tane sáf yau so kor yéfandati. Féyo yémofem engémnjoyénd ndimbal boutfaf.
english: sorrowing most of all because of the word which he had spoken , that they should see his face no more . Then they accompanied him to the ship .

nama: Yéndfem yao si engwaramayém yéfeyot, yéné soramé yénd boutan nénguflayém a tényotau kétán Kos férot. Ámb efghon yénd nénguflayém kétán Rodas férot a kétándmé kétán Fatara yuwot.
english: When we had departed from them and had set sail, we came with a straight course to Cos , and the next day to Rhodes , and from there to Patara .

nama: Yéndfem ndimbal bout yinjoyém kéférmetau kétán Fonisia yuwot, wéi nénguflayém ténngmorwén.
english: Having found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia , we went aboard and set sail.

nama: Soramé Saifrus yu yéngwinjoyém, yéngwaindayém náikáita kétán Siria yuwot. Yénd nurtoyém Taire yuwon, kété árém turtotawét rokar ndimbal boutta.
english: When we had come in sight of Cyprus , leaving it on the left hand , we sailed to Syria and landed at Tyre , for the ship was there to unload her cargo .

nama: Yesuene waitogh ár ewinjoyém kété, yémafé yénmorangérmén sefen efogh. Ngánján Mbérmbér waftayan yémofem yémndayénd Fol yau álighét kétán Jerusalemét.
english: Having found disciples , we stayed there seven days . These said to Paul through the Spirit that he should not go up to Jerusalem .

nama: Efogh njam nawerai frangoghét, yéndfem yéfrangoyém a néngwanoyém boutfaf mérne méinyotio waitogh ár a yéfene amaf a ambumtértér tesenta. Kété arakaf téndon yénd budme niayém Ngánjánafé korayawém.
english: When those days were over , we departed and went on our journey . They all , with wives and children , brought us on our way until we were out of the city . Kneeling down on the beach, we prayed .

nama: Yao si soramé norayayénd yéfendio, yénd nénguflayém ndimbal boutan, a fá nénangoyénd méngot.
english: After saying goodbye to each other , we went on board the ship , and they returned home again .

nama: Yéné soramé yéndfem áligh yéngwasamngoyém kétándmé Taireta a néngamghayém Tolemais tesen. Kété yénd áuwéghafé nawinjoyém fútarafé kété yénmorangérmén ámbiro efogh.
english: When we had finished the voyage from Tyre , we arrived at Ptolemais . We greeted the brothers and stayed with them one day .

nama: Ámb efghon yénd néngufaryém Kaisaria tesen a yénmorangérmén Filifene méngon. Yéné Filif siyém si waitogh ár a ámb sefenmén efe yékafayénd ménát kwiré amafat wáutáréghét.
english: On the next day , we who were Paul ’s companions departed and came to Caesarea . We entered into the house of Philip the evangelist , who was one of the seven , and stayed with him .

nama: Yáne awerghofnar mérés tamorwén fo emofem Ngánjánene si táitotawét.
english: Now this man had four virgin daughters who prophesied .

nama: Yénd kété yénmorangérmén tárfár efogh, Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár yétkwén Agabus yénmormén kétándmé Judiata.
english: As we stayed there some days , a certain prophet named Agabus came down from Judea .

nama: Téfefaf nénaflai, yémon tékafangé Folene beles. Féyo yémo yánjo ngángé a kafkaf eumbai yéné siafé, “Ngánján Mbérmbér norayai, 'Yéné ende Jerusalemén Ju árém so yumbetat yéné beles yuré a so téramangi Téndo árfene ngángat.'”
english: Coming to us and taking Paul ’s belt , he bound his own feet and hands , and said , “ The Holy Spirit says : ‘ So the Jews at Jerusalem will bind the man who owns this belt , and will deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles .’”

nama: Yénd njam náyárayém yéné si, yénd a ámb ár norayayém Fol, “Fém yau so niota Jerusalemét.”
english: When we heard these things , both we and the people of that place begged him not to go up to Jerusalem .

nama: Féyo Folém yénmawangoi, “Némamén fém ye nendati tane tikéf karéfkaréf yafrotati? Yénd wém afrengéghkaf umbghét a kérot Jerusalemén Yuré Yesuene yétkwénot.”
english: Then Paul answered , “ What are you doing , weeping and breaking my heart ? For I am ready not only to be bound , but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus .”

nama: Yéndfem ndernáye ende yau so áfrat kényátongém, féyo yénd nékmboyém a norayayém, “Yurane méndero so nufrote.”
english: When he would not be persuaded , we ceased , saying , “ The Lord ’ s will be done .”

nama: Yéné soramé, yénd nafrendamém a néngwanoyém Jerusalemét.
english: After these days we took up our baggage and went up to Jerusalem .

nama: Ámb waitogh ár kétamamén Kaisaria yénngmaitoyénd a yénngémnjoyénd kétán Nasonene méngot ánde yénd so ténmorangérwén kété. Fá témorwén Saifrusmén ár a ámb fronde waitogh ár. Fol nufaryéng Jerusalemét
english: Some of the disciples from Caesarea also went with us , bringing one Mnason of Cyprus , an early disciple , with whom we would stay.

nama: Njam yénd néngufaryém Jerusalemét, efalo yátogh árém yénmumyoyénd yénd áuwéghafé.
english: When we had come to Jerusalem , the brothers received us gladly.

nama: Ámb efghon Fol a ámb ár néngaflayém kétán Jemsefaf, méinyotio sénko ár kété tamorwén.
english: The day following , Paul went in with us to James ; and all the elders were present .

nama: Folém áuwégh si ewaramai a tawaufrotau méinyotio yam némé Ngánjánam tafrotau Téndo árfefaf yáne ásáfoghan.
english: When he had greeted them , he reported one by one the things which God had worked among the Gentiles through his ministry .

nama: Yémofem njam káyáretawét yéné si, yémofem Ngánján tuwetawét. Féyo yémofem korayangi Folefaf, “Fútar, téfanda nderé tárfár 1,000 Ju árém efalo yiyátoyénd, yémofem méinyotio sote mé táitotawét nák.
english: They , when they heard it , glorified God . They said to him , “ You see , brother, how many thousands there are among the Jews of those who have believed , and they are all zealous for the law .

nama: Yéné Ju ár té náyárangi fémon némé taitote Ju árfefaf efe tamorangérwén Téndo árfefafmé. Fá ndené kemnawét fémo kar tamndetau, 'Fém yau táf náyáretati Mosesene nákfaf ndenayu. Fémofem yau táf ghéngan enfetati féfene ambumtértér. Fém yau so emor ndené ende Ju ár kés emor.'
english: They have been informed about you , that you teach all the Jews who are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses , telling them not to circumcise their children and not to walk after the customs .

nama: Némé yéndfem so yafrotam? Fá so káyárangi ndené fém té kéném.
english: What then ? The assembly must certainly meet , for they will hear that you have come .

nama: Féyo tafrota némé yéndfem némndetam. Yéna em yéndfé fo Ju efalo yátogh ár emofem unyéghofnar si norayayénd Ngánjánefaf.
english: Therefore do what we tell you . We have four men who have taken a vow .

nama: Tangémnjongi yéné ár wáwefnogh méngot, so ngufnyangi yéfenjo wányányégh téméndfaf a so fai nufrotat ghéngén raméghmén. Yéné soramé yémofem so yéfenjo sénko séf ewérnat. Féyo méinyotio ár mat so em kété siyém efalo si yau yényau afrogh sian fenemén, wénde fém fémyo némor ndernáye nák noraye.
english: Take them and purify yourself with them , and pay their expenses for them , that they may shave their heads . Then all will know that there is no truth in the things that they have been informed about you , but that you yourself also walk keeping the law .

nama: Ndenaye téndo efalo yátogh árfenemén, yéndfem téfarotam yéfe téfene mwighé ndené yémofem yau táf enetat mewang njamke, a férék, a férék eréghofnar wérár, ndenéyameyo téndo wal.”
english: But concerning the Gentiles who believe , we have written our decision that they should observe no such thing , except that they should keep themselves from food offered to idols , from blood, from strangled things , and from sexual immorality .”

nama: Ámb efghon Foléf engmormén ámb árfé a yémofem yafrotaménd yéné téméndmén yam ndernáye témorwén wányányéghmén. Yéné soramé, fá néngwanoi wáwefnogh méngo sifayat a tamndetau ghéngén wárogh ár, “Kétán árké efghot so em sái kénamndangi njúnnjúnofnar yam so kefongé, yéndfem so enérsam ghéngén yénat.” Fol yerayénd
english: Then Paul took the men , and the next day purified himself and went with them into the temple , declaring the fulfillment of the days of purification, until the offering was offered for every one of them .

nama: Njam sefen efogh awaingétro tamorwén, ámb Ju ár kétándmé enmormén Esia marat yuta Fol yinjoyénd wáwefnogh méngo sifayan. Fá nékwafé naindaménd ár mérén eumendayénd a Fol yerayénd.
english: When the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia , when they saw him in the temple , stirred up all the multitude and laid hands on him ,

nama: A fá korayawét ndené, “Israil ár, ténmáutánangiǃ Yéné ár fiyém emo ewatamete méinyotio ár méinyotio yuwomé téfene ár ewasérmbote a téfene nák a yéné wáwefnogh méngo. Yémon enwasroi Grikmén ár wáwefnogh méngot a taghnawét yéné njúnnjúnofnar sifayé.”
english: crying out , “ Men of Israel , help! This is the man who teaches all men everywhere against the people , and the law , and this place . Moreover , he also brought Greeks into the temple and has defiled this holy place !”

nama: Méné téfnan fá norayayénd ndené ende ménamén yémofem té ewinjeyayénd Téndo ár Trofimus Efesosmén Folfé tesenan a fá nemnayénd ndené Folém ténémnje wáwefnogh méngot.
english: For they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city , and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple .

nama: Méinyotio tesen ár néngenngoyénd wéla end kafemé. Kété Fol yerayénd, yémofem téngroghawét kétándmé wáwefnogh méngota. Tekofnaye wáwefnogh méngo sésafne emnayénd.
english: All the city was moved and the people ran together . They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple . Immediately the doors were shut .

nama: Yémofem ngiot yafngoyénd Fol njam Rom fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom náyárai ndené méinyotio Jerusalem tesen sarakeyan témorwén.
english: As they were trying to kill him , news came up to the commanding officer of the regiment that all Jerusalem was in an uproar .

nama: Yémon táf fété ámb fiyaré sénko ár a fiyaré ewerai a néngenngoyénd kétán ár mérnafefaf. Njam ngi téfnáram yinjoyénd fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár a yáne fiyaré, fá nékmboyénd Fole ngita.
english: Immediately he took soldiers and centurions and ran down to them . They , when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers , stopped beating Paul .

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár nénaflai a yerai Fol a ewáfáretai umbghét ménaye sómbio seine. Féyo fá namotai, “Efe fá yém a némé yémo tafrote?”
english: Then the commanding officer came near , arrested him , commanded him to be bound with two chains , and inquired who he was and what he had done .

nama: Ámb yéné mérén kénjún kutnawét, yémo tafrotau yéné yam a ámb fénatayam tafrote. Wénde ménamén fá ménjtaye kutnawét minde ke, fiyaré ndimbal sénko árém efalo si yau yékafai. Féyo yémo emndai, “Téngémnjongi yéné ár kétán fiyarafene sifayat!”
english: Some shouted one thing and some another , among the crowd . When he couldn’ t find out the truth because of the noise, he commanded him to be brought into the barracks .

nama: Njam Fol raté kaka namndai, ár mérén minde nékwafé nafghetaménd. Yénémamén fiyaram yuyoyénd ménát yambayénd.
english: When he came to the stairs, he was carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the crowd ;

nama: Ár mérén efe kuwaitotawét ménjtaye mé kutnawét, “Téngésmangiǃ Téngésmangiǃ” Fol korayau ár mérnafefaf
english: for the multitude of the people followed after , crying out , “Away with him !”

nama: Njam Fol ménjághétro témorwén kétán fiyarafene sifayat, yémo témotangé fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár, “Féyotaro yénd so korayangén ámb si fefaf?” Fiyarafene ndimbal sénko árom yémotai Fol, “O, fém mat ném Grik si?
english: As Paul was about to be brought into the barracks , he asked the commanding officer, “ May I speak to you ?” He said , “ Do you know Greek ?

nama: Fém yéné ném Ijifmén ár emo yérnyai awifarégh nambétio elimai 4,000 fiyaré kétán fanfne sifayat?”
english: Aren’ t you then the Egyptian who before these days stirred up to sedition and led out into the wilderness the four thousand men of the Assassins ?”

nama: Folém yawangoi, “Yau, yénd yau! yénd swém Ju ár, kété náráftamén Tarsus tesenan, árké yém mer marataye matkaf tesen Silisia kénjún. Féyotaro yénd so kwumyo yéné árfefaf siot.”
english: But Paul said , “ I am a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia , a citizen of no insignificant city . I beg you , allow me to speak to the people .”

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom yumyoi siyot, Fol nokayoi ratayan a ekmboi ár. Fá njam métar náyátoyénd, fá korayangé yéfefaf Ibru sie.
english: When he had given him permission , Paul , standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people . When there was a great silence , he spoke to them in the Hebrew language , saying ,

nama: “Fútaréf a afaf, kénáyárangi tane awamarogh sifaf.”
english: “Brothers and fathers , listen to the defense which I now make to you .”

nama: Njam yémofem yiyárayénd Fol korayau Ibru sie, fá métar namndayénd. Féyo Folém korayangé,
english: When they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language , they were even more quiet . He said ,

nama: “Yénd swém Ju ár, náráftamén Tarsus tesenan Silisia yuwon, wénde nanotamén Jerusalemén. Wémormén watametaménd afafene náke a Gamaliel siyém tane watamegh ár. Yénd ménde kwémorwén sái tiyáretawén Ngánján merkéráye ndené méinyotio fémofem sewafrotati sénonjo.
english: “ I am indeed a Jew , born in Tarsus of Cilicia , but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel , instructed according to the strict tradition of the law of our fathers , being zealous for God , even as you all are today .

nama: Yéndon tásmetawén ár kérotio efe kuwaitotawét yéné Endén, taweretawén ár a amaf a tangwitawén kwéfkwéf méngot.
english: I persecuted this Way to the death , binding and delivering into prisons both men and women ,

nama: Ghéngén wárogh sénko téfnár a méinyotio Ju sénko árfene áumengégh téfnáram so nafrotat yéné simén efalo yém. Yémofem kwaramangayénd férén Ju árfefaf Damaskos kénjún. Féyo yénd kwéngmorwén kétán yéné ár tánémnjawén ndené fété kwéfkwéf méngo ásáfogh ár kétán Jerusalemét kértayan kémghét.
english: as also the high priest and all the council of the elders testify , from whom also I received letters to the brothers, and traveled to Damascus to bring them also who were there to Jerusalem in bonds to be punished .

nama: “Ámb efogh sotan, njam kaka yéngyátoyén Damaskos, sélngwéle njafarmén ndimbal kwayaghayam wélálái yénd.
english: “ As I made my journey and came close to Damascus , about noon suddenly a great light shone around me from the sky .

nama: Yénd nuyáyén mbandot a náyárayén kém a norayai tafaf ndené, 'Solǃ Solǃ Fémo némamén yénd wésmete?'
english: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me , ‘ Saul , Saul , why are you persecuting me ?’

nama: “Yénd namotayén, 'Fém efe ném, Yuré?' “Yémon ta wawangoi, 'Yénd wém Yesu Najaretmén. Fémo wésmete.'
english: I answered , ‘ Who are you , Lord ?’ He said to me , ‘ I am Jesus of Nazareth , whom you persecute .’

nama: Tane mérnam yinjoyénd kwayaghé, wénde yémofem yau yáyámngoyénd yáne kém efe korayau tafaf.
english: “ Those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they didn’ t understand the voice of him who spoke to me .

nama: “Yéndon yémotayén, 'Némé so yéndon yafrotan, Yuré?' “Yuram kwémndetau, 'Kokai, kuwano kétán Damaskosét. Kété árém so kémaufrongé méinyotio fene ásáfogh némé so yém.'
english: I said , ‘ What shall I do , Lord ?’ The Lord said to me , ‘Arise, and go into Damascus . There you will be told about all things which are appointed for you to do .’

nama: Tane mérnam kwémnjawét Damaskos kénjút, ménamén ndimbal kwayaghayam ghékéf kwéfkwéf wiyátoi.
english: When I couldn’ t see for the glory of that light , being led by the hand of those who were with me , I came into Damascus .

nama: “Ár yétkwén Ananias yénmormén ta fandaghét. Fá témorwén Ngánján wáwefnogh ár a ndimbal kéráye tutémndawét Ju árém efe méinyotio kété tamorangérwén.
english: “ One Ananias, a devout man according to the law , well reported of by all the Jews who lived in Damascus,

nama: Fá nokayoi ta kaka korayangé, 'Fútar Sol, kéretaǃ' Táf fété néretayén yinjoyén Ananias.
english: came to me , and standing by me said to me , ‘Brother Saul , receive your sight !’ In that very hour I looked up at him .

nama: “Féyo Ananias norayai, 'Téfene ákifene Ngánjánam némafer fém ménát yáne mwighé mat fém so ném a ménát so tinjo mbarkánd ár a náyáreta si yáne mbírmbírta.
english: He said , ‘ The God of our fathers has appointed you to know his will , and to see the Righteous One , and to hear a voice from his mouth .

nama: Fém so ném yáne fandagh ár méinyotio árfefaf némé té téfandau a káyáretau.
english: For you will be a witness for him to all men of what you have seen and heard.

nama: Sénonjo némat fém nawanjete? Kokai, so némálitotat a Ngánján so tawalindangé fene yaufi táf njam Ngánjánafé oraighan ném Kerisoene yétkwénan.'
english: Now why do you wait? Arise, be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord .’

nama: “Yéné soramé yénd néngangoyén Jerusalemét a Ngánjánafé koraiwém wáwefnogh méngon. Yéndon yinjoyén ghékéf waingégh yam
english: “ When I had returned to Jerusalem and while I prayed in the temple , I fell into a trance

nama: a Yuram kwémndetau, 'Wékeye kuwano Jerusalem téfrango ménamén yémofem yau so tumyongi fene afrogh si tanemén.'
english: and saw him saying to me , ‘ Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly , because they will not receive testimony concerning me from you .’

nama: “Yéndon yawangoyén, 'Yuré, yéné ár mat em tanemén ndené yénd kwiotau tárfár Ju árfene áumengégh méngomé. Yéndon taweretawén kwéfkwéf méngot élaughét a tásmetawén ár emofem efalo kényátotawét fém.
english: I said , ‘ Lord , they themselves know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue those who believed in you .

nama: Njam árém yésmetaménd Stifen ménamén fá korayau árfefaf fenemén, yénd kété kwakayongérwén a ámbiro tikéfan ténmorwén yémafé. Yéndo tawanjetawén yéfene sáláme emofem tésmetawét kérot Stifen.'
english: When the blood of Stephen , your witness , was shed , I also was standing by , consenting to his death, and guarding the cloaks of those who killed him .’

nama: “Féyo Yuré norayai tafaf, 'Kuwano, yéndon so némáfáretan fém nayu yuwot kétán Téndo árfefaf.'” Fol naufaryéng yénd swém Rommén ár
english: “ He said to me , ‘ Depart , for I will send you out far from here to the Gentiles .’ ”

nama: Ár mérnam kor merkéráye káyáretawét Folefaf kétánotio njam fá norayai téndo árfenemén. Yémofem kém euyoyénd nékwan a kutnawét, “Yéné ár titérmbongi yéné yutaǃ Fá yau féyotaro yém mé ghérsé sái yémǃ”
english: They listened to him until he said that ; then they lifted up their voice and said , “Rid the earth of this fellow , for he isn’ t fit to live !”

nama: Fá ke kutnawét a tafnendawét yéfenjo sáláme a ngwérméng tétnendawét tukét.
english: As they cried out , threw off their cloaks , and threw dust into the air,

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko árém yánjo ár ewáfáretai Fole wasroghét kétán fiyarafene sifayat. Yémon emndai yánjo ár Fole ngiot. Yéné ende fá ménde témorwén yáne árém so tinjongayénd némamén ár mérén kutnawét Folefaf.
english: the commanding officer commanded him to be brought into the barracks , ordering him to be examined by scourging , that he might know for what crime they shouted against him like that .

nama: Njam fiyaram ngiot yafngoyénd, yémon yémndai fiyaré sénko ár, “Yufi yém Rommén ár ngiot endene yaufi yau yinjongi!”
english: When they had tied him up with straps, Paul asked the centurion who stood by , “ Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman , and not found guilty?”

nama: Njam fiyaré sénko ár náyárai yéné si, fá naflai kétán fiyaré ndimbal sénko ároefaf a yémndai, “Yau yafrota yéné yam yéné árofefaf. Yéné ár siyém Rommén ár.”
english: When the centurion heard it , he went to the commanding officer and told him , “Watch what you are about to do , for this man is a Roman !”

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár néngaflai Folefaf a yémotai, “Kwaufro ta, fém fété ném Rommén ár?” Folém yawangoi, “A, yénd fété wém.”
english: The commanding officer came and asked him , “ Tell me , are you a Roman ?” He said , “ Yes .”

nama: Féyo fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár norayai, “Yéndo ndimbal mani té ekman so kamndangé Rommén ár.” Folém yawangoi, “Wénde yénd náráftamén Rommén árot.”
english: The commanding officer answered , “ I bought my citizenship for a great price .” Paul said , “ But I was born a Roman.”

nama: Fiyaré efe ngiot a ámoghétro femormén Folefaf, táf fété yau yafrotaménd. Fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár férféram yifoi, ménamén yémon emndai yánjo ár Fole umbghét, wénde Fol témorwén Rommén ár. Fol yakai Ju sénko árfene áumengégh sáfrére
english: Immediately those who were about to examine him departed from him , and the commanding officer also was afraid when he realized that he was a Roman , because he had bound him .

nama: Ámb efghon, fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom téronjau téfén némamén Fol tékarotawét Ju árém. Féyo ámb efghon fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom Folene sein yaferyéng. Fá wéi emndai limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a méinyotio Ju sénko ár áumengéghét. Yémon yéngémnjoi Fol a Ju sénko árfene sáfrére nokayoi. Foléf korayawét Ju árfene limánégh árfé (Acts 22.30)|src="CN02020B.tif" size="col" copy="© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission." ref="23.30"
english: But on the next day , desiring to know the truth about why he was accused by the Jews , he freed him from the bonds and commanded the chief priests and all the council to come together , and brought Paul down and set him before them .

nama: Fol néngretai mbarkánde kétán Ju sénko árfene áumengéghfaf a norayai, “Tane fútaréf, yéndon tafrotan tanjo ásáfogh Ngánjáne sénonjo méinyotio mer mwighaye.”
english: Paul , looking steadfastly at the council , said , “Brothers, I have lived before God in all good conscience until today .”

nama: Féyo Ananias limánégh ghéngén wárogh árom ewáfáretai ár efe kaka tawakayongérwén Folefaf mbírmbíran ngiot.
english: The high priest, Ananias, commanded those who stood by him to strike him on the mouth .

nama: Féyo Fol norayai yáfaf, “Ngánjánam so kéntarmangé, fém farun nérete mer wénde kénjún yau! Fém yéné nénámnjo ta moghét Mosesene nákafé, wénde fémon yéráfne nák yénaye yénd swésmetati.”
english: Then Paul said to him , “ God will strike you , you whitewashed wall ! Do you sit to judge me according to the law , and command me to be struck contrary to the law ?”

nama: Efe tawakayongérwén Fole kaka norayayénd, “Fémon yasalite Ngánjánene limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár!”
english: Those who stood by said , “ Do you malign God ’s high priest?”

nama: Folém ewawangoi, “Fútaréf, yénd yau mat kwémorwén ndené fá siyém limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár. Ménamén si ndené yékai, 'Yaufi si yau norayati féfenjo limánégh ároefaf.'”
english: Paul said , “ I didn’ t know , brothers, that he was high priest. For it is written , ‘ You shall not speak evil of a ruler of your people .’”

nama: Fol mat náyátoi ndené ámb ár tamorwén Saduki ár a ámb Farasi ár, fá néngutnayéng ár mérnafefaf, “Tane fútaréf, yénd swém Farasi ár, Farasi árfene ambum. Yénd wakayong ufoghamé ménamén tane méghmégh kérta anoghmén.”
english: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees and the other Pharisees , he cried out in the council , “ Men and brothers, I am a Pharisee , a son of Pharisees . Concerning the hope and resurrection of the dead I am being judged !”

nama: Njam fá norayai yéné si, Farasi ár a Saduki ár nafaloyénd a éwigh yérnyayénd.
english: When he had said this , an argument arose between the Pharisees and Sadducees , and the crowd was divided .

nama: Ménamén Saduki ár korayawét ndené kér ár yau so nanotat ghérsat, yémofem efalo yau tiyátotawét ndené njafar si résagh ár a mbérmbér kété em. Wénde Farasi árém efalo tiyátotawét méinyotio yéné yam.
english: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, nor angel, nor spirit ; but the Pharisees confess all of these .

nama: Yénémamén kété témorwén ndimbal sarake, ámb nák watamegh ár efe tamorwén Farasi ár tawakayongérwén éwigh nufngoyénd, yémofem norayayénd, “Yéndfem yau yinjongém yéné ároene yaufi, némé mbérmbér o njafar si résagh ár korayat yáfaf?”
english: A great clamor arose , and some of the scribes of the Pharisees ’ part stood up , and contended, saying , “ We find no evil in this man . But if a spirit or angel has spoken to him , let ’s not fight against God !”

nama: Yéné éwigh minde tukén namndai, yénémamén fiyarafene ndimbal sénko ár férféram yifoi ménamén Fol mété téftérnawét árém. Yémon ewáfáretai fiyaré yéngwanayénd Fol yéfenemén wékeye kétán fiyarafene sifayat.
english: When a great argument arose , the commanding officer, fearing that Paul would be torn in pieces by them , commanded the soldiers to go down and take him by force from among them and bring him into the barracks .

nama: Yéné sémbáran Yuré nokayoi kaka Folefaf a yémndai, “Férfér yau némǃ Ndené fété kés noraye tanemén Jerusalem kénjún, ndenéyameyo tanemén so wéi nafrote kété Romén.” Yémofem mwighé téfaronawét Fole ngiot
english: The following night , the Lord stood by him and said , “Cheer up , Paul , for as you have testified about me at Jerusalem , so you must testify also at Rome .”

nama: Ámb akwan ámb Ju ár náumendayénd a unyéghofnar si norayayénd, “Njamke a nu yau so enetam kétánotio Fol kor yésmetam krotio.”
english: When it was day , some of the Jews banded together and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink until they had killed Paul .

nama: Kété tamorwén tárfáryo 40 Ju ár tambén emofem mwighé téfaronawét mérne.
english: There were more than forty people who had made this conspiracy .

nama: Fá néngaflayénd limánégh ghéngén wárogh árfefaf a norayayénd, “Yéndfem unyéghofnar si norayam ndené yau so mé enetam kétánotio Fol kor yésmetam krotio.
english: They came to the chief priests and the elders , and said , “ We have bound ourselves under a great curse to taste nothing until we have killed Paul .

nama: Fém a áumengégh ár tangém kétán fiyaré ndimbal sénko ároefaf a taunyangi ndené ménde yéném kor ronjaghét matot Folenemén. Tamnendangi fá a ténmasrongi fene sáfrére moghét. Yénd yéna so nawanjetam end néndkéfan Fole ngiot.”
english: Now therefore , you with the council inform the commanding officer that he should bring him down to you tomorrow, as though you were going to judge his case more exactly. We are ready to kill him before he comes near .”

nama: Wénde njam Fole yánjafam náyárai ndernáye Ju ár mwighé kémnawét yá ngiot, fá táf néngaflai fiyarafene sifayat a Fole yaufroi.
english: But Paul ’s sister’s son heard they were lying in wait , and he came and entered into the barracks and told Paul .

nama: Féyo Folém yémotai ámb fiyaré sénko ár a témndangé, “Téngwasro yéné totér ár kétán fiyaré ndimbal sénko ároefaf, ménamén yáfaf ámb si em yá waufroghét.”
english: Paul summoned one of the centurions and said , “Bring this young man to the commanding officer, for he has something to tell him .”

nama: Féyo yémo yéngémnjoi fiyaré ndimbal sénko ároefaf. Fiyaré sénko árom témotangé, “Fol, kwéfkwéf méngo árom wémnde. Fá ténmasro yéné totér ambum fefaf, ménamén yáfaf ámb si em fe sái kémaufrongé.”
english: So he took him and brought him to the commanding officer and said , “ Paul , the prisoner , summoned me and asked me to bring this young man to you . He has something to tell you .”

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom yéngwasroi totér ár téndot ménát ár yau so káyárangayénd a témotangé, “Fene ménde némé yém kwaufro?”
english: The commanding officer took him by the hand , and going aside , asked him privately , “ What is it that you have to tell me ?”

nama: Yémon korayangé, “Ámb Ju árém mwighé téfaronat ménát fém sái kénmotangi Fole wasroghét Ju sénko árfene áumengégh sifayat kaitau. Fá ménde em kor sái tawinjongi mbarkánd si yánemén, wénde fá so nuindat.
english: He said , “ The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul down to the council tomorrow, as though intending to inquire somewhat more accurately concerning him .

nama: Yau yafrota némat fá namotangi wafroghét, ménamén tárfáryo 40 árta nawanjetat Fole ngiot. Yémofem unyéghofnar si norayayénd yau njamke a nu nénet kétánotio Fol yésmetat. Sénonjo fá nawanjetat fene uwangogh siotro.”
english: Therefore don’ t yield to them , for more than forty men lie in wait for him , who have bound themselves under a curse to neither eat nor drink until they have killed him . Now they are ready , looking for the promise from you .”

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom yémndai totér ár, “Yau taufro ende ndené fémo té waufro yéné ta.” Féyo yémo yéngwatembai totér ár. Fiyaré sénko árom yáfáretam Fol kétán Kaisariat
english: So the commanding officer let the young man go, charging him , “ Tell no one that you have revealed these things to me .”

nama: Yémon emotangeyayai yánjo sómbio fiyaré sénko ár a emndeyai, “Takafend 200 fiyaré, 70 os wérse ár a 200 námb táikér ár so kuwanongi Kaisariat 9 aklok sénonjo sémbár.
english: He called to himself two of the centurions , and said , “ Prepare two hundred soldiers to go as far as Caesarea , with seventy horsemen and two hundred men armed with spears, at the third hour of the night .”

nama: Ámb os taramend Fole ménát fá merro so néngérse gafana Feliksefaf.”
english: He asked them to provide mounts , that they might set Paul on one , and bring him safely to Felix the governor .

nama: Yémo yéné férén yéfarotam ndené,
english: He wrote a letter like this :

nama: Klaudius Lisias, Kétán fene utmanégh yétkwén Gafana Feliks, Áuwégh yém.
english: “ Claudius Lysias to the most excellent governor Felix : Greetings .

nama: Yéné ár yerayénd Ju árém a yémofem ngiot térametawét, wénde yénd tanjo fiyaré mérnafé wénmormén wanjarghét, ménamén yénd mat namndayén ndené fá témorwén Rommén ár.
english: “ This man was seized by the Jews , and was about to be killed by them when I came with the soldiers and rescued him , having learned that he was a Roman .

nama: Yénd ménde kwémorwén matot némamén yémofem tánangotawét, yénémamén yéndon yéngwasroyén yéfene sénko árfene áumengégh sifayat.
english: Desiring to know the cause why they accused him , I brought him down to their council .

nama: Yéndon yinjon yéné wátaranégh siyém moghan so yém yéfene nákan, wénde kété ámb téfén yau témorwén yáfaf árkémamén krot so yém o kwéfkwéf méngon tényawangi.
english: I found him to be accused about questions of their law , but not to be charged with anything worthy of death or of imprisonment .

nama: Njam yénd mat wiyátoyénd yéfene wátaranéghmén krot yéné ároenemén, yéndon táf fété yéngwáfáretayén fefaf.
english: When I was told that the Jews lay in wait for the man , I sent him to you immediately , charging his accusers also to bring their accusations against him before you . Farewell.”

nama: Fiyaram yéfenjo si ewaitoyénd. Yémofem yéngwasroyénd Fol sémbáran a yéngémnjoyénd Antifatris yuwot.
english: So the soldiers , carrying out their orders , took Paul and brought him by night to Antipatris.

nama: Ámb efghon ámb fiyaré nénangotaménd Jerusalemét fiyarafene sifayat, wénde os wérse ár mé tangmorwén Folfé.
english: But on the next day they left the horsemen to go with him , and returned to the barracks .

nama: Njam fá nufaryénd Kaisariat, yémofem yaramayénd férén Gafana Felikse, a yafnamoyénd Fol yáfaf.
english: When they came to Caesarea and delivered the letter to the governor , they also presented Paul to him .

nama: Gafanam férén yaftam a yémotai árké yumén fá témorwén. Njam fá mat náyátoi ndené Silisiamén témorwén,
english: When the governor had read it , he asked what province he was from . When he understood that he was from Cilicia , he said ,

nama: yémon yémndai, “Yénd so náyáretan fene kérté njam fene wátaranégh ár kufarngi.” Féyo yémon ewáfáretai fiyaré Fole wanjengét warsagh ár Erodene méngon.
english: “ I will hear you fully when your accusers also arrive .” He commanded that he be kept in Herod ’s palace .

nama: Faif efogh soramé limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár Ananias néwanoi Kaisariat yémafé ámb sénko árfé a néndkéf ár Tétulus, yémofem yénérsaménd yéfenjo áwátaranégh Folenemén gafanafaf.
english: After five days , the high priest, Ananias, came down with certain elders and an orator , one Tertullus . They informed the governor against Paul .

nama: Njam Fol yanmoyénd kénjút, Tétulusém yáne áwátaranégh yéramai Feliksefaf. Fá norayai, “Fémo té yénmaramai tombé yu krufráte, a fene mwighé té yénérsam mer téfene yuwot.
english: When he was called , Tertullus began to accuse him , saying , “ Seeing that by you we enjoy much peace , and that prosperity is coming to this nation by your foresight ,

nama: Utmanéghafé Feliks, méinyotio yéné yammén yéndfem kánjo némarametam mindefifi yénémamén feyot.
english: we accept it in all ways and in all places , most excellent Felix , with all thankfulness .

nama: Wénde yénd yau ménde wém yongnayafé sái kényátongén fém, yéndo némamnendan fémo so kwumyo sénonjo siot.
english: But that I don’ t delay you , I entreat you to bear with us and hear a few words .

nama: “Yéndfem té yinjongém yéné ár ndené kérté wafrogh ár yém, kérté té tawafrote Ju árfefaf méinyotio yuwon. Fá siyém yéné mérénmén sénko ár yémndetat Najaretmén ár,
english: For we have found this man to be a plague, an instigator of insurrections among all the Jews throughout the world , and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes .

nama: wéi téfé yafngoi wasérmbogh wáwefnogh méngo, féyo yéndfem yerayém fá.
english: He even tried to profane the temple , and we arrested him .

nama: Wénde fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár Lisias yénmormén a yéngwasroi fá téfe tambén minde waftafé.
Fémon so tinjo efalo si téfene wátaranéghmén ménaye fémonyo sái yafénmeta fá.”
english: By examining him yourself you may ascertain all these things of which we accuse him .”

nama: Ámb Ju ár nufnyayénd wátaranégh kénjút, fá norayayénd ndené méinyotio yam Tétulusém árké noyarai efalo yém. Fol nawamarote fáyo
english: The Jews also joined in the attack, affirming that these things were so .

nama: Njam Gafanam yéméindai fá siot, Folém yawangoi, “Yénd mat wém yéné yunjéf tárfár fém té kéném mwighé farorégh ár yéné ndimbal yuwot, féyo yéndon áuwéghafé tanjo awamarogh yafrotan fene sáfrére.
english: When the governor had beckoned to him to speak , Paul answered , “ Because I know that you have been a judge of this nation for many years , I cheerfully make my defense,

nama: Fémon so wékeye tayamngo ndené 12 efogh soramé yénd nufaryén Jerusalemét wáwefnoghét.
english: seeing that you can verify that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship at Jerusalem .

nama: Tane wátaranégh árém yau winjoyénd yénd éwighan ámb árofé wáwefnogh méngon, yau taméindan awifaréghét ár mérén Ju árfene áumengégh méngomé o árkamé ámb yau ndimbal tesenan.
english: In the temple they didn’ t find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd , either in the synagogues or in the city .

nama: Fá féyotarofnar em yátoghét safén fefaf yéné yammén árkémamén yénd watarnat.
english: Nor can they prove to you the things of which they now accuse me .

nama: “Wénde ámb si kafe némé fá norayat siyém efalo, árké yém ndené yéndon yáwefnotan tane ákifene Ngánjáne ndené fété End waitogh ár. Fá norayat yémofem wáwefnogh yafrotat efalo Ju árfene ende, wénde fá norayat yéndo wáwefnogh yafrotan fénaté efalo yátoghe. Wénde yéndon efalo eyátotan méinyotio némé ekai nákan a némé efarotaménd Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém.
english: But this I confess to you , that according to the Way , which they call a sect, so I serve the God of our fathers , believing all things which are according to the law , and which are written in the prophets ;

nama: Tafaf yém ndené méghméghyo Ngánján kénjún yéné árfefaf kés em, kété so anogh yém mbarkánd árfefaf a yaufi árfefaf.
english: having hope toward God , which these also themselves look for , that there will be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.

nama: Yénémamén yénd so nufotan túmén ménát tane emarégh kwayaghé so yém Ngánjánrére a árfefaf.
english: In this I also practice always having a conscience void of offense toward God and men .

nama: “Tárfár yunjéf yukér soramé, yénd wénmormén Jerusalemét engérsamén tane árfene mani yauyau árét a ghéngén ramghét.
english: Now after some years , I came to bring gifts for the needy to my nation , and offerings ;

nama: Tane wátaranégh árém winjoyénd yénd wáwefnogh méngon njamandio yénd kwémorwén wányányégh wifogh tárfáran. Kété yau ár mérén tamorwén yéndfé, yau ámb usérmbogh.
english: amid which certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple , not with a mob , nor with turmoil.

nama: Wénde kété ámb Ju ár tamorwén kétamamén Esia yumén. Táf yéfene njam ewam áwátaranégh tafaf, fá fiya tanmorwén yéna fene sáfrére a tanérsawét wátaranégh.
english: They ought to have been here before you and to make accusation if they had anything against me .

nama: Tamotangi yéné ár yéna, yémofem so tawaufrongi ndené mámáfi yau yinjoyénd njam yémofem wéngwanayénd moghfaf Ju sénko árfene áumengégh kénjút.
english: Or else let these men themselves say what injustice they found in me when I stood before the council ,

nama: Ámbiro yaman yénd nutnayén njam yénd kwakayongérwén yéfene sáfan: 'Yénd ufoghan wém féfene sáfan sénonjo ménamén tane efalo yátogh yém árfene anoghmén kérta!'”
english: unless it is for this one thing that I cried standing among them , ‘ Concerning the resurrection of the dead I am being judged before you today !’”

nama: Feliks mer mat náyátoi Yesuene Endmén, féyo njam náyárai yéné sioene fifi, yékmboi mogh a yémndai, “Njam fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár Lisias kénaflangé, yéndon so mwighé yéfaronan fene awataranéghmén.”
english: But Felix , having more exact knowledge concerning the Way , deferred them , saying , “ When Lysias , the commanding officer, comes down , I will decide your case .”

nama: Yémon yáfáretam fiyaré sénko ár Fole wanjengét wénde yaramai ámb enagh a yumyoi yáne fútaram so tawarametawét yáne méndefé rokar.
english: He ordered the centurion that Paul should be kept in custody and should have some privileges, and not to forbid any of his friends to serve him or to visit him .

nama: Tárfár efogh soramé Felikséf enérmén yánjo amaf Drusila, efe témorwén Jumén amaf. Yémo yáfáretam yánjo ásáfogh ár Fole wasroghét. Fá ménde tamorwén sái káyáretawét Folefaf árfene ndimbal mwighamén Keriso Yesuene kénjún.
english: After some days , Felix came with Drusilla his wife , who was a Jewess , and sent for Paul and heard him concerning the faith in Christ Jesus .

nama: Fol korayawéng mbarkánd ghérsamén, fémyo awaraweréghmén a mogh efoghtaumén árkéméné yéném, Feliks férfér témorwén a norayai, “Féyo tém sénonjoyot. Fém téu kuwano. Efogh njam wam yéndon so kéngmotangén ménát sái néném tafaf wéikor.”
english: As he reasoned about righteousness , self-control , and the judgment to come , Felix was terrified , and answered, “ Go your way for this time , and when it is convenient for me , I will summon you .”

nama: Yéné efghonyo fá témorwén emarghan ndené Folém so mani taramangai, féyo fá túmén yémafé kafángowét a koraiwét yémafé.
english: Meanwhile, he also hoped that money would be given to him by Paul , that he might release him . Therefore also he sent for him more often and talked with him .

nama: Sómbio yunjéf soramé, Forsius Festus nosoi Gafana Feliksene sifayan. Wénde ménamén Feliks ménde témorwén Ju árfene ménde sái tawawafrotau, yémo yéfrangoi Fol kwéfkwéf méngon.
english: But when two years were fulfilled , Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus , and desiring to gain favor with the Jews , Felix left Paul in bonds .

nama: Festus namndai gafana, nambio efogh soramé fá nuwanoi Kaisariata kétán Jerusalem.
english: Festus therefore , having come into the province , after three days went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea .

nama: Jerusalem kénjún, limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a Ju árfene sénko ár nufaryénd yáne sáfrére a yéramayénd wátaranégh si Folenemén.
english: Then the high priest and the principal men of the Jews informed him against Paul , and they begged him ,

nama: Yémofem yamnendayénd Festus yéfene méndeyot Fole wasarghét Jerusalemét, ménamén fá káfrendawét yá ngiot endén.
english: asking a favor against him , that he would summon him to Jerusalem , plotting to kill him on the way .

nama: Festusém ewawangoi, “Fol siyém té yangongi kwéfkwéf méngo ár yém Kaisarian, a yéndio so wéngém tekofnaye.
english: However Festus answered that Paul should be kept in custody at Caesarea , and that he himself was about to depart shortly .

nama: So ámb féfene limánégh árfé yénngém yéndfé, a njam yéné árom némndafi mámáfi yafrota, yémofem so wátaranéghan tékmangi fá.”
english: “ Let them therefore ”, he said , “ that are in power among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong in the man , let them accuse him .”

nama: 8 o 10 efogh ámnjogh soramé yémafé, Festus néwanoi Kaisariat. A ámb efghon yémon yanmoi mogh a emndai Fol sái ténémnjongayénd yáfaf.
english: When he had stayed among them more than ten days , he went down to Caesarea , and on the next day he sat on the judgment seat , and commanded Paul to be brought .

nama: Njam Fol nélawai kénjút, Ju ár efe enmormén kétándmé Jerusalemta yarendayénd fá. Yémofem enérsaménd yáfaf tárfár kwérnarafé wátaranégh si, wénde fá féyotarofnar emormén yátoghét yéfene wátaranéghan.
english: When he had come , the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him , bringing against him many and grievous charges which they could not prove,

nama: Féyo Fol fáyo nawamarotai, “Yéndon yau ámb mámáfi tafrotan kétán Ju árfene nákfaf o wáwefnogh méngofaf o Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisarefaf.”
english: while he said in his defense, “ Neither against the law of the Jews , nor against the temple , nor against Caesar , have I sinned at all .”

nama: Festus ménde témorwén Ju ár sái áuwéghét taméindau, Folefaf norayai, “Fém ménde té ném álighét Jerusalemét a sái némakai tane sáfrére yéné wátaranéghan?”
english: But Festus , desiring to gain favor with the Jews , answered Paul and said , “ Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem and be judged by me there concerning these things ?”

nama: Folém yawangoi, “Yénd sénonjo wakai Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisarene mogh sáfrére, árké yém mbarkánd sifayé tayot moghét. Fém fémyo mer mat em ndené yéndo ámb yau mámáfi tafrotan Ju árfefaf.
english: But Paul said , “ I am standing before Caesar ’s judgment seat, where I ought to be tried . I have done no wrong to the Jews , as you also know very well .

nama: Wénde yéndon njam té tafrotan mámáfi, féyo moghan yénd so wésmetat kérotio, yénd yau nawatnan kérot. Wénde njam fá nuindat moghan féyo emo yau so kwengongé yénd kétán yéné árfefaf ménát sái wésmetat kérotio. Yénd mé nawatarnan Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisar sái náyáreta tane mogh!”
english: For if I have done wrong and have committed anything worthy of death , I don’ t refuse to die ; but if none of those things is true that they accuse me of , no one can give me up to them . I appeal to Caesar !”

nama: Festus yánjo árfé korayawét, yéné soramé fá norayai Folefaf, “Fém té norayai, 'Kaisarém so náyárete tane mogh.' Yénémaménnde, fém so kuwano Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisarefaf!” Festusém yaufroi warsagh ár Agrifai Folenemén
english: Then Festus , when he had conferred with the council , answered, “ You have appealed to Caesar . To Caesar you shall go .”

nama: Nambio efogh soramé warsagh ár Agrifa a Bernisa nufareyayénd Kaisariat. Fá enérmén ménát sái tumyeyayénd Festus.
english: Now when some days had passed , King Agrippa and Bernice arrived at Caesarea and greeted Festus .

nama: Njam fá emorarmén tárfár efogh kété, Festusfem taruinwét Agrifafé Ju árfene wátaranéghmén Folefaf. Fá norayai, “Ár yéna yém kwéfkwéf méngo ár efe Feliksém yéngéfrango.
english: As he stayed there many days , Festus laid Paul ’s case before the king , saying , “ There is a certain man left a prisoner by Felix ;

nama: Njam yénd wéngmormén Jerusalemét, limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a Ju árfene sénko árém enérsaménd wátaranégh a wémotayénd ménát Fol sái kértayan tékmangayén.
english: about whom , when I was at Jerusalem , the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed me , asking for a sentence against him .

nama: “Yéndon ewawangoyén, 'Fém ndené norayati yéné árom té tafrote mámáfi yam. Fá fronde so kokayongé féfene sáfrére efe nawatarnat yáfaf. Féyo yémon so tawawangongé féfe, féyo yéndo so táf moghan tákman efe yém mbarkánd. Yéné fiyém téfene Rom nák.'
english: I answered them that it is not the custom of the Romans to give up any man to destruction before the accused has met the accusers face to face and has had opportunity to make his defense concerning the matter laid against him .

nama: Njam yáne wánangogh ár yénmormén yénan, yéndon yau yalayén yéné kérté. Wénde yénd náumendayém moghét ámb efghon a emndayén yéné ár yénmasroyénd kénjút.
english: When therefore they had come together here , I didn’ t delay, but on the next day sat on the judgment seat and commanded the man to be brought.

nama: Yáne wánangogh ár nokayoyénd a norayayénd. Yénd kemnan ndené fá so norayat ndené Folém té ámb minde mámáfi yam tafrote. Wénde fá yau yéné si norayayénd.
english: When the accusers stood up , they brought no charges against him of such things as I supposed ;

nama: Wéndende, fá yémafé kéwitawét Folfé ménamén némé Ju árém tawatametawét Ngánjánenemén. Fá kéwitawét yánemén efe kér té yémormén Yesu. Wénde Fol ndené korayau fá ghérsé mé yém.
english: but had certain questions against him about their own religion and about one Jesus , who was dead , whom Paul affirmed to be alive .

nama: Yénd yau mat wém ndernáye so tawafénmetawén yéné áwátaranégh. Féyo yéndon yémotayén fá, 'Fém té ménde ném ménát álighét Jerusalemét a némakai kété ufoghan yéné wátaranéghan.'
english: Being perplexed how to inquire concerning these things , I asked whether he was willing to go to Jerusalem and there be judged concerning these matters.

nama: Wénde Folém wawangoi ta, 'Yau, yénd ménde wém Kaisarém sái náyáreta tane si.' Yénayan yénd norayayén tane fiyarafaf, 'Ménát Fol sái tumbetau yéna kétánotio yéndon téngwatemban Kaisarefaf.'”
english: But when Paul had appealed to be kept for the decision of the emperor , I commanded him to be kept until I could send him to Caesar .”

nama: Féyo Agrifa norayai Festusefaf, “Yéndon so yiyáretan fá némé si njam noraya.” Yémon yawangoi, “Káye fémo so yiyárete.” Fol noraye Agrifafaf
english: Agrippa said to Festus , “ I also would like to hear the man myself.” “Tomorrow,” he said , “ you shall hear him .”

nama: Ámb efghon Agrifa a Bernisam numaneménd mer sáláme. Fá nélaweyayénd Gafaman méngot yémafé fiyaré sénko árfé a utmanégh ár tesenmén. Festusém emndai yánjo ár, “Ténmasrongi Fol yénan.”
english: So on the next day , when Agrippa and Bernice had come with great pomp , and they had entered into the place of hearing with the commanding officers and the principal men of the city , at the command of Festus , Paul was brought in .

nama: Festus norayai, “Warsagh ár Agrifa a méinyotio efe yéna em yéndfé, fémofem téfandangi yéné ár! Méinyotio Ju árém wamnendangi yénd Jerusalemén a yéna Kaisarian, kutnawét ndené fá yau so nakete.
english: Festus said , “ King Agrippa, and all men who are here present with us , you see this man about whom all the multitude of the Jews petitioned me , both at Jerusalem and here , crying that he ought not to live any longer .

nama: Wénde yéndon yau yinjoyén yáne yaufi wafroghyam árkémamén fá krot yékmetat. Wénde ménamén fá wéikor norayai fá ménde témorwén Kaisar sái káyáretau yáne áwátaranégh. Féyo yéndon mwighé yafrotamén yá watembghét kétán Rom.
english: But when I found that he had committed nothing worthy of death , and as he himself appealed to the emperor , I determined to send him ,

nama: “Yénd yau mat wém némé yéndon so yéfarotan Rom tuk warsagh árfefaf yánemén. Yénémaménnde yéndon té ténémnjan fá méinyotio féfefaf. Warsagh ár Agrifafaf, yéndon némamnendan fém kor merkéráye náyáreta némé Fol noraye. Féyo yénd so táf norayam yéné kértamén mérne. Soramé yéndon so mbarkánde férén yéfarotan kétán Kaisarefaf.
english: of whom I have no certain thing to write to my lord . Therefore I have brought him out before you , and especially before you , King Agrippa, that , after examination I may have something to write .

nama: Yénd norayan yéné ménamén njam yéndfem tatembangém kwéfkwéf méngo ár kétán Rom tuk warsagh ároefaf téfnofnar yuwon, féyo yéné yau so mer yém.”
english: For it seems to me unreasonable, in sending a prisoner , not to also specify the charges against him .”

nama: Féyo Agrifa norayai Folefaf, “Koraya sénonjo.” Féyo Folém ngángé euyoi a norayai awamarogh si,
english: Agrippa said to Paul , “ You may speak for yourself .” Then Paul stretched out his hand , and made his defense.

nama: “Warsagh ár Agrifa, yénd áuwéghafé wém fe sáfrére sénonjo táf yéndon norayan awamarogh si méinyotio Ju árfene wánangoghmén,
english: “ I think myself happy , King Agrippa, that I am to make my defense before you today concerning all the things that I am accused by the Jews ,

nama: ménamén fém mer mat ném ndernáye Ju ár emor a yéfene fénatayam. Féyo fém náyáreta tafaf métare!
english: especially because you are expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews . Therefore I beg you to hear me patiently .

nama: “Méinyotio Ju ár mat em tanemén kétándmayo yénd tosé. Fá mat em némé sáfot ghérsafé yénd kwémorangérwén tanjo yuwon a ndenáyo Jerusalemén.
english: “ Indeed , all the Jews know my way of life from my youth up , which was from the beginning among my own nation and at Jerusalem ;

nama: Fá mat té tamorwén tanemén tekefifi. Fá so nafrotat ndené yénd kwémorwén Farasi ár. Yéndo mbarkánde ewaitotan Ngánjánene nák ndenanit méinyotio Farasi árém kés ewafrotat.
english: having known me from the first , if they are willing to testify , that after the strictest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee .

nama: Tane méghmégh siyém ménáyan némé Ngánjánam té nénorayai téfene ákiafefaf, yéné téfnan yénd ufoghan wém sénonjo.
english: Now I stand here to be judged for the hope of the promise made by God to our fathers ,

nama: Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si norayam ndimbal yammén kétán téfene 12 Israil ár mérénfaf. Yéndfem méinyotio yánjetam ménát so tanangém némé Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si néngorayai téfefaf. Yéné téfnan yéndfem Ju árém túmén yáwefnotam Ngánjáne sémbár a efogh. Ndené yéné Ju ár sem yéna, yéndo efalo yiyátotan Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si. Wénde yéné yém téfén ndené fá nawatarnat tafaf.
english: which our twelve tribes , earnestly serving night and day , hope to attain . Concerning this hope I am accused by the Jews , King Agrippa!

nama: Ngánjánam so taméindangé kér ár sái kanongi ghérsat wéikor. Fémofem so efalo wiyátotati yénd njam norayan yéné si.
english: Why is it judged incredible with you if God does raise the dead ?

nama: “Yénd yéndio ménde kwémorwén sái kásáfotawén tékarotawén Yesu Najaretmén.
english: “ I myself most certainly thought that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth .

nama: Yéné fiyém yéndon némé tafrotawén Jerusalemén. Limánégh ghéngén wárogh árfene si muyayan, yéndon efalo yátogh ár tárfár takmetawén kwéfkwéf méngot. Njam téfene limánégh árém korayangayénd, 'Yéné efalo yátogh ár táf kér em,' Yéndon yéfene mwighé ámbiro tiyátongayén.
english: I also did this in Jerusalem . I both shut up many of the saints in prisons , having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death I gave my vote against them .

nama: Tárfár mbinotma yénd kwiyotau Ju árfene áumengégh méngomé ménát yéfe tékrátan kémghét. Yéndon tawáfáretawén fá yaufi siot Ngánjánefaf. Yéndon tawakeindawén nékw kwémorwén yéfefaf a tawifnawén dábi árfene tesenamé.
english: Punishing them often in all the synagogues , I tried to make them blaspheme. Being exceedingly enraged against them , I persecuted them even to foreign cities .

nama: “Ámb efghon yénd álighan kwémorwén Damaskosét limánégh ghéngén wárogh árfene si muyafé a wáfáretayénd.
english: “ Whereupon as I traveled to Damascus with the authority and commission from the chief priests,

nama: Warsagh ár Agrifa, séite karéfta, njam yénd kwéngmorwén endmé, yéndo yinjoyén kwayaghé njafarta, yau ndené efogh kwayaghé, wénde yéné yáindai, a warendai yénd a yéndfé áligh ár.
english: at noon, O king , I saw on the way a light from the sky, brighter than the sun , shining around me and those who traveled with me .

nama: Yénd méinyotio nuyáyém mbandot, a yénd náyárayén kém korayau Ibru sie, 'Sol, Sol, némamén fémo yénd wesmete? Fémo njam kafkaf tautar wím minan, fémon ndémke so yéfande.'
english: When we had all fallen to the earth , I heard a voice saying to me in the Hebrew language , ‘ Saul , Saul , why are you persecuting me ? It is hard for you to kick against the goads .’

nama: “Féyo yéndon yémotayén, 'Efe fém ném, Yuré?' “Yuram wawangoi, 'Yénd wém Yesu, efe fémon yésmete.
english: “ I said , ‘ Who are you , Lord ?’ “ He said , ‘ I am Jesus , whom you are persecuting .

nama: Kano a kokai fenjo kafkafe. Yénd té nufarén fefaf fém némuyafon tane ásáfoghét a fandagh ár. Fémon so ewaufrote tanemén némé fémon téfande a so mé yéngfande.
english: But arise, and stand on your feet , for I have appeared to you for this purpose : to appoint you a servant and a witness both of the things which you have seen and of the things which I will reveal to you ;

nama: Yéndon so kémanjarén kétamé Ju ár a Téndo ár. Yéndon némáfáretan téndo árfefaf
english: delivering you from the people and from the Gentiles , to whom I send you ,

nama: ménát yéfene ghékéf sauréghét a so ewangote kwéfkwéfta kwayaghat, kétamé Sáiféroene waftata kétán Ngánjánefaf. Féyo yémofem so tanangi yaufiyam wighét yaufita a sifayé tawaramangi Ngánjánene árfefaf emofem yénd efalo wiyátotat.'
english: to open their eyes , that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God , that they may receive remission of sins and an inheritance among those who are sanctified by faith in me .’

nama: “Féyo warsagh ár Agrifa, yénd áyárégh kwémorwén ghékéf waingégh yaman njafarmén.
english: “ Therefore , King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision ,

nama: Fronde ewaufrotan kétán efe tamorwén Damaskosén, ndenáyo yéfefaf Jerusalemét a méinyotio Judiat a méinyotio Téndo árfefaf. Yéndon táitotawén ménát fá so nawengotat yaufita Ngánjánefaf a so yufarotat yéfenjo awengogh yam wafroghe
english: but declared first to them of Damascus , at Jerusalem , and throughout all the country of Judea , and also to the Gentiles , that they should repent and turn to God , doing works worthy of repentance .

nama: Yéné téfnan ámb Ju árém wérayénd wáwefnogh méngo sifayan a kwérametawét ngiot.
english: For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple and tried to kill me .

nama: Wénde Ngánjánam kwáutáne ta méinyotio efogh kétánotio sénonjo. Féyo yénd wakai yéna a nafrotan efalo si yétkwénofnar ár a yétkwénafé árfefaf. Yéndo yéné norayan némé Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a Moses korayau so nufrotat:
english: Having therefore obtained the help that is from God , I stand to this day testifying both to small and great , saying nothing but what the prophets and Moses said would happen ,

nama: Kerisom so tékrát yáyárete, a fronde so yém anoghét kétamé kérta, so yénérse si kwayaghé yánjo árfefaf a Téndo árfefaf.”
english: how the Christ must suffer , and how, by the resurrection of the dead , he would be first to proclaim light both to these people and to the Gentiles .”

nama: Njam Folém mé korayau, Festusém nutnai, “Fol, fém onon ném! Fenjo ndimbal otamegham néméinde ononot!”
english: As he thus made his defense, Festus said with a loud voice , “ Paul , you are crazy! Your great learning is driving you insane !”

nama: Folém yawangoi, “Minde utmanéghafé Festus. Yénd yau onon wém. Yénd némé norayan siyém efalo a fifiyafé.
english: But he said , “ I am not crazy, most excellent Festus , but boldly declare words of truth and reasonableness.

nama: Warsagh ár mat yéné yammén yém. Yénd so norayan férférofnaye fefaf. Yénd mat wém ndené warsagh ár mat yém némé yénd norayan, ménamén yéné yam yau nufrotam átnoghe kwéndkwéndan.
english: For the king knows of these things , to whom also I speak freely . For I am persuaded that none of these things is hidden from him , for this has not been done in a corner .

nama: Warsagh ár Agrifa, fémon efalo té eyátote Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár? Yénd mat fémo fété.”
english: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets ? I know that you believe .”

nama: Féyo Agrifa norayai Folefaf, “Fém ndené nemne yéné tekofnare fémon so kwiyáto wékeye yénd ndené fété efalo yátogh ár?”
english: Agrippa said to Paul , “ With a little persuasion are you trying to make me a Christian ?”

nama: Folém yawangoi, “Ténjfi o tombé kéráye, tane orayégh Ngánjánefaf feyot a méinyotio emofem wiyáretat yénd sénonjo so kénamndangi efalo yátogh ár ndené yénd swém, wénde yénd yau ménde wém fém sái kamndangi kwéfkwéf méngo ár ndené yénd.”
english: Paul said , “ I pray to God , that whether with little or with much , not only you , but also all that hear me today , might become such as I am , except for these bonds .”

nama: Warsagh ár, gafana, Bernis a méinyotio efe tamorangérwén yémafé fá nokayoyénd.
english: The king rose up with the governor and Bernice, and those who sat with them .

nama: Soramé yémofem yéfrangoyénd a fá nufngoyénd si yéfe kénjúnro, “Yéné árom némé yau tafrote némndafi árkémamén krot so tékmangi o kwéfkwéf méngot tényawangi.”
english: When they had withdrawn, they spoke to one another , saying , “ This man does nothing worthy of death or of bonds .”

nama: Agrifa norayai Festusefaf, “Njam yéné ár yau té norayai Kaisarefaf ménát sái káyáretau yane si, féyo yéndfem so téngwurtongém.”
english: Agrippa said to Festus , “ This man might have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar .”

nama: Njam mwighé nufaryéng nde yénd so kuwanongayém ndimbal boute kétán Italia, yémofem eramayénd Fol a ámb kwéfkwéf méngo ár yáfaf Rom fiyaré sénko ár Juliés. Fá tafnyangérwén tukén warsagh ároene fiyaré mérnan.
english: When it was determined that we should sail for Italy , they delivered Paul and certain other prisoners to a centurion named Julius , of the Augustan band .

nama: Yénd nuwanoyém ndimbal boutfaf árkéméné yénmormén kétamé Adramitum. Yéné bout témorwén kafrendau. Yéné so kuwanongai yénamé tesenamé arakaf téndomé Esia ndimbal yuwon. Yénd nuflayém yéné boutan. Ár yétkwén Aristakus efe témorwén Tesalonika tesenmén Makedonia yuwon nuwanoyém yémafé. Folene áligh Romét (Acts 27.1—28.14)|src="nmx_225wbt_Paul to Rome [bwf-light] @en_v3.tif" size="col" copy="© 2025 Wycliffe Bible Translators, Inc." ref="27.1—28.14"
english: Embarking in a ship of Adramyttium, which was about to sail to places on the coast of Asia , we put to sea, Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica being with us .

nama: Ámb kwéfon nufaryém Sidonét. Juliésém mere Fol yumyoi fandaghét yá fútarfefaf ménát yémofem so taramangayénd yáne méndefé rokar.
english: The next day , we touched at Sidon . Julius treated Paul kindly and gave him permission to go to his friends and refresh himself .

nama: Njam yéndfem yéfrangoyém Sidon tesen, ménamén ndimbal mbormboram wangogh yénmafngoi, féyo yénd kérsawém Saifrés fér téndomé árkamé mbormbor yau téfnjawéng muyafé.
english: Putting to sea from there , we sailed under the lee of Cyprus , because the winds were contrary .

nama: Yéné soramé néférmayém marat arakaffaf kaka kétán arakaf téndot Silisia a Famfilia yuwon, nénufaryém kétán Mirat tesen Lisia yuwon.
english: When we had sailed across the sea which is off Cilicia and Pamphylia , we came to Myra , a city of Lycia .

nama: Mira tesenan, fiyaré sénko árom yinjoi Aleksandria árfene ndimbal bout árké kafrendawét álighét Italiat, féyo yémo yénmaflai boutan.
english: There the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing for Italy , and he put us on board.

nama: Yénd áfráe kérsawém tárfár efogh, a táikér mé téfandawém ufarghét Sénidus yuwot. Yau mbormboram ténmumyotau yénd álighét yéné sifayamé, féyo yénd téndomé nuwanoyém kétán náikái tambén Krit fér, yéngwaindayém Salmone ghém.
english: When we had sailed slowly many days , and had come with difficulty opposite Cnidus , the wind not allowing us further, we sailed under the lee of Crete , opposite Salmone .

nama: Yénd áfráe kérsawém arakaf téndomé a nufaryém yéné sifayé yétkwén Wanjarégh Sur, yau nayu témorwén kétándmé Lasia tesenot.
english: With difficulty sailing along it we came to a certain place called Fair Havens, near the city of Lasea .

nama: Yénandmé yéndfem té tárfár efogh tanérsam, féyo yéné so yém mámáfi njam mé nérsam boute ménamén Ju árfene ndimbal awafarmanégh efogh soramé arakaf namndai minde mbormborafé. Féyo Folém emndai ár boutan,
english: When much time had passed and the voyage was now dangerous because the Fast had now already gone by , Paul admonished them

nama: “Fútaréf, káyárangi tafaf yénd njam kuwanongém sénonjo téfene ndimbal bout rokarafé so kértayan yém, a tárfár ghérsé so etarnam.”
english: and said to them , “Sirs, I perceive that the voyage will be with injury and much loss , not only of the cargo and the ship , but also of our lives .”

nama: Wénde fiyaré sénko ár yau náyárai Folefaf némé fá norayai, wénde ewaiteyai ndimbal bout wawerégh ár a ndimbal bout yurane si.
english: But the centurion gave more heed to the master and to the owner of the ship than to those things which were spoken by Paul .

nama: Ménamén sur yau mer témorwén yunjfan yéfe kémghét, tárfár ár ménde tamorwén awaingéghét kétán sur yétkwén Finiks yunjéf ámnjoghét kété. Finiks sur témorwén sauréghkaf kétán arakafot sómbio ende a muyafé mbormbor yau kor ténéfnjau kété. Fol korayau Boutan mbormboran (Acts 27.9-10)|src="CN02042B.tif" size="col" copy="© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission." ref="27.9-10" Ndimbal Mbormbor
english: Because the haven was not suitable to winter in , the majority advised going to sea from there , if by any means they could reach Phoenix and winter there , which is a port of Crete , looking southwest and northwest .

nama: Njam métar náikái mbormbor waufégh yénmafngoi, yémofem yinjoyénd mer témorwén álighét. Féyo táf yurtoyénd werégh téfén ndimbal boutoene a nuwanoyénd Krit arakaf téndomé.
english: When the south wind blew softly , supposing that they had obtained their purpose , they weighed anchor and sailed along Crete , close to shore.

nama: Wénde ndimbal mbormbor yétkwén Yambérmawér Mbormboram eutfoi fá a yénmaufetai kétamé férta.
english: But before long , a stormy wind beat down from shore, which is called Euroclydon .

nama: Mbormbor témaufetau minde muyafé ndimbal boutfaf. Féyotarofnar tamorwén wawerégh téfnáram sái taweretawét ndimbal bout mbarkánde kétán mbormborfaf. Féyo yumyoyénd ndimbal bout mbormboram yéngwanai.
english: When the ship was caught and couldn’ t face the wind, we gave way to it and were driven along .

nama: Yénd yénamé yéngmormén Kauda fér néndkéfamé. Bout wawerégh téfnáram wénmauyoyénd Boutoene ndingi nutambén ménát wanjarghét.
english: Running under the lee of a small island called Clauda , we were able , with difficulty , to secure the boat .

nama: Féyo yémofem yarmboyénd ndingi ndimbal bout tukét. Yéné soramé yémofem wumbégh yafngoyénd ángéghe ndimbal bout ménát taikrot. Ménamén fá férfér tamorwén nde mété táf kásmetawét wíman Siritisén, yémofem yeworyénd boutoene wérégh tot téfén yumyoyénd ndimbal bout yéngwanayéng mbormboram.
english: After they had hoisted it up , they used cables to help reinforce the ship . Fearing that they would run aground on the Syrtis sand bars, they lowered the sea anchor , and so were driven along .

nama: Ndimbal mbormbor mindefifi taufetau, féyo ámb efghon ámb rokar taranégh ewafngoyénd ndimbal boutta.
english: As we labored exceedingly with the storm, the next day they began to throw things overboard.

nama: Nambiomén efghon yémofem taranégh ewafngoyénd ámb bout ásáfoghmén rokar.
english: On the third day , they threw out the ship ’s tackle with their own hands.

nama: Tárfár efghon yéndfem yau tafandawém efogh a kénd. Ndimbal mbormbor mé ténmaufetau muyafé a yénd féyo yénmormén a téfene kamnang nefoyénd awanjarghét.
english: When neither sun nor stars shone on us for many days , and no small storm pressed on us , all hope that we would be saved was now taken away .

nama: Yéné téfnan yéndfem yau njamke tánetawém ndené teke. Fol nokayoi emndai ár: “Fém fai káyáretawét tafaf njam tamorwén kété Krit féran, fémofem yau yéné wasérmbogh so tinjongayénd a té kawaghnat.
english: When they had been long without food, Paul stood up in the middle of them and said , “Sirs, you should have listened to me , and not have set sail from Crete and have gotten this injury and loss .

nama: Wénde sénonjo yéndon ewamnendan fém, yau férfér em ménamén emo ámb yau ghérsé so yétarne, mé ndimbal boutro so nawasérmbote.
english: Now I exhort you to cheer up , for there will be no loss of life among you , but only of the ship .

nama: Yénd wém Ngánjánene, a yéndon yáwefnotan yáyot. Sémbáran yémon ténmáfárete njafar si résagh ár
