english: For there stood by me this night an angel, belonging to the God whose I am and whom I serve ,

nama: ta waufroi, 'Fol, yau férfér ném! Fém so némakai ufoghan Rom tuk warsagh ároene rére. Ménamén fenemén Ngánjánam so tawanjarngé efe méinyotio em ndimbal boutan.'
english: saying , ‘Don’ t be afraid , Paul . You must stand before Caesar . Behold , God has granted you all those who sail with you .’

nama: Féyo férfér yau em! Yénd mat wém ndené Ngánjánam so yafrote mbarkánd némé fá norayai.
english: Therefore , sirs, cheer up! For I believe God , that it will be just as it has been spoken to me .

nama: Wéndende, ndimbal bout so nirone ámb féran.” Ndimbal bout niroyéng
english: But we must run aground on a certain island .”

nama: 14 sémbáran yénd ténmimndau mbormboram yénamé Mediterian arakafamé. Wénde sémbár néndkéf ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár mat náyátoyénd yénd kaka náyátoyém mbandfaf.
english: But when the fourteenth night had come , as we were driven back and forth in the Adriatic Sea, about midnight the sailors surmised that they were drawing near to some land .

nama: Yémofem yufotaménd nuoene kénjkam ndené 40 mité. Yéné soramé wéi yufotaménd a yinyoyénd 30 mité kénjkam témorwén.
english: They took soundings and found twenty fathoms . After a little while , they took soundings again , and found fifteen fathoms .

nama: Ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár férfér tamorwén ménamén mété táf kásmetawét wíman. Féyo yémofem ewitaménd 4 werégh tot téfén ndimbal boutoene kwémb tambén a fá Ngánjánafé orayégh nufngoyénd efogh kwayaghat.
english: Fearing that we would run aground on rocky ground, they let go four anchors from the stern , and wished for daylight .

nama: Bout ásáfogh ár ménde tamorwén sái téfrangongayénd ndimbal bout. Féyo yémofem yeworyénd ndingi arakafot. Fá yéta kwankwane álighét nufngoyénd. Fá norayayénd, “Yénd yéngém kétán ndimbal bout sénkofaf ménát ndimbal boutoene werégh tot téfén so taweworngém arakafot.” Wénde kuindawét.
english: As the sailors were trying to flee out of the ship and had lowered the boat into the sea , pretending that they would lay out anchors from the bow ,

nama: Wénde Folém emndai fiyaré sénko ár a fiyaré, “Ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár yau njam emor ndimbal boutan, féfene kamnang ámb yau yém ménát awanjarghét.”
english: Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers , “ Unless these stay in the ship , you can ’ t be saved .”

nama: Féyo fiyaram yénfayénd ángégh árké teretawét ndingi a nuyai arakafot.
english: Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the boat and let it fall off .

nama: Fauréghétro témorwén Folém ewamnendai njamke nénet, a emndai, “14 efogh fém té tam mámé mwighé a ámb njamke yau tánetati.
english: While the day was coming on , Paul begged them all to take some food , saying , “ Today is the fourteenth day that you wait and continue fasting, having taken nothing .

nama: Yéndon ewamnendan fém, féyo, njamke tánetangi, ghérsé sái mé em. Yéné yafrotati a fém so mer em.”
english: Therefore I beg you to take some food , for this is for your safety; for not a hair will perish from any of your heads .”

nama: Si soramé, Folém yanayéng ámb féskaf flawé karéf a kánjo si yaramayéng Ngánjáne. Féyo méinyotio árfene sáfrére, yémo yétfoi féskaf flawé a ténetau.
english: When he had said this and had taken bread, he gave thanks to God in the presence of all ; then he broke it and began to eat .

nama: Fá méinyotio náuwétaménd a ámb njamke enetaménd.
english: Then they all cheered up , and they also took food .

nama: Kété tamorwén méinyotio 276 ár ndimbal boutan.
english: In all , we were two hundred seventy-six souls on the ship .

nama: Njam yémofem enetaménd a eremngoi, yémofem tawitawét flawé baik kétán arakafot ndimbal bout ofat.
english: When they had eaten enough , they lightened the ship , throwing out the wheat into the sea .

nama: Ámb akwan, yémofem yau yáyámngoyénd mband, wénde yémofem yinjoyénd trewar ménáyafé miri mbandafé. Féyo yémofem mwighé yinjoyénd ménát wérset miriamé.
english: When it was day , they didn’ t recognize the land , but they noticed a certain bay with a beach, and they decided to try to drive the ship onto it .

nama: Yémofem enfayénd werégh téfén ángégh féyo engéfrangoyénd arakafan. Táfandio yémofem eferyénd ángégh árkéménayam teretawét ndimbal bout waknyéghmén. Yénamayo yémofem yakayoyénd sáimai ndimbal bout sénko tambén a mbormboram ndimbal bout résagh yafngoi kétán mirifaf.
english: Casting off the anchors, they left them in the sea , at the same time untying the rudder ropes . Hoisting up the foresail to the wind , they made for the beach.

nama: Wénde ndimbal bout miri mbandot náyátoi. Féyo ndimbal bout sénko nékmboi kété yau kimndau, a ndimbal sófam yésmai ndimbal bout kwémbta méinyotio yiroryéng.
english: But coming to a place where two seas met , they ran the vessel aground . The bow struck and remained immovable, but the stern began to break up by the violence of the waves.

nama: Fiyaré méndefnar tamorwén efe yau so kakmetawét tunat a kéngongayénd, féyo yémofem mwighé téfrendawét kwéfkwéf méngo árfe ngiot.
english: The soldiers ’ counsel was to kill the prisoners , so that none of them would swim out and escape .

nama: Wéndende, fiyaré sénko ár ménde témorwén Fole wanjarghét a yémo yau taumyotau yéné yam wafroghét. Wéndende, “Méinyotio ár efe mat em akmégh, katnangi arakafot, a kakmetangi tunat.
english: But the centurion , desiring to save Paul , stopped them from their purpose , and commanded that those who could swim should throw themselves overboard first to go toward the land ;

nama: Ámb árém táf ewaitotat. Nawéretat wén karfan o ndimbal bout karéfkaréfan.” Féyo fá nufaryénd merro kétán tunat.
english: and the rest should follow , some on planks and some on other things from the ship . So they all escaped safely to the land .

nama: Njam yénd merro nufaryém, yéndfem yáyamngoyém fér yétkwén Malta.
english: When we had escaped , then they learned that the island was called Malta .

nama: Fér ár efe tákmangérwén yémofem yénmumyoyénd mere a fútar náyátoyém. Yémofem fés yétayaménd a yénmumyoyénd yénd méinyotio ménamén nu kafamndau a kurférmén.
english: The natives showed us uncommon kindness ; for they kindled a fire and received us all , because of the present rain and because of the cold .

nama: Folém ámb fés sut enwanai a efayoi fését. Fés waftayam yéméindai kamban étorghét a yararyéng yáne ngángé.
english: But when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire , a viper came out because of the heat and fastened on his hand .

nama: Njam fér árém yinjoyénd kamban térnyangérwén yáne ngángayan, fá korayangi yéfe kénjúnro, “Yéné ár mété yém ár ngi téfnár! Fá yau kolitote arakafan, wénde ngánjánam yau yumyo ghérsat.”
english: When the natives saw the creature hanging from his hand , they said to one another , “ No doubt this man is a murderer , whom , though he has escaped from the sea , yet Justice has not allowed to live .”

nama: Wénde Folém yéfnendai kamban fését yéneghoi a ámb némé kérté yau yanai.
english: However he shook off the creature into the fire , and wasn’ t harmed.

nama: Ár kemnawét yáfaf ndené mbwe kar so tarenau o sune kér témorwén. Wénde teke awanjengégh soramé yau némndafi nufrotam yáfaf, yéfefaf mwighé nufaryénd a norayayénd, “Fá siyém ngánján.”
english: But they expected that he would have swollen or fallen down dead suddenly, but when they watched for a long time and saw nothing bad happen to him , they changed their minds and said that he was a god .

nama: Gafana témorwén Fablis yéné féran, yáne ámb mband kété témorwén. Fablis témorwén ngárám ár téfefaf a yénmumyoi yánjo méngot nambio efogh ámnjoghét.
english: Now in the neighborhood of that place were lands belonging to the chief man of the island , named Publius , who received us and courteously entertained us for three days .

nama: Yárefé enjne tékmangérwén yameyan fifi waftamén a oghanemén. Fol nénglawai kénjút Ngánjánafé orayéghét a ngángé yéfayoi yáfaf a mer yiyátoi.
english: The father of Publius lay sick of fever and dysentery . Paul entered in to him , prayed , and laying his hands on him , healed him .

nama: Njam yéné yam nufrotam, yéné férta enjne téfnár tanmorwén a mer táyátotau.
english: Then when this was done , the rest also who had diseases in the island came and were cured .

nama: Yémofem ténmutémndawét yénd tárfár ende, a yénd njam álighétro ténmorwén, yémofem ténmarametawét téfene méndefé rokar. Fol nufaryéng Rom
english: They also honored us with many honors ; and when we sailed , they put on board the things that we needed .

nama: Nambio ghérare soramé mbormboram yiroyéng ndimbal bout. Ndimbal bout yéméghangérmén Maltan mbormbormén yunjfan. Yéné témorwén Aleksandriamén ndimbal bout a kété tárwén sómbio wafroghmén ngánján ndimbal bout sénkoyan. Yénd néngufaryém ndimbal boute.
english: After three months , we set sail in a ship of Alexandria which had wintered in the island , whose figurehead was “ The Twin Brothers .”

nama: Yénd ngérsawém arakafamé nungufaryém Sirakus tesen a yénmorangérmén kété nambio efogh.
english: Touching at Syracuse , we stayed there three days .

nama: Kétándmé yénd nufaryém Regium tesen. Ámb efghon náikái mbormbor waufégh yafngoi, sómbio efogh soramé yénd nufaryém Fotioli tesen.
english: From there we circled around and arrived at Rhegium . After one day , a south wind sprang up , and on the second day we came to Puteoli ,

nama: Kété yéndfem ewinjoyém Yesuene waitogh ár, emofem yénmumyoyénd yémafé ámnjoghét 7 efghot. A féyo yénd néngwanoyém Rom.
english: where we found brothers, and were entreated to stay with them for seven days . So we came to Rome .

nama: Ámb Yesuene waitogh ár Rommén náyárayénd téfenemén a enmormén téfe wanféghét Afius térarégh sifayat a méngotu yétkwén Nambio wan téfnár méngot. Njam Folém ewinjoi fá, yémon kánjo yaramai Ngánjáne a frengégh yanai.
english: From there the brothers, when they heard of us , came to meet us as far as The Market of Appius and The Three Taverns . When Paul saw them , he thanked God and took courage .

nama: Yénd njam nufaryém Rom, Fol yumyoyénd yambia ámnjoghét fiyaram tambetau. Folém efangoi efalo yátogh ár Rommén (Acts 28.16)|src="CN02055B.TIF" size="col" copy="© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission." ref="28.16" Folém táitotau mer si Romén wambégh mbanmbanan
english: When we entered into Rome , the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard , but Paul was allowed to stay by himself with the soldier who guarded him .

nama: Nambio efogh soramé yémo eumendai Ju árfene sénko ár a Fol norayai yéfefaf, “Tane fútaréf, yéndon némé yau tafrotan téfene árfefaf o téfene ákifene túmén yam, wénde yénd werayénd Jerusalemén a wéramayénd Rom árfene ngángat.
english: After three days Paul called together those who were the leaders of the Jews . When they had come together , he said to them , “ I , brothers, though I had done nothing against the people or the customs of our fathers , still was delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans ,

nama: Yémofem kwémotawét yénd a ménde tamorwén ta utorghét, ménamén yaufi yau yinjoyénd tafaf árkémamén kér so kwémorwén.
english: who , when they had examined me , desired to set me free , because there was no cause of death in me .

nama: Ju sénko ár korayawét tafaf, féyo yéné yamam kwéméindangé yénd sái korayangén Kaisar náyáreta tane si, ndemamén tane ámb yau témorwén wánangogh tane árfefaf.
english: But when the Jews spoke against it , I was constrained to appeal to Caesar , not that I had anything about which to accuse my nation .

nama: Féyo yéndo té kénmarawetan fém yénan álighét ménát yéndo so némaufrotan némamén yénd wém kwéfkwéf méngon. Yéné ménamén yém yéndo efalo yiyátotan Keriso, efe yém Israil árfene kamnang té yénmormén.”
english: For this cause therefore I asked to see you and to speak with you . For because of the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.”

nama: Yémofem yawangoyénd, “Yéndfem ámb yau némé férén ewinjoyém Judiamén fenemén. Ámb yau téfene ár efe enmormén kétándmé nafroyénd o norayayénd yaufi si fenemén.
english: They said to him , “ We neither received letters from Judea concerning you , nor did any of the brothers come here and report or speak any evil of you .

nama: Wénde yénd ménde yéném áyárghét fene si némé em, ménamén yénd mat yéném árém norayat yaufi si marataye yéné totér mérénmén.”
english: But we desire to hear from you what you think . For , as concerning this sect, it is known to us that everywhere it is spoken against .”

nama: Yémofem yufoyénd ámb efogh Fole álighét. Njam yéné efogh nawerai, tárfár ár enmormén mérne yéné sifayat ánde fá tékmangérwén. Yémo táitotau yéfefaf akw kétán férégh, tawawafenau Ngánján limánéghmén, a kétamé Mosesene nák a kétamé Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfenemén. Yémo tamndetau ménát yaufiyamta awengoghét a ménát awengoghét Yesuefaf.
english: When they had appointed him a day , many people came to him at his lodging . He explained to them , testifying about God ’s Kingdom, and persuading them concerning Jesus , both from the law of Moses and from the prophets , from morning until evening .

nama: Ámb árém efalo tiyátotawét némé fá korayau, wénde ámb árém yau efalo tiyátotawét.
english: Some believed the things which were spoken , and some disbelieved.

nama: Fá kéwitawét yénémamén yéfenjo kénjún. Njam yémofem frangoghét yafngoyénd, Fol norayai, “Ngánján Mbérmbéram norayai efalo si Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár Aisaya kénjúmé féfene ákifefaf:
english: When they didn’ t agree among themselves , they departed after Paul had spoken one message : “ The Holy Spirit spoke rightly through Isaiah the prophet to our fathers ,

nama: “'Kuwano yéné árfefaf a koraya, “Fém so náyáretati wénde mat yau so náyátotati. Fémofem so yéfandati wénde yau so tayamngongi.
english: saying , ‘ Go to this people and say , in hearing, you will hear, but will in no way understand . In seeing , you will see , but will in no way perceive .

nama: Ménamén yéné árfene tikéf té nénamndai táikér, yémofem táikér yéfandat áyárghét, a yémofem yéfenjo ghékéf té eumbértongi. Féyo mété táf yau néretat yéfenjo ghékfe, a yau náyáretat yéfenjo téngane, a efalo yau yiyátotat yéfenjo tikfe a kawengongi ménát yéndon so mer táyáton fá.”'”
english: For this people ’s heart has grown callous. Their ears are dull of hearing . Their eyes they have closed . Lest they should see with their eyes , hear with their ears , understand with their heart , and would turn again , then I would heal them .’ + 28:27 Isaiah 6:9-10

nama: Yénémaménnde yénd Fol ménde wém fém mat so em Ngánjánene wanjarégh té yéngramai kétán Téndo árfefaf, a fá so náyáretat!
english: “ Be it known therefore to you that the salvation of God is sent to the nations , and they will listen.”
When he had said these words, the Jews departed, having a great dispute among themselves.

nama: Sómbio yunjéf Fol yémorangérmén kété yáne mani faimén méngon a taumyotau fá efe tanmormén yá fandaghét.
english: Paul stayed two whole years in his own rented house and received all who were coming to him ,

nama: Yémo tawaufrotau Ngánján limánéghmén a tawatametau Yuré Yesu Kerisoenemén férférofnar a emo yau tékmbongai.
english: preaching God ’ s Kingdom and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness , without hindrance.
